From: (Eng.) < (Latin) < (Grk.).  O lo la ɛmɛrald o nuu ni. (She wore an emerald on her finger.) Ɛmɛrald nyɛla kuɣ' viɛlli. (Emerald is a beautiful stone.)
A kana fa? (You really have come, then?) Jaŋa lari juɣu zuɣu polo ka ŋun jaŋ' gbina fa? (The monkey laughs at the vulture's bald head, but what about his own buttocks?)

english: to take by force, confiscate, extort, rob; to let free, rescue
example: Serial: darigi fa, fa bahi. Colloc: suhu fa. Sim: ʒe.  Bɛ daa fa o liɣiri maa zaa. (They took all his money by force.) O daa naɣi kuli gurila soya n-fari niriba buni. (He just lay in wait by the roads robbing people of their goods.) Fam' ma bahi! (Help me! / Rescue me!) O kɔŋ fabu. (He couldn't escape.)
Di kɔtomsi nyɛ zaɣ' piɛlli faa kaman dɔzim la. (Its appearance was a pale yellow like dawadawa powder.) O situra nyɛla ʒee faa. (His shirt is reddish.)
See: fa.  Faa bi viɛla, di niŋdi niriba wahala. (Extortion is not good, it causes people trouble.) Bɛ gbaai la faa niŋda maa. (They caught the one doing robbery.)

Variant: fadda, faara. Colloc: faadanima sikuri. Sim: Silimiŋa, Nasara, batule. From: (Eng.).  Faada maa yɛli waazu cheechi ni. (The priest gave a sermon in church.) Faada, a kana ti nya ti. (Father, you have come to see us.) O chaŋ faadanima sikuri ni n ti bɔhim. (He went to the seminary to study.)

Colloc: faai X nyɛvili. Syn: dibigi, zo. See: fa; faara.  O faag' o nol' ni n-zɔ m-bɔrigi. (He escaped out of its mouth and ran away.) A yi chɛ ka o faai zo, a ni zaŋla a nyɛvili n-yo o nyɛvili samli. (If you let him escape, I will take your life to pay for his.)

Colloc: faai siɣ' o yaannim' sani. Sim: baliga, dani, falim, faɣafaɣa, filim, faluŋ, fuɣu, fuliŋ, gaŋsi, kɔɣisi, kɔɣu, 'kpaɣisili, nyuui, pib, viɛbiluŋ, wab. Cpart: bari, barilim, jinjini, karigi, 'kpabili, ŋmɛlim, tibigi. Syn: sheei. See: falim; faluŋ; faɣafaɣa.  N shia faai siɣi. (My spirit fades away.) O faai siɣ' o yaannim' sani. (He passed away and joined his ancestors.)

Colloc: faai siɣ' o yaannim' sani, X zilinli faai.  O ni daa zaŋ o zɔri yomyom maa ka o faai lu. (When he took her and ran hurriedly, she slipped and fell.) Bɛ ni shɛb' faai lu kootaali tandɔri maa ni. (Some of them slipped and fell into the pits.) Zilima faaya ku lan labisi. (A slip of the tongue cannot be taken back.)

Colloc: niŋ faako, Faako Naa. Sim: Zalla Naa, Zallakudura. From: ? (Ha.) ? < (Ar.).  O ni chɛ ka bɛ nya faako n-yi dɔro maa ni. (He will give them relief from the disease.) A yi daŋ yirigi amaŋ tibbu zuɣu a ni nya faako yom yom. (If you are quick with treatment, you'll get speedy relief.) Faako Naa n-nyɛ Naawuni. (God is the King of all mercy.)
Sim: mbɔ, mfaa. See: mfaa.  M-bo n-faaŋa, bi' suŋ! (Well done, my good child!) O tuma maa nyɛla faaŋa. (His work is very good.)
See: faada.  Faara maa yɛli waazu. (The priest gave a sermon.) Faara maa kana ti nya ti. (The priest came to see us.)
See: faai; fa.  A daa ti ba ninvuɣ' faariba. (You provided them with deliverers.) Faara maa sɔŋ o ka o tiligi. (The rescuer helped him to escape.)

english: unless, except; until; it is necessary that... must...
example: Variant: fashee. Sim: hal, shee.  Ti ku tooi baŋ ŋun dini galisi bee m-pɔra faashee ti kali ŋa mi. (We can't know which is larger unless we count them.) Miriya ka yi yɛli so, faashee kum ni neebu nyaaŋa. (Don't tell anyone until after the resurrection.) Faashee nir' chaŋla fukumsi tuma du' shɛli. (A person has to go to the government office.) Faashee kabiɛlli kpe tiŋgban' ni pooi. (A seed must enter the earth and rot.)

See: fabili.  O fabili maa wumiya, o niŋdi la wahala. (His complaint is heard, he is suffering.) Dauda daa fabili fabila ŋɔ Sool min' o bia Jɔnatan zuɣu. (David made this lament for Saul and his son Jonathan.) Alibarika bia ŋun baŋ fabila ku yaɣisi zoobu. (A blessed child who knows how to present his needs will not lag in growth.)

english: to complain; to worry, complain to oneself
example: Serial: fabili niŋ, fabili sɔŋ. Sim: ŋum, nuhi, gaɣi, feei, siɣim.  O chaŋya n'o ti fabli n yɛla n-niŋ Naa. (He went to complain to the chief about me.) N fabilimi niŋ naa ni o di lan zaŋ ma labisi yiŋa. (I was pleading with the chief not to send me back home.) Bɛ daa fabili sɔŋ o, ka maag' o suhu. (They comforted him and consoled him.) (n) kul chani gindi n-fabinda dabisi muni puuni zaa. (I'm just going around complaining the whole day.)
Sim: baa, farilɛɣu, saŋ.  Fadama maa ni, kom be ni yuuni zaa. (In the marsh, there is water all year round.) Bɛ ko la bindira fadama yaɣili. (They farm crops near the marsh.)
Sim: falim, faluŋ, filim, kɔɣisi, faai, 'fuɣu, fuliŋ.  O kpiɣi la dɔɣu faɣafaɣa. (He carried a light stick.) Bia maa nyɛla faɣafaɣa, o bi mali yaa. (The child is weak, he has no strength.)
See: shee.  Gooni maa mali faɣafaɣashee, di ni tooi lu. (The wall has a weak point, it could fall.) O tuma ni, o mali faɣafaɣashee. (In his work, he has a weak point.)

Colloc: faɣiri dira, faɣiri piɛlli, faɣiri nimmɔhi. Syn: fɔɣiri. Sim: birapu.  Tɔbbihi maa daa zaŋ faɣiri n labi yiŋa. (The soldiers took loot and returned home.) O nya la faɣiri piɛlli daa ni. (He got a lucky find in the market.) Faɣiri nimmɔhi n-nyɛ li, di ni tahi barina na. (It's a find that will bring trouble.)
Colloc: faɣisi kuɣili. Sim: nɛli, nɛ(li)kaŋa.  O zaŋ faɣisi kuɣili n tɔri bindirigu. (She used a grinding-stone to grind food.) Faɣisi kuɣili maa nyɛla din bala. (The grinding-stone is smooth.)

english: to sob; to lose air, leak; to become lean
example: Sim: baliga, dani, falim, faai, gaŋsi, kɔɣisi, kɔɣu, 'kpaɣisili, nyuui, pib, viɛbiluŋ, wab. Cpart: bari, barilim, jinjini, karigi, 'kpabili, ŋmɛlim, tibigi.  O faɣisiri la dama o biɛri pam. (He is sobbing because he is in great pain.) Taya maa faɣisiri la pɔhim. (The tyre is leaking air.) A yi bi di, ŋuna ka a faɣisiri a puli. (If you don't eat, it's to him you show your lean stomach.)

english: to stuff up, patch; to be stuffed up, constipated; to be quiet
example: Sim: balim balim, baalim, biɛla biɛla, fo, fɔhi, fooi, gbim, juŋ, nyamm, surim. See: fo; fɔhi; fooi; pufahili.  O fahi la gooni maa ni yɛri. (He patched the wall with mud.) O puli fahiya, o chaŋ asibiti. (He is constipated, he went to hospital.) Bɛ zaa fahi ka wum yɛltɔɣa maa. (They all became quiet and listened to the story.)

Variant: fɔhim. Colloc: mali fahim, niŋ fahim, nyu fahim. Sim: filigi, yɛm, baŋsim. From: (Ha.) > (Ar.).  O mali fahim pam, o tooi baŋ binshɛɣu yomyom. (He has great intelligence, he can understand things quickly.) Bia maa niŋ la fahim o karim ni. (The child applied his intelligence in learning.) O nyu la fahim n ti tooi baŋ.

Variant: dufahirili.  Pii nyɛla du' shɛli din du pam ka nyɛ du' fahirili. (The upper room is a very high room and it is a level room.) Ti mali la tiŋa fahirili n mɛ duu. (We have a level piece of land to build a house.)

Colloc: nyu fahiru, mali fahiru. Sim: bara, fɔhiri, fuhi, maɣam, filigi.  O nyuri la fahiru dama o di nasara. (He is boasting because he won.) O mali fahiru pam, niriba bi jɛri o. (He is very boastful, people don't like him.)

From: (Ha.). Syn: fara.  Fakari gbaagi o, o bi lahi mali liɣiri. (Poverty caught him, he no longer has money.) Fara lu a zuɣu kaman ŋmɛnditol' la, ka fakari lu a zuɣu kaman tɔbbia. (Poverty will come on you like a bandit and scarcity like an armed man.)
Syn: babiɛlifu, baabi, muɣim, muɣimuɣi. Sim: shɛli.  O ku tooi niŋ fako. (He can't do anything.) O ka fako o nuu ni. (He has nothing in his hand.)
Colloc: binfalafala.  Binfalafala daa be binnɛma maa zuɣusaa. (There was a vault above the heads of the living creatures.) Bɛ zaŋ falafala n pili duu maa. (They covered the room with a vaulted roof.)

Colloc: piri fali, di fali. Sim: tarili. See: falidira; falitarita.  A zaligunim' maa nyɛla n fali sahakam. (Your commandments are my inheritance at all times.) Bɛ pirigi la ba fali bihi maa sunsuuni. (They divided the father's inheritance among the children.) Di tuya ka a mali bɛ fali piribu shili. (You should prepare the division of their inheritance.)
Variant: fali. Sim: faliŋko. From: (Ha.). See: faliŋko; fariŋka.  Faliha ha, ti daa be yiŋa. (In the beginning, we were at home.) Faliha n-nyɛ din viɛla. (The beginning is what is good.)

Variant: falira. Sim: suɣilo. Colloc: kpa faliira. Syn: talahi. Ant: suuna.  O mali faliira pam, o bi je saha shɛli. (He has great patience, he never gets angry.) Binshɛɣu kam din kpa faliira daadam biɛhigu ni. (Everything essential for human life.) Di nyɛla faliira ni a chaŋ asibiti. (It's obligatory that you go to hospital.)

Sim: baliga, dani, faai, gaŋsi, kɔɣisi, kɔɣu, 'kpaɣisili, nyuui, pib, viɛbiluŋ, wab, faɣafaɣa, filim, fuliŋ. Cpart: bari, barilim, jinjini, karigi, 'kpabili, ŋmɛlim, tibigi. See: faɣafaɣa; falofalo; filim; faai; fuliŋ.  O falimya zaa dama o bi diri viɛnyɛla. (He's gone all thin because he doesn't eat well.) Bindirigu maa nyɛla falim, di bi tibsa. (The food is light, it's not heavy.)
Variant: fali. Sim: faliha.  Faliŋko ha, ti daa be yiŋa. (In the beginning, we were at home.) Faliŋko n-nyɛ din viɛla. (The first is what is good.)

See: fali; tari; falidira.  Zɔri, ŋuni n-lee zaŋ ma leei yi sariakarita bee yi falitarita? (Friend, who made me your judge or your inheritance-divider?) Falitarita maa pirigi la fali bihi maa sunsuuni. (The executor divided the inheritance among the children.)
Variant: fulofulo. See: faɣafaɣa; falim; filim; faai; fuliŋ.  O kparila chinchin' falofalo. (She wore a light cloth.) Pɔhim falofalo maa ʒɛya. (A light wind is blowing.)

Sim: faɣafaɣa, kɔɣisi, filim, faai, fuliŋ. Cpart: tibisili.  Naɣpiɛliga nyɛla nɔ' faluŋ. (The egret is a light bird.) Di doli ninvuɣ' faluŋ yɛlichɔɣa. (Don't follow an unimportant person's foolish suggestions.)
Di nyɛla faluvaɣili kɔŋko. (It's just a trifle.) O yɛltɔɣa nyɛla faluvaɣili, miri ka a doli li. (His talk is a joke, don't follow it.)

english: stick used to beat down grass to mark farm boundaries
example: Colloc: fandɔɣu dari. See: dɔɣu.  O zaŋ fandɔɣu n baŋsi o puu tariga. (He used a stick to mark his farm boundary.) Fandɔɣu dari maa be mɔɣu ni. (The boundary-marking sticks are in the bush.)

Variant: panpantua. Colloc: ŋme fanfantua. From: (Twi).  Bɛ ŋme la fanfantua ni bɛ boli tɔbbihi na. (They beat the war-alarm to call the soldiers.) Fanfantua maa kum wumiya katiŋa. (The war-alarm sound was heard far away.)

From: (Ha.). Colloc: fari biɛri. Syn: fakari. Sim: nandana. Ant: bundana.  Fara gbaagi o, o bi lahi mali liɣiri. (Poverty caught him, he no longer has money.) Fara lu a zuɣu kaman ŋmɛnditol' la. (Poverty will come on you like a bandit.)
InvNact: fa. See: fa.  Fara maa zu la liɣiri maa n zo. (The robber stole the money and ran.) Fariba maa gbahi ya. (The robbers have been caught.)

See: fara; -lana. Syn: nandana. Ant: bundana. From: (Ha.).  O nyɛla faralana, o bi mali liɣiri pam. (He is a poor person, he doesn't have much money.) Faralana mini bundana zaa be ni. (Both poor and rich people are there.)

english: to simmer, cook to thick consistency
example: Saɣim maa fari la buɣim ni. (The t.z. is simmering on the fire.) Kom maa fari la duɣu ni. (The water is simmering in the pot.)
Sim: ʒim, kɔri.  O mali farigu, o chaŋ asibiti. (He has a haemorrhage, he went to hospital.) Farigu maa zuɣu, o gbarigi pam. (Because of the haemorrhage, he is very weak.)

Syn: lampoo. Colloc: deei farigu. Nabstr: farigudeebu. Nact: farigudeera. See: fa; farigudeera; farigudeebu.  Bɛ yo la farigu n-ti gomnanti yuuni kam. (They pay tax to the government every year.) O zaŋ liɣiri n yo o bihi shikuru farigu. (He used the money to pay his children's school fees.) Dimbɔŋɔ ni tooi filim nira ni din yɛn zaŋ laɣi' shɛli n da bindirigu. (This can reduce the money a person has to buy food.)

See: farigu; deei. InvNabstr: farigu.  Yisa daa yi nimaani chana nti nya Matiu, ka o ʒi farigudeebu shee. (Jesus went on from there and saw Matthew sitting at the tax-collection place.) Farigudeebu shee maa, niriba pam be ni. (At the tax-collection place, there are many people.)

InvNact: farigu. See: farigu; deei.  Matiu ŋun daa nyɛ farigudeera la. (Matthew who was a tax-collector.) Bɔzuɣu ka yi karimba maa mini farigudeeriba ni alahichinima laɣim timdi dira? (Why does your teacher eat with tax-collectors and sinners?)
Sim: baa, fadama, saŋ.  Farilɛɣu maa ni, kom be ni yuuni zaa. (In the bog, there is water all year round.) O firi la farilɛɣu ni. (He sank in the quagmire.)
From: (Ha.).  O diri la farinkasa. (He eats edible white clay.) Farinkasa nyɛla din mali alaafee n-ti paɣiba. (Edible clay is healthy for women.)
From: (Eng.) 'front'.  Dirɔba maa ʒiya farintɔŋ ni. (The driver is sitting in the cab of the lorry.) Farintɔŋ maa viɛla. (The lorry cab is nice.)
From: (Ha.). Syn: chilibi.  Farinwuta nyɛla din mali anfaani pam. (Sulphur is very useful.) Bɛ zaŋdi farinwuta n tibiri dɔriti. (They use sulphur to treat illnesses.)
Fariŋga maa yi la Paris na. (The French person came from Paris.) Farinsi pam be Ghana. (Many French people are in Ghana.)
O chaŋ la Fariŋgu ni n ti bɔhim Faransi. (He went to a French-speaking territory to learn French.) Fariŋgu nyɛla tiŋgbani din mali alaafee. (French-speaking territory is a pleasant land.)
Sim: faliha.  Fariŋka maa gbibi la tuuta tobu ni. (The standard-bearer held the flag in battle.) Fariŋka maa kpaŋsi tɔbbihi maa. (The war-leader encouraged the soldiers.)

Colloc: yihi fasara, ti fasara. Sim: waazu, molo. From: (Ha.) < (Ar.).  Karimba maa yihi la fasara n-ti bihi maa. (The teacher gave a lecture to the students.) O ti la fasara zaŋ chaŋ alaafee yɛla. (He gave an address about health matters.)

Variant: falwɔɣili, fawoli. Colloc: niŋ fawɔɣili, mali fawɔɣili. See: 'wɔɣili.  Miriya ka yi niŋ fawɔɣili n chaŋ daa. (Mind you don't delay going to market.) Fawɔɣili dolinkpee n-nyɛ sarati. (Carelessness is the companion of an accident.) O mali fawɔɣili o tuma ni. (He is careless in his work.)

See: fawɔɣili; -lana.  O nyɛla fawɔɣililana, o bi guli o buni. (He is a negligent person, he doesn't look after his goods.) Fawɔɣililana maa zuɣu, tuma maa bi viɛla. (Because of the negligent person, the work is not good.)

Variant: fawɔɣili. SynD: fowoli (E.D.).  O fawoli zuɣu, o bi naai tuma maa. (Because of his carelessness, he didn't finish the work.) Fawoli bi viɛla, di saɣinda tuma. (Procrastination is not good, it spoils work.)

From: (Ar.). Sim: azafari, lahisari, mangariba, liisa.  O yiɣisi la faziri ni n puhi jiŋli. (He got up at dawn prayer time to pray.) Faziri saha paai, ti chaŋ jiŋli ni. (Dawn prayer time has come, we go to the mosque.)
From: (Eng.). See: fɔɔsi.  Di nyɛla fɔɔsi ni a chaŋ. (It is compulsory that you go.) Shikuru chandi nyɛla fɔɔsi Ghana. (School attendance is compulsory in Ghana.)
Sim: fɔɔsi, kpiɔŋ, kpuŋkpani, moliga, yaburiga, yaa, yiko. From: (Eng.). See: fɔɔsi; fɔɔsi.  O niŋ li ni fɔɔsi. (He did it by force.) Fɔɔsi bi viɛla. (Compulsion is not good.)
From: (Eng.). See: fɔɔsi; fɔɔsi.  Bɛ fɔɔsi o ni o chaŋ. (They forced him to go.) Di fɔɔsi a maŋa ni a niŋ li. (Don't force yourself to do it.)

See: ninfɔɣu.  Amaa ka Ammɔnnim' naa Nahash daa yɛl' ba, "Yi yi ni saɣi ka n fɔɣi yi nudiriti polo nimbihi zaa..." (But Nahash the Ammonite king replied, "I will make a treaty only if I gouge out the right eye of every one of you...") Ŋun fɔɣ' o kpee nini bɛn' fɔɣim' o dini labisi. (He who knocks out his fellow's tooth should have his own knocked out in revenge.) Ziŋ' yi yi kom ni na nti yɛli ni nyɛbiga nini fɔɣiya, nira ŋun di chih' o. (If a fish comes out and says a crocodile's eye is knocked out, no one should argue.)
Syn: faɣiri.  O nya la fɔɣiri mɔɣu ni. (He found booty in the bush.) Fɔɣiri maa nyɛla nimdi. (The spoils are meat.)
Sim: ne, neei, nyaɣisi, nyɛlisi, nyeei, peei, 'nyɛlisirili, piɣipiɣi, wuliwuli.  Wuntaŋa maa fɔhi wumiya. (The sun's brilliance is felt.) Salima maa mali fɔhi. (The gold has brilliance.)

Sim: balim balim, baalim, biɛla biɛla, fahi, fo, fooi, gbim, juŋ, nyamm, surim. See: fo; fooi.  Bɛ zaa fɔhi ka wum yɛltɔɣa maa. (They all became quiet and listened to the story.) O fɔhi o noli. (He became silent.)
Tiŋa maa fɔhi ya. (The ground is uneven.) Bɛ waɣilim fɔhi taba. (Their heights are unequal.)

Amaa ka o daa fɔh' o nuu maa kpɛhi. (But he drew his hand back inside.) Bɔ n-niŋ ka a fɔh' a nuu? (Why do you hold back your hand?) Sool tɛh' o suhu ni ni o dii zaŋ kpani maa to Dauda tabili dukpini, amaa ka Dauda daa fɔhi li buyi zaa. (Saul thought to pin David to the wall, but David dodged twice.)
Syn: fɔrigi.  O fɔhi la kom maa. (He sipped the water.) O fɔhifɔhi la dam maa. (He sipped the pito repeatedly.)
See: fahim.  O mali fɔhim pam. (He has great intelligence.) Fɔhim zuɣu, o tooi baŋ binshɛɣu yomyom. (Because of his intelligence, he understands things quickly.)
Sim: filigi, fuhi, golisi, bara, maɣam.  O fɔhiri maa wumiya. (His boasting is heard.) Fɔhiri bi viɛla, niriba bi jɛri li. (Boasting is not good, people don't like it.)
O zaŋ fɔnbilim n zo. (He used trickery to escape.) Fɔnbilim bi viɛla, di saɣinda yɛda. (Trickery is not good, it spoils trust.)

Cpart: tiŋkpannima. See: nira; -nima.  Bɛ daa zo chaŋ ti yɛli fɔnnim' mini tikpannima. (They ran off to tell the townspeople and villagers.) Fɔnnira maa mi tiŋa maa yɛla. (The townsman knows the town's affairs.)

Variant: fɔnnima. See: -nima.  Bɛ ni ti zaŋ takɔbiri lu bɛ fɔntinsinim' zuɣu. (They will take swords and fall on their townspeople.) Fɔntinsinima mini tiŋkpannima zaa laɣim ya. (Both townspeople and villagers gathered.)

Variant: fontiŋa. Cpart: tiŋkpaŋa. See: fɔŋ; tiŋa.  Bɛ me bɛ fontiŋa. (They built their city.) O daa me goondahi gili Juda fɔntinsi. (He built walls around Judean cities.) Yisa daa gindi fɔntinsi mini tiŋkpansi yihiri waazu. (Jesus went around the towns and villages preaching.)
O ʒiya fɔŋ. (He is sitting quietly.) Duu maa nyɛla fɔŋ. (The room is empty.)

Variant: fɔŋdi. Cpart: tiŋkpaŋa, mɔɣu. Sim: tiŋa. Colloc: Zɔhi fɔŋ. See: yaŋɔ.  Di moliya di yɛla gili Gaat fɔŋ ni. (Publish it not in the town of Gath!) Jɛrigu ka saa buri fɔŋ ni. (A fool is beaten by the rain even in town.) Ti daa pun niŋdi shɛm Juda fɔndi ni. (What we were doing in the towns of Judah.) Paɣ' maa daa chɛ o kochuɣu zali nimaani ka lab' fɔŋ ni. (The woman left her waterpot and went back to town.) Tiŋkpaŋ' bia bi veeni ka o baŋ fɔŋ ni. (A village boy shouldn't get to know the town.)
Syn: fɔhi.  O fɔrigi la kom maa. (He sipped the water.) O fɔrigifɔrigi la dam maa. (He sipped the pito repeatedly.)

Bɛna n-ŋmahi foto shɛŋa dim be kalanda ŋɔ ni maa. (They took the photos which are in this calendar.) O yaai la fɔto n ti o zo. (He took a picture for his friend.) Colloc: ŋmaai/yaai fɔto. Sim: anfooni. From: (Eng.).
See: fawɔɣili.  O fɔwɔɣili zuɣu, tuma maa bi viɛla. (Because of his negligence, the work is not good.) Fɔwɔɣili bi viɛla. (Negligence is not good.)

Variant: fɛra. Colloc: fe nyinyee. Sim: feei. See: feei.  O fe la o nyinyee ni. (He pinched with his fingernail.) Sakuɣa maa daa lu n-fiɛri puri ni bimbilima zaa. (Hailstones fell and beat down everything growing in the fields.)
Di feeya. (It is scarce.) Bindirigu feei ya yuuni ŋɔ. (Food is scarce this year.)

english: to grumble at, complain, make discouraging remark
example: Sim: fabili, ŋum, gaɣi.  O feei la o tuzɔ. (He grumbled at his younger sibling.) Di feei niriba ka bɛ je a. (Don't grumble at people and make them dislike you.)
See: fe.  O fee omaŋ nyinyee. (He pinched himself.) O fee o zo maa. (He pinched his friend.)

Syn: kpɔkpɔrɔtikpo.  Bɛ ŋmɛri la feensi salima ni. (They play finger-bells at the festival.) Feensi maa kum nyɛɣisa. (The finger-bells' sound is sweet.) Jɛra mali biŋkumda pam. Di shɛŋa n-nyɛ guŋgɔna, feensi, nti pahi lumbil' zuɣu. (The performers had many instruments: drums, finger-bells and small hourglass drums.)

Variant: fiɛraa, fiara. Syn: sitɔɣu.  A kpee yi da ka di biɛri a, a mi nyin' da, di be feera ni. (If your companion buys something and you envy it, go and buy it yourself; it's in the store.) Pɔhim kpiɔŋ din piligiri yiya mini shikuriti ni feera duri. (A strong wind which unroofs houses, schools and stores.) A ni tooi ka Damaskus na nti me feeranim' zali. (You can come to Damascus and set up trading-posts.)

english: to hide sth., cover, bury; to redistribute
example: Variant: pi. Sim: pi. See: firi; firigi; pi.  Bɛ zaŋ li fi tiŋa. (They buried it in the ground.) Jɛrusalɛm paɣisara zaŋla bɛ zuɣuri fi tiŋa. (The young women of Jerusalem bowed their heads to the ground.) Di pa shɛli ka bɛ no li fi taŋkpaɣu ni. (Perhaps they will trample them into the ground.)
Variant: fubigi. Colloc: fubigi X zuɣu.  Bɛ zuɣu fibgiya. (Their head was chopped off.) O fubigi la tia maa wula. (He chopped off the tree's branches.)

english: to surpass, overdo, be excessive; to outrun
example: Variant: fubigi.  O fibigi la o zo zoobu ni. (He outran his friend in running.) A fibigi ya, di niŋ lala yaha. (You've overdone it, don't do that again.)
Syn: findi.  A yi saɣi "Naa, naa!" nti je, a saɣirila "Fiɔŋfiɔŋ!" (If you respond "Yes, yes!" without success, then you respond "I dare you!") O niŋ o fiɔŋ. (He dared him to say it.)

english: to whip, flog, beat with a switch; to cut grass
example: Colloc: fiɛb' bɔbili, fiɛbi kpini, fiɛbi lani. Sim: bu, ŋmɛ. Ninst: barazim, fiɛbiga. See: fiɛbiga; fiɛbira.  O fiɛbi la o bia dama o tum taali. (He whipped his child because he did wrong.) Nira yi kuli ʒi o dɔzaba, di yɛn fiɛbila o tiba. (If someone carries dawadawa pods on his head, they'll whip his ears.) Dovihi ka bɛ mali fiɛbiri kpini bɛ yi ti puhiri Kpini Chuɣu. (They use dawadawa leaves to whip guineafowl at the Guineafowl Festival.) N di fiɛbila mɔɣu. (I am just cutting the grass.)

Variant: fiɛbigu. InvNinst: fiɛbi. Sim: barazim.  O yi ti tum taali, n ni zaŋ fiɛbiga fiɛb' o. (If he does wrong, I will punish him with a whip.) Sokpaŋ' fiɛbiga ku fiɛbi nin' yino. (A wayside whip will not beat only one person.) Fiɛbisi vuri mini chɛchɛbunsi kalima vuri. (The sound of whips and the wheels of chariots.)
InvNact: fiɛbi.  Fiɛbira maa fiɛbi la bia maa. (The whipper whipped the child.) Fiɛbiriba maa be tobu ni. (The whippers are in the battle.)
Colloc: vuhi fiɛfiɛ. Sim: fiɛɣufiɛɣu.  O vuhiri la fiɛfiɛ dama o mali fiɛɣufiɛɣu. (He is wheezing because he has catarrh.) Fiɛfiɛ maa wumiya. (The wheezing is heard.)
O ti yih' o fiɛɣu min' o kɔbiri bahi. (He removed its crop and feathers.) Kɔringa daa yɛli ni nyaaŋa booi, fiɛɣu gilata. (The partridge said its back is wet, but its crop is full.)

Variant: fiɛɣfiɛɣu. Sim: nyomuɣili, fiɛfiɛ.  Sumaŋa nyaɣisa ka fiɛɣufiɛɣu ka narili? (It's nice to be independent, but what about catarrh and boils?) O mali fiɛɣufiɛɣu, o vuhiri ni wahala. (He has catarrh, he breathes with difficulty.)
Colloc: fiɛhi nyee.  O fiɛhi o nyee. (He blew his nose.) O fiɛhi o nina ni kom. (He wiped his eyes with water.)

english: to be equal; to be square
example: Sim: mulisi, 'mɔɣu.  Ti bi fiɛlim Naawuni sani. (We are not equal in God's sight.) Duu maa fiɛlim ya. (The room is square.)

Variant: finfali, finafali, fufali. Sim: bindiripooli, kum.  Mɔ' nyori biɛɣu maa gba lan tahiri sanzali ni bindiri' pooli bee finafali na. (Excessive bush-burning brings drought, food-shortage or famine.) Muɣisigu bee wahala bee alaka bee finfali bee gbinfini bee barina? (Trouble, hardship, persecution, famine, nakedness or danger?) Fifali gbaagi tiŋa maa. (Famine caught the land.)

english: making first cut through grass to mark boundary
example: O niŋ la fifani n baŋsi o puu tariga. (He made the first cut to mark his farm boundary.) Fifani maa nyaaŋa, o pili kɔribu. (After marking the boundary, he started farming.)
Variant: fifii. Sim: fifinyiiŋa.  O mali la binshɛɣu fifia. (He has a very small thing.) Bia fifia maa ʒiya. (The very small child is sitting.)

Variant: fifiyiiŋa, fifinŋa. Sim: fifia.  Zuɣusaa mini dunia ŋɔ ni ti yiɣisi, amaa Naawuni fukumsi zaligunim' ŋɔ ni sabbu bachibi' fifinyiiŋa gba ku yiɣisi. (Heaven and earth will pass away, but not the smallest letter of God's law will disappear.) O mali la binshɛɣu fifinyiiŋa. (He has a very tiny thing.)

Sim: chaaji, chom.  Bɛ daa ŋma gbaŋ fiɣisi Judanima. (They wrote a letter accusing the Judah people.) A bi wum bɛ ni fiɣisir' a shɛm maa? (Don't you hear how they are accusing you?) Ti ku nya ti ni yɛn zaŋ yɛl' shɛli fiɣisi o. (We won't find anything to accuse him of.)
Sim: nyo, zaha. See: fihim.  Di fihimi. (It stinks.) Nayiga maa fihi pam. (The thief smells very bad.) Nimdi maa fihi ya, ti ku tooi di li. (The meat stinks, we can't eat it.)
Colloc: mali fihiga, di X fihiga. Sim: halli.  O mali fihiga viɛlli. (He has a good personality.) Di di o fihiga, di niŋ viɛlli. (Don't belittle him, it's not good.)
See: fihi.  Fihim maa wumiya. (The acid smell is there.) Kom maa mali fihim. (The water has an acid smell.)

english: to be in abundance (of grass or fruit)
example: Mɔri maa fihim ya yuuni ŋɔ. (The grass is abundant this year.) Tia wala fihim ya. (The tree fruits are abundant.)

Variant: fiiva. From: (Eng.).  Di yi duɣi du n gari lala , dindina fiiba bee akpaɣila gbaai di lana. (If temperature goes above , the person has fever or ague.) O mali fiiba, o chaŋ asibiti. (He has fever, he went to hospital.)

english: sound of cat purring or asthma patient
example: Saɣi maa fiifii wumiya. (The cat's purring is heard.) O vuhiri la fiifii dama o mali nyomuɣili. (He breathes with a purring sound because he has asthma.)

Colloc: fiig tia. From: (Eng.).  O ko la fiig tihi o pɔŋ ni. (He planted fig trees in his farm.) Fiig wala maa nyɛɣisa pam. (The figs are very sweet.) Ŋunkam maani fiig tia viɛnyɛliŋga ni di di wala. (Whoever cares for the fig tree will eat its fruit.)

From: (Eng.) 'wheel'. Whole: kalinli, gbali.  Chɛchɛ maa fiili maa gɔŋ ya. (The bicycle wheel rim is bent.) O daa mali di gbaya maa ka di ŋmanila chɛchɛbuŋ' gbaya. Di fiilinim' ni di duɣuri zaa daa nyɛla daanya. (It had wheels like chariot wheels; its rims and hubs were all bronze.)
From: (Eng.).  O fiili la suhupiɛlli. (He felt happiness.) O fiili li o nubihi ni. (He felt it with his fingers.)
See: fiiba.  O mali fiiva, o chaŋ asibiti. (He has fever, he went to hospital.) Fiiva maa zuɣu, o bi tooi tum tuma. (Because of the fever, he can't work.)
Syn: fino.  Filaŋgao! n bi mi o yɛla. (I don't care! I don't know his matters.) A yi yɛli lala, n fino. (If you say that, I don't care.)
Filasiko tia maa be mɔɣu ni. (The senna tree is in the bush.) Bɛ zaŋdi filasiko vari n tibiri dɔriti. (They use senna leaves to treat illnesses.)

english: to abhor dirt; to clean oneself after getting dirty
example: Sim: paɣi.  O filigiri la daɣiri. (He abhors dirt.) O tuma naai, o filigi o maŋa. (After work, he cleaned himself.)

english: to show off one's knowledge, boast
example: Sim: (nyu) bara, fuhi, golisi, fɔhiri, maɣam, futili.  A filgiya dama a mi baŋsim. (You are boasting because you know something.) O filigiri la o baŋsim niriba tooni. (He shows off his knowledge before people.)

english: to decrease; to reduce, lose flesh; to lighten; to make small; to disgrace
example: Sim: pɔra, chɔɣim, gbarigi, chaɣisi, kɔɣisi, faai, falim, faɣafaɣa, faluŋ, fuliŋ, nyuui, booi. Cpart: zooi, pahi.  Bɛ filim kpalim biɛla. (They decreased and only a few are left.) O kpiɔŋ daa kul filimda. (His strength kept diminishing.) A nam farigu liɣiri filim. (Your tax revenues decrease.) Yɛlimi ŋun kpaɣ'a tuma ni maa, ka o filim hawa shɛŋa. (Tell your boss to reduce your hours.) Ti ni doli ŋarili soli n-filim kaŋmɛri ŋmani. (We will cheat by making the measuring calabash small.) O filim karimbaannima ka zooi tarimba. (He humbled the proud and exalted the humble.)

Variant: filuŋ. Cpart: tibiginsim, jilima.  Izraɛlnim' ni ti nya filiŋ, ka kɔɣisi yi fini zaa. (The Israelites will see deprivation and become thin and wretched.) Zom ti nambɔɣu yomyom; dama ti nya filiŋ pam. (May your mercy come quickly, for we are in desperate need.) Ninvuɣ' so yi ku doo ŋɔ ka yihi filiŋ ŋɔ tiŋgbɔŋ ni, bɔ ka bɛ yɛn niŋ ti dilana? (What will be done for the man who removes this disgrace?)

english: to sink, dive, go under water
example: Sim: shim. See: fi.  Di simdi ni di kpuɣi du zuɣusaa n-lalilali gili nti lab' ti fim. (It should rise up, be stirred up, and then sink back.) O fim tiŋa kom ni. (He sank to the bottom of the water.)

english: to kibble, cut into tiny pieces; to nibble
example: O fimsi la nimdi maa n ti bia maa. (She cut the meat into tiny pieces for the child.) Bia maa fimsi la bindirigu maa. (The child nibbled the food.)

Colloc: yɛli finafina. See: fini; fini.  Izraɛlnim' mini Yawɛ ni daa yɛl' tab' finafina. (The Israelites and the Lord disputed with each other.) Bɛ yɛli la finafina zaŋ chaŋ liɣiri yɛla. (They disputed about money matters.)

english: expression for picking quarrel ("I dare you")
example: Syn: fiɔŋ. Correl: gandi.  O niŋ o findi. (He dared him.) Sal' yɛlibiɛri ku niŋ findi. (A stranger's improper speech is not answered with a dare.) A yi saɣi findi, o ni yɛli gandi. (If you accept the dare, he'll say "I take you up".)

english: to miss each other on the road; to surprise sb., catch sb. in a bad act
example: Variant: fundi. Conv: chirigi.  Bɛ chaŋmi findi taba. (They were going but missed each other.) O findi o la ni o zura. (He caught him stealing.)

Sim: yoli.  Ŋunkam tu o kpee, 'Ninvuɣ' fini' ni ti be yɛla ni saria karibu shee. (If you call your brother 'Good-for-nothing!', you'll be brought before the council.) O nyɛla ninsali' fini, so bi jɛri o. (He is a bad person, no one likes him.)
Sim: fio.  O tɔri piɛm fini. (He shoots arrows very well.) O tum tuma maa fini. (He did the work extraordinarily well.)
Syn: finyia.  O fini zuɣu, o bi chɛ ti. (Because of his importunity, he didn't leave us.) O yihi o fini. (He disgraced him.)

Variant: findim. Syn: gbinfini. See: gbinfini.  O ni daa yi zahira tumdi o pagɔrisigu tuma, ka yihiri omaŋ' fini shɛm maa. (When she carried on her prostitution openly and exposed her nakedness.) O chani la fini. (He is walking stark naked.)

Colloc: yi fini, yihi X fini.  Izraɛlnim' ni ti nya filiŋ, ka kɔɣisi yi fini zaa. (The Israelites will experience deprivation and become thoroughly wretched.) Niriba bɛn mali AIDS nyɛla fiiva mini binsaa ni gbahira shɛba ka bɛ kɔɣisi n-yi fini. (People with AIDS get fever and diarrhoea and become thin and wretched.)
Syn: filaŋgao.  N fino. (I don't care, it's not my business.) Bɛ fino. (They don't care.) A yi yɛli lala, n fino. (If you say that, I don't care.)
Variant: fimshe. See: firigbɛha.  O deei la finshee o tuma maa zuɣu. (He received commission for his work.) Finshee maa nyɛla liɣiri biɛla. (The commission is a little money.)
Variant: funyia, fini. Colloc: yihi finyia.  O finyia zuɣu, o bi chɛ ti. (Because of his importunity, he didn't leave us.) O yihi o funyia niriba tooni. (He disgraced him publicly.)
Sim: fini.  O ŋmɛri malifa fio. (He shoots very well.) O tum tuma maa fio. (He did the work very well.)

Colloc: firanki tulaale zim. Sim: aloosi, mɛɛr, kaasia, sinnamɔn. From: (Eng.).  O zaŋ firanki tulaale zim n ti o. (He gave him frankincense.) Pina maa daa nyɛla salima mini tulaale zim shɛŋa bɛ ni booni firanki tulaale la ni mɛɛr. (The gifts were gold, frankincense and myrrh.)
From: < (Ar.) < (Heb.) < (Egyptian).  Yuma ayi nyaaŋa ka Firawuna daa ti zahim zahindi. (Two years later, Pharaoh had a dream.) Firawuna daa nyɛla Ijipti Naa. (Pharaoh was the king of Egypt.)

english: to sink in, bog down; to sink sth. in; to push sth. into a hole
example: Serial: yiɣi firi. Colloc: sinkaafa furi. Cpart: firigi. See: fi; firigi; firiginli.  O firi la baɣiri ni. (He sank in the mud.) Ka bɛ chɛchɛbunsi daa firi bayaɣiti ni n-kɔŋ chandi. (Their chariots sank in the mud and couldn't move.) Gbuluɣu bi firiti ni mia. (A frog with a string tied to it cannot sink.) Pipi' shɛli din yiɣi ka firi. (The cup that went missing.) Kuɣili maa chaŋ ti furi o gbɛɣu ni. (The stone went and embedded itself in his forehead.)
See: finshee.  O deei la firigbɛha o tuma maa zuɣu. (He received commission for his work.) Firigbɛha maa nyɛla liɣiri biɛla. (The commission is a little money.)

Sim: sibigi. Cpart: firi. See: fi; firi.  O vooi ma yihi barina bɔɣ' zilinli ni na, ka firigi ma baɣiri ni. (He pulled me out of a dangerous pit, out of the quicksand.) O firigi la kuɣili maa. (He pulled out the stone.)

Variant: nyiriginli. Sim: wɔrilani.  O duɣi la firiginli n diri. (She cooked aerial yam and is eating.) A yi tɔhi baɣiŋga mihi ni a lo firigima nyin' ka di yɛn wum. (If you strip fibres to tie up aerial yams, you'll get tired.)

english: bog, place where one can get stuck
example: Variant: firili. See: firi.  Firiginli maa ni, niriba tooi firiti. (In the bog, people often get stuck.) Di chaŋ firiginli maa ni. (Don't go into the bog.)
From: (Eng.).  O zaŋ bindirigu n niŋ firiiji ni. (She put food in the fridge.) Firiiji maa mali kom ni. (The fridge has water in it.)
Sim: dariza, jilima, jiri, sizuura, yiko. From: (Ha.).  O nam mali firijita pam. (His reign has great grandeur.) Firijita zuɣu, niriba zaa gbiri o. (Because of his power, everyone bows to him.)
Sim: poli.  O mali firikpi, o chaŋ asibiti. (He has a hernia, he went to hospital.) Firikpi maa nyɛli o pam. (The hernia hurts him a lot.)

Variant: furila. Colloc: firila galli, firila mia, firila yoli, firila wɔɣu, firila laa. Sim: ataninmiɛɣu, kania, gaasi. From: (Ha.) < (Ar.).  O firila bi kpiri yuŋ. (Her lamp does not go out at night.) A yɛligu nyɛla n naba firila mini n soli neesim. (Your word is the lamp of my feet and the light of my path.) O ku nyo firila kolikol' o yiŋ' zaa m-bo li. (She lit a lamp and thoroughly swept her whole house searching for it.) O mi ku kpihi firila mia din diri biɛlabiɛla. (He won't put out a lamp-wick burning low.)
See: firila; duhi.  Firiladuhirisi maa be daaŋa ni. (The tongs are in the kitchen.) O zaŋ firiladuhirisi n duhi firila mia. (He used tongs to raise the lamp wick.)

Sim: firilakpihindisi. See: firila; kpihim.  O zaŋ firilakpihindigu n kpihi firila maa. (He used snuffers to put out the lamp.) Firilakpihindi' bina maa be teebuli zuɣu. (The snuffers are on the table.)

Sim: firilakpihindigu. See: firila; kpihim.  Firilakpihindisi maa be daaŋa ni. (The lamp-snuffers are in the kitchen.) O zaŋ firilakpihindisi n kpihi firila maa. (He used lamp-snuffers to put out the lamp.)
See: firila; tamdigu.  O zaŋ salima alali mali firilatamdigu. (He used pure gold to make a lampstand.) Firilatamdisi maa be jɛmbu duu ni. (The lampstands are in the worship room.)

From: (Eng.).  Di dunoya zaa mini di takɔriti zaa firimnim' daa nyɛla zaɣ' mɔɣiri. (All its doors and window-frames were square.) O mali la firim n-niŋ o foto maa ni. (He put a frame around his photo.)

english: to be in abundance and crowded (grass or crop)
example: See: firimanyaɣima.  Mɔri maa firim ya yuuni ŋɔ. (The grass is abundant this year.) Bindirigu firim ya puu ni. (The crop is crowded in the farm.)
Sim: balɔrigu. See: firim; nyaɣim.  O mali firimanyaɣima o nina ni. (He has freckles on his face.) O nyɛla firimanyaɣima. (He is ugly.)
From: (Ha.).  N yi niŋ o firimiinsi o ni chɛ ka bɛ yi. (If I treat him harshly, he'll let them go.) Firimiinsi bi viɛla, di niŋdi niriba wahala. (Cruelty is not good, it causes people suffering.)
Variant: firimbila.  O mali la binshɛɣu firiŋga. (He has a tiny thing.) Bia firiŋga maa ʒiya. (The tiny child is sitting.)

english: to sneeze; to snort; to whinny, neigh
example: Variant: furisi. Syn: tihim.  O firisi la. (He sneezed.) Wahu maa firisiri la. (The horse is neighing.) O fursila o nyee ni. (He is snorting through his nose.)
From: (Eng.).  Fita maa mali moto maa. (The mechanic is repairing the car.) O boli fita na n ti lihi o chɛchɛ. (He called a mechanic to check his bicycle.)

Variant: fitina. Sim: alaka, barina, muɣisigu, nahiŋgu, wahala, yɛlli. From: (Ha.) < (Ar.).  Miriya ka yi niŋ sana zaɣ' biɛɣu bee n-niŋ o fitiina. (Do not mistreat or oppress a foreigner.) Africa tiŋgban' shɛŋa gba mali ayodin pooli fitiina maa. (Other African countries also suffer from iodine deficiency trouble.) Fitiina be tiŋa maa ni. (There is trouble in the town.)

See: fitiina; -lana.  O nyɛla fitiinalana, o bi zɔri niriba nambɔɣu. (He is a cruel person, he has no mercy on people.) Fitiinalana maa zuɣu, niriba zɔra. (Because of the cruel person, people are afraid.)
Sim: pum, tipum. From: (Eng.).  O ko la flawas o sambani ni. (She planted flowers in her compound.) Flawas maa viɛla pam. (The flowers are very beautiful.)

Colloc: fo noli, vuri fo, fo chim. Syn: shini. Sim: balim balim, baalim, biɛla biɛla, fahi, fɔhi, fooi, gbim, juŋ, nyamm, surim. See: fɔhi; fooi.  O lu tiŋa n-fo n-do paɣ' maa naba ni. (He fell down and lay quiet at the woman's feet.) Ka o neei tahi sanʒɛɣu maa zuɣu ka yɛli teeku maa, "Fom' baalim!" Ka sanʒɛɣu maa daa chɛ ʒɛbu, ka di zaa dii fo baalim. (He woke up, rebuked the wind and said "Quiet! Be still!" and the wind died down and it was calm.) Nyini n-chɛ ka teeku vuri fɔra. (You made the noise of the sea be quiet.)

Syn: lai. InvRes: pe. From: (Eng.) via (Ha.) 'Fall in!'.  A yiŋa yi ka dɔri nira, a bi chɛn' ti piɛri foli. (If there's no sick person at home, you don't go to stand in a queue.) Bɛ piɛri la foli n guri bindirigu. (They are standing in a queue waiting for food.)

english: narrow passageway, gap; row, line; firmament
example: Sim: foli.  O daa buɣisi foliŋga din kpe dunɔdali maa ni. (He measured the passage in the gateway.) Sulemaana daa mɛla Milo gooni ŋari foliŋga din daa be o ba Dauda tiŋ' la ni la. (Solomon built the Millo wall and closed the gap in his father David's city.) Bɛ bela bɛ oliv tihi folinsi ni n-kahindi kpam. (They are in the rows of olive trees squeezing oil.) "Foliŋga, niŋmi kom ŋɔ sunsuuni m-pirigi kom maa buyi!" ("Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, dividing the waters!")
Colloc: niŋ folo. Sim: fooi.  Folo gbaagi tiŋa maa. (Scarcity caught the land.) Bindirigu niŋ folo yuuni ŋɔ. (Food is scarce this year.)

Sim: balim balim, baalim, biɛla biɛla, fahi, fo, fɔhi, gbim, juŋ, nyamm, surim. See: fo.  O fooi dooni. (He lay down quietly.) Bɛ daa fooi baalim. (They stayed quiet.) O yɛn fooi la nu' yini n-yi nyaaŋa dama bia maa ti sarigi lu tiŋ' zuɣu. (She will slip one hand to the back because the child might slide to the ground.)

english: to be scarce, fall short; to draw back
example: Sim: folo.  Bindirigu fooi ya yuuni ŋɔ. (Food is scarce this year.) O fooi o maŋa. (He drew back.)
From: (Eng.).  Foomani maa kpaŋsiri tuntumdiba maa. (The foreman encourages the workers.) O nyɛla foomani suŋ. (He is a good foreman.)
See: fawoli. SynD: fawoli (W.D.).  O fowoli zuɣu, o bi naai tuma maa. (Because of his carelessness, he didn't finish the work.) Fowoli bi viɛla. (Carelessness is not good.)
Mult: fuhi. See: fuhi.  O fu la dakuɣu maa. (He dragged the log.) Bɛ fu la lɔri maa yiɣisibu. (They dragged to lift the lorry.)

english: to draw air in through mouth
example: Cpart: ha.  O fu la pɔhim o noli ni. (He drew air in through his mouth.) Fu pɔhim na. (Breathe in.)
See: fibigi.  O fubigi la tia maa wula. (He chopped off the tree's branches.) Bɛ fubigi o naba. (They amputated his leg.)
See: fibigi.  O fubigi la o zo zoobu ni. (He outran his friend.) A fubigi ya, di niŋ lala yaha. (You've overdone it, don't do that again.)
See: fifali.  Fufali gbaagi tiŋa maa. (Famine caught the land.) Niriba diri la mɔɣuni bindira fufali saha. (People eat bush foods during famine.)

Sim: bomaboma, bɔribɔri, gandimgandim, chɛrisi, fuhi, liɣisi, pɔrisi. Read: Naden, Tony 00.  N daa lihi, n nya wɔr' fufuhigu. (I looked and saw a dappled horse.) Wɔr' fufuhisi vɔri chɛchɛbuŋ' din pah' anahi. (Dappled horses drew the fourth chariot.)

english: to boil, foam; to scald, burn with hot water
example: Variant: fuɣim. Sim: fuɣiri, gbirigi, kpeei, tam, di, kabi, nyo, nyooi, wɔbigi, taai. See: fuɣifuɣi; fuɣiri; fuɣurili.  O fuɣiya. (He boiled with anger.) Kom maa fuɣi ya. (The water boiled.) O fuɣi o nuu ni kotulim. (He scalded his hand with hot water.)

english: to remove by force, tear off; to strip, undress
example: Colloc: fuɣi napɔŋ, fuɣi X kparibu. Sim: yeei. Cpart: ye kparibu.  O fuɣi o chinchini maa bahi, ka guui ni o zaɣim. (He tore off his cloth and ran away naked.) Bɛ daa darigimi fuɣ' o binyɛr' shɛli o ni daa yɛ. (They pulled off the garment he was wearing.) Miriya ka yi chaŋ hal ka yi naba ti fuɣi. (Don't walk till your feet wear out.) Bɛ fuɣi o kparibu, ka o bi lahi nyɛ naa. (They stripped his chieftaincy robes, and he was no longer chief.)

Sim: fuɣuluŋ. See: nintɔfuɣifuɣi.  Nyini n-zaŋ a yuri siɣi teeku ni, ka fuɣifuɣi pani di ko' titali zuɣu. (You trampled the sea with your horses, and the mighty waters foamed.) Nintɔfuɣifuɣi ŋariti o noli. (Foaming spittle blocks his mouth.) Bɛ vi tuma ŋmanila kofuɣifuɣi. (Their shameful deeds are like foam on the water.)
O ŋmɛ la fuɣifuɣi n pili bindirigu. (He broke clods to cover the seeds.) Fuɣifuɣi maa be puu ni. (The clods are in the farm.)
See: fuɣi.  Kom maa fuɣim ya. (The water is boiling.) O fuɣim la suli ni. (He boiled with anger.)
Variant: fuɣiri.  O fuɣim la binyɛra maa zaa. (He scattered all the things.) Di fuɣim binyɛra yiŋa. (Don't litter things around the house.)
Sim: fuɣi, gbirigi, kpeei, tam. See: fuɣi.  Kom maa fuɣiri la duɣu ni. (The water is boiling over in the pot.) Saɣim maa fuɣiri la. (The t.z. is boiling over.)
See: fuɣim.  O fuɣiri la binyɛra maa. (He is scattering the things.) Di fuɣiri binyɛra yiŋa. (Don't scatter things around the house.)

english: to slough the skin; to peel, be rough (skin); to be damaged on surface
example: See: waɣifuɣisinli; fuɣi.  Toliga fuɣisiya ka yiɣi chaŋ. (Locusts shed their skins and fly away.) Wahu maa fuɣisiya. (The snake has sloughed its skin.) Di fuɣsimi. (It is tarnished/rusted.)
O nya wahu fuɣisili mɔɣu ni. (He saw a snake's sloughed skin in the bush.) Fuɣisili maa be tiŋa. (The sloughed skin is on the ground.)
See: fuɣisili. SynD: fuɣisili (W.D.).  Wahu fuɣisim maa be mɔɣu ni. (The snake's slough is in the bush.) O zaŋ fuɣisim n wuhi bihi maa. (He showed the slough to the children.)
Sim: falim, faluŋ, filim, kɔɣisi, faai, faɣafaɣa, fuliŋ.  O kpiɣi la dɔɣu fuɣu. (He carried a light stick.) Bindirigu maa nyɛla fuɣu. (The food is light.)
From: (Moore).  O kparila fuɣu. (He wears a Mossi-type smock.) Fuɣu maa viɛla pam. (The smock is very beautiful.)
Variant: fuɣulum. Sim: fuɣifuɣi.  Kom maa ni, fuɣuluŋ yiɣira. (In the water, bubbles rise.) Fuɣulima pam be kom maa ni. (There are many bubbles in the water.)

See: fuɣi; fuɣifuɣi. Sim: alahali, anyɔɣiti, chamba, chɛchito, diɣi, guŋgumbo, guŋgunsalli, guŋguŋkpilli, gbiliŋgbi, kalinchaŋ, kaŋgbariŋga, kulikuliga, kpahijo, kpina/kpinu, kpiriŋga, laribako/laabako, lilia, nanuɣila, nyɛŋkpɛliŋga, nyusoli, nyuwɔɣipiɛlli, nyuwɔɣu, pakolzandee, sangalinjo, sayɛto, tawulli, tɛrili, wɔrilani, ziɣilaŋgbo, zɔŋ.  O ko la fuɣurili o pɔŋ ni. (He planted fuɣurili yams.) Fuɣurili maa wɔla pam. (The fuɣurili yams have produced well.)
Sim: bomaboma, bɔribɔri, 'fufuhigu, gandimgandim, chɛrisi, liɣisi, pɔrisi. See: 'fufuhigu.  O noo maa fuhi ya. (His fowl is spotted.) Binkɔbigu maa fuhi ya. (The animal is patterned.)

Sim: filigi, golisi, bara, fɔhiri, maɣam.  A yi fuhiri a baŋa barilim bansi yi ti laɣim a dini maa ni lɛbi nintua. (If you boast about your bracelet's thickness when bracelets gather, yours will become a ring.) Zilinli fuhiri ŋun ni di nyaɣisim shɛm, ka yoli kɔhim ka di fo. (The tongue boasts of enjoyment, but when the penis ejaculates, the tongue becomes silent.)
Gen: fu. See: fu.  O fuhi la dakuɣu maa. (He dragged the log.) Bɛ fuhi la lɔri maa yiɣisibu. (They dragged to lift the lorry.)

english: to spit (cobra); to spit out water in sprinkles; to sniff
example: Sapaɣu maa fuhi la. (The cobra spat.) O fuhi la kom maa. (He spat out the water in sprinkles.) O fuhi la o nyee ni. (He sniffed.)
See: kaɣili.  O zaŋ fukaɣili n niŋ tim maa ni. (He put lemon-grass in the medicine.) Fukaɣili maa nyɔm viɛla. (Lemon-grass smells nice.)

From: (Ha.) > (Ar.). Colloc: su fukumsi.  Paɣ' kpuɣibo yuya sabbu fukumsi tuma duu zuɣulana. (The head of the marriage registry office.) Nahu maa yi pun ʒiɛri niriba ka bɛ yɛl' o lana ka o bi niŋ o fukumsi. (If the ox has gored before and the owner didn't control it.) Yinim' bɛn zaa tumdi tuma ka di fukumsi ʒi' tibisa ʒi yi zuɣu. (All you who work and are heavy-laden.) Piimiya niriba ayopɔin ka ti chɛ ka bɛ su tuma ŋɔ fukumsi. (Choose seven people to be responsible for this work.)
Di simdi ni sokam doli nam fukumsinim' yɛligu. (Everyone should obey the authorities.) Fukumsinima maa zaa laɣim ya. (All the people in authority gathered.) Colloc: nam fukumsinima. See: -nima.
From: (Ha.).  O diri la fula ni bihim. (He eats fura with milk.) Fula nyɛla bindirigu Dagbaŋ. (Fura is a food in Dagbong.)
Fulani maa guli la niɣi. (The Fulani person herds cattle.) Fulannima pam be tiŋa ŋɔ ni. (Many Fulani people are in this area.)
Colloc: to fule.  O to la fule n sabigi o nyina. (He used tobacco flower to colour his teeth.) Fule maa be tia maa zuɣu. (The flower is on the plant.)

english: to become dim, clouded (eyes); to fail to mature (grain)
example: See: 'fuluŋ.  N daa lihila zuɣusaa hal ka n nina ti fulim. (I looked to the heavens till my eyes grew dim.) O nina daa fulim ka o bi lan nyara. (His eyes were dim, he couldn't see.) Ka' maa fulim ya. (The millet failed to mature.)
Colloc: ka'fulima. Sim: falim, faluŋ, filim, kɔɣisi, faai, 'fuɣu, faɣafaɣa.  O kpiɣi la binshɛɣu fuliŋ. (He carried a light thing.) Ka' fulima so mɔri. (Immature millet is better than mere grass.)

english: to ward off a curse (by crying)
example: See: fulo.  O fulisi la ni o gu o maŋa ka chɛ noli. (He warded off the curse to protect himself.) "A fulo fuu. N daŋ a!" ka o fulisi. ("A fig for your curse, I got in first!" he cried.)
O diri la fulli ni bihim. (He eats fura balls with milk.) Fulli maa nyɛla din mali alaafee. (Fura balls are healthy.)
See: fulisi.  Fulo! di niŋ lala. (God forbid! don't do that.) A fulo fuu. N daŋ a! (A fig for your curse, I got in first!)
See: falofalo.  O kparila chinchin' fulofulo. (She wore a light cloth.) Pɔhim fulofulo maa ʒɛya. (A light wind is blowing.)
See: fulim; ninfuluŋ.  O nyɛla ninfuluŋ. (He is blind.) Nimbihi' fulima maa bi lahi neera. (The blind eyes no longer see.)
Kum saha, niriba diri la fuluŋfuɣu. (In famine, people eat fuluŋfuɣu plant.) Fuluŋfuɣu maa be mɔɣu ni. (The plant is in the bush.)
Colloc: naɔgban' fumfulum lana.  O mali fumfulum, o yɛri yomyom. (He speaks quickly.) Naŋgban' fumfulumlana m-piiri dabili. (The one who speaks quickly finds a slave.)
O yɛri funafuna. (He speaks rudely.) Funafuna bi viɛla. (Rudeness is not good.)

english: to miss each other; to surprise, catch in bad act
example: See: findi.  Bɛ chaŋmi fundi taba. (They went and missed each other.) O fundi o la ni o zura. (He caught him stealing.)

english: mark made on virgin land to indicate intention to farm
example: O niŋ la funfani n baŋsi ni o yɛn ko. (He made a mark to show he will farm.) Funfani maa be mɔɣu ni. (The farm-mark is in the bush.)
See: finyia.  O yihi o funyia. (He disgraced him.) Funyia bi viɛla. (Disgrace is not good.)

Variant: fun. Colloc: suhu fuŋ. See: suhufuŋbo.  Dabiɛm gbaag' o ka o suhu fuŋ pam. (Fear overcame him and his heart fainted.) O zuɣu yi yaara pampam ka o nina lihiri fun. (She has bad headaches and her eyes look dim.) Dabiɛm yi gbaai so bee so suhu yi fuŋ, dilana ŋun' labimi kul' o yiŋa. (If anyone is afraid or fainthearted, that person should go home.)

english: to sink; to push into a hole
example: See: firi.  O furi la baɣiri ni. (He sank in the mud.) Kuɣili maa furi o gbɛɣu ni. (The stone embedded in his forehead.)
See: firila.  O zaŋ furila n neei duu maa. (He used a lamp to light the room.) Furila maa neeri pam. (The lamp shines brightly.)
Syn: kaakaachi.  O zaŋ furintua n niŋ kpam maa. (He used a funnel to pour the oil.) Furintua maa be teebuli zuɣu. (The funnel is on the table.)

english: to sneeze; to snort; to whinny, neigh
example: See: firisi.  O furisi la. (He sneezed.) Wahu maa furisiri la. (The horse is neighing.)

english: to show off (giving money), compete; to hiss (snake)
example: Sim: filigi, golisi.  O futili la ni o ti liɣiri pam. (He showed off by giving much money.) Wahu maa futili la. (The snake hissed.)
Di nyɛla fuu. (It is whitish/unclear.) O nina lihiri fuu. (His eyes look unclear.)
Colloc: vuhi fuu.  O vuhi la fuu. (He breathed a sigh of relief.) Fuu maa wumiya. (The sigh was heard.)

english: to button, hook; to fasten into yoke; to chain
example: O ga o daliya maa dama wuntaŋa maa biɛla. (He buttoned his shirt because it was a bit cold.) Bɛ ga la niɣi maa dapua ni n ti ko puu. (They yoked the oxen to plough the farm.) O ga o maŋa ka chɛ o zoobu. (He held himself back from running.)

See: gaai.  O ga la bɔɣili o pɔŋ ni n ti sɔɣi saɣiri. (He dug a pit in his farm to bury rubbish.) Bɛ ga la voli n ti sɔɣi kpiimba maa. (They dug a hole to bury the dead.) O ga la tiŋa n ti ko sima. (He dug the ground to plant groundnuts.)

english: to eat without broth; to eat dry food
example: Saha shɛli kum ni, niriba ga la bindirigu ka bi nyuri kom. (During famine, people eat dry food without drinking water.) Biɛla maa ga la sima ka bi bɔri ʒiɛri. (The children ate groundnuts without wanting soup.) O ga la saɣim dama ʒiɛri maa naai ya. (He ate dry t.z. because the soup finished.)

english: to eat without broth; to eat faeces
example: O ga la saɣim ka bi di ʒiɛri. (He ate t.z. without soup.) Baa maa ga la bindi. (The dog ate faeces.)
From: (Ha.).  Gaa sima! n kɔhiri li laɣifu. (See! Groundnuts! I'm selling them cheap.) Gaa nyuya! di nyɛɣisa pam. (See! Yams! They are very good.)
See: kulagaa.  O zaŋ gaa n ko o puu Asibiri kam. (He uses a hoe to weed his farm every Saturday.) Gaa maa nyɛla din nyɛɣisa, o tooi ko viɛnyɛla. (The hoe is sharp, he can farm well.)

Etym: OV *GA.  Gaa tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, biɛla tooi diri di wala. (The ebony tree is in the bush, children often eat its fruits.) Gaa wula kpe nee wula ni. (The ebony's branches enter the nee's branches.) Fiig tia ni tooi wali olivi wala bee gaa ni tooi wali fiig wala? (Can a fig tree bear olives or an ebony bear figs?)

Colloc: niŋ gaaba. Sim: gaasa.  O niŋ la gaaba ni o nyɛŋ o zo tuma ni. (He challenged to surpass his friend in work.) Gaaba maa zuɣu, bɛ zaa tum viɛnyɛla. (Because of the challenge, they all worked well.)

From: (Ha.) < (Ar.). Colloc: bo gaafara, nya gaafara, niŋ gaafara, suhi gaafara.  O tum taali ka bo gaafara o ba sani. (He did wrong and sought forgiveness from his father.) Gaafara! m bɔri ni n gari. (Excuse me! I want to pass.) O niŋ gaafara ka bɛ yoogi o. (He knocked and they opened for him.)

Colloc: gaai bɔɣili. Sim: gbi.  O gaai bɔɣili n gili duu maa n ti sɔɣi saɣiri. (He dug a trench around the room to bury rubbish.) Bɛ gaai voli n ti sɔɣi bini. (They dug a shallow hole to bury something.)
O gaai o bia pam, dinzuɣu o bi wumdi o. (She pampers her child too much, so he doesn't obey her.) Gaai biɛɣu bi viɛla, di saɣinda bihi. (Being too indulgent is not good, it spoils children.)

english: to unbutton; to unhook; to release, untie, set free
example: Cpart: ga.  O gaai o daliya maa dama tula pam. (He unbuttoned his shirt because it's very hot.) Pumpɔŋɔ ti yɛn gaai la o zoligɔnim' maa. (Today we will free him from his chains.) Ŋuni n-lee gaai mɔɣu ni bun' naba ni tanachi? (Who loosed the wild donkey's feet from the hobble?) Colloc: gaai zuli 'to die'

See: Ghana.  Gaana tiŋgbani ŋɔ ni, ti diri la bindira pam. (In this country of Ghana, we eat many foods.) Gaana bela tuuli tiŋgbana anu shɛŋa din mali alaafee yɛla. (Ghana is one of the first five countries with health matters.)
See: gali.  O gaani yuŋ maa zaa ka guli o ba. (He stayed awake all night watching his father.) N gaani gom waawaayili, n bi tooi gbihi. (I often stay awake at night, I can't sleep.)

From: (Ha.).  O diri la gaari ni ko'mahim asiba kam. (He eats garri with cold water every morning.) Gaari nyɛla bindirigu din niŋdi yomyom, a yi mali kom. (Garri is food that is made quickly, if you have water.)

english: effort, competitive effort, desire to outdo
example: Colloc: niŋ gaasa. Syn: nia.  O niŋ gaasa ni o naai tuma maa tuuli. (He made an effort to finish the work first.) Gaasa maa zuɣu, o tum viɛnyɛla. (Because of the competitive effort, he worked well.)

From: (Eng.) 'gas'. Sim: firila.  O zaŋ gaasi n neei duu maa yuŋ. (He used a pressure lamp to light the room at night.) Gaasi maa neeri pam, ti tooi karim di ni. (The pressure lamp shines brightly, we can read by it.)

english: to mix up, confuse; to tangle
example: O gabi la mi' shɛŋa maa zaa, dinzuɣu di bi tuɣi taba. (He mixed up all the threads, so they don't match.) A yi chana viɛnyɛla, binshɛli ku gabi a naba. (If you walk carefully, nothing will tangle your feet.) Kpaluhi maa gabi la mi' piɛlli mini mi' sabinli. (The weavers mixed white and black threads.)

Syn: mia.  O zaŋ gabiga n lo bua maa ka di bi zo. (He used a rope to tie the goat so it wouldn't run away.) Gabiga maa chihiya dama di kurigi pam. (The rope broke because it was too old.) Biɛla maa zaŋ gabiga n gbahi o ba wahu. (The child used a rope to catch his father's horse.)

Gen: wahu.  O ba la gabiga viɛlli daa ni Asibiri dali. (He bought a beautiful strawberry roan horse at the market on Saturday.) Gabiga maa nyɛla wahu din zɔri yomyom. (The strawberry roan is a horse that runs fast.) N chaŋ ti nya o gabiga maa o yiŋa. (I went to see his strawberry roan horse at his house.)

See: gabiliga.  Biɛla maa tɔhi la gabila n diri mɔɣu ni. (The children picked gabila fruits in the bush and ate them.) Gabila maa nyɛɣisa pam, n bɔri ni n lan tɔhi. (The gabila fruits are very sweet, I want to pick more.)

See: gabo.  O ma duɣi la gabilee n-ti bihi maa asiba zaŋ piligu. (Her mother cooked bean patties for the children early in the morning.) Gabilee nyɛla bindirigu din niŋdi yomyom ka di nyɛɣisa. (Bean patties are food that cooks quickly and is tasty.)

O gaba biɛri o dama o lu ya zuŋɔ asiba. (His breastbone hurts because he fell this morning.) Bɛ zaŋ gaba n ti o dama o nyɛla saamba. (They gave him the shoulder joint because he is a guest.) O mali gabili pam dama o di nasara. (He has great pride because he won.)

Colloc: Gab' Naa 'elder title'.  Gabiliga tia maa be la mɔɣu ni ka biɛla bɔri di wala. (The gabiliga tree is in the bush and children look for its fruits.) Gabilisi pam be tiŋa ŋɔ ni, bɛ wala nyɛɣisa. (Many gabiliga trees are in this area, their fruits are sweet.)
See: gabiliga.  O tɔhi la gabilli n ti o ma. (He picked gabila fruits and gave to his mother.) Gabilli maa wɔla pam yuuni ŋɔ. (The gabila fruits have produced a lot this year.)

See: gabo; nyuli. Sim: alibasa.  O zaŋ gabinyuli n niŋ chɛri ni ka di nyɛɣisa. (She put onion in the soup and it became tasty.) Gabinyuya maa du zuɣusaa pam daa ni. (The onions are piled high in the market.)

See: gabiga.  O da la gabiŋmani n kɔhi daa ni ka niriba pam da li. (He bought a wholesale lot of 00 ropes to sell in the market and many people bought.) Gabiŋmana ata n-be o butiki ni. (He has three wholesale lots of 00 ropes in his shop.)
Kom daɣiri maa gabisi la bindirigu maa, ti bi lan tooi di li. (The dirty water tainted the food, we can no longer eat it.) Di gabisi a nuu maa ni daɣiri. (Don't dirty your hands with filth.)

From: (Ha.). Sim: alibasa.  O zaŋ gabo n niŋ chɛri ni ka di ʒɛri maa nyɛɣisa. (She put onion greens in the soup and the soup became tasty.) Gabo maa zooi pam o pɔŋ ni yuuni ŋɔ. (The onion greens grew a lot in her garden this year.)

From: (Ha.). Colloc: niŋ gadama.  Bɛ niŋ la gadama soli zuɣu dama bɛ bi tooi laɣim taba. (They had a dispute on the road because they couldn't agree.) Gadama be tiŋa maa ni, niriba zaa zɔra. (There is violence in the town, everyone is running.) Gadama maa zuɣu, daa maa bi lahi niŋ. (Because of the rioting, the market didn't happen.)
See: -nima.  Gadamanima maa wurindi la tiŋa maa. (The rioters are destroying the town.) Bɛ gbaai la gadamanima maa zaa. (They caught all the rioters.)

From: (Ha.).  O zaŋ o Alikuraan n niŋ gafaka ni ka di bi daɣira. (He put his Qur'an in a leather case so it doesn't get dirty.) Gafaka maa nyɛla gbanzaba ni shɛ shɛli. (The leather case was sewn by a leatherworker.)
O tuma maa nyɛla gafugafu, di bi viɛla. (His work is sloppy, it's not good.) O chani la gafugafu ka niriba lara o. (He walks sloppily and people laugh at him.)

english: to hold between teeth; to recriminate, grumble
example: Colloc: tab' gaɣibu.  O gaɣi la sigarɛti maa o nyina ni. (He held the cigarette between his teeth.) Bɛ gaɣiri la taba dama bɛ bi saɣi ti yɛltɔɣa maa. (They are recriminating against each other because they don't agree on the matter.)

See: -lana.  O nyɛla gaɣigaɣilana, o mini so bi be. (He is a quarrelsome person, he doesn't get along with anyone.) Gaɣigaɣilana maa zuɣu, ti bi tooi tum tuma viɛnyɛla. (Because of the quarrelsome person, we can't work well.)

O zaŋ gaɣili n ʒi kom yiŋa na. (She used a gourd to carry water home.) Gaɣili din mali kom bi chaɣisira, gaɣ' sɔɣu n chaɣisira. (A gourd with water doesn't rattle, an empty one rattles.) O zaŋ gaɣili n niŋ sima. (She put groundnuts in a gourd.)

english: to walk as if something in anus; to stutter
example: O gaɣindi la o chandi ni kamani o biɛri shee. (He walks strangely as if something hurts him.) O gaɣindi la yɛltɔɣa tɔɣisibu ni, di yuui pam. (He stutters when speaking, it takes a long time.)

english: to swing, wave; to beat off (flies); to beckon
example: Colloc: gahi nuu.  O gahi o nuu n boli o zo maa na. (He waved his hand to call his friend.) Zɔhi maa pam, o gahiri la o nuu n kariti ba. (There are many flies, he's waving his hand to chase them away.) O gahi o zuli ka baa maa kana. (He wagged his tail and the dog came.)

english: to choose, sort out; to pick up; to show favouritism
example: Syn: gaŋ, pihi.  O ʒini tiŋa n gahim bindira maa ka yihi din viɛla. (He sat down sorting the food and took out the good ones.) O gahim la kuɣ' polipoli diba anu n ti o bia. (He picked out five smooth stones for his child.) Karimba maa bi gahimdi bihi, o tibirila sokam yim. (The teacher doesn't show favouritism, he treats everyone the same.)

See: gahim.  Ŋɔ nyɛla n bindir' gahindili, n diri li Asibiri kam. (This is my favourite food, I eat it every Saturday.) Bɛ ti o la ʒishe' gahindili dama o nyɛla saamba. (They gave him a special seat because he is a guest.) Kuɣ' shɛli bɛ ni zaŋ mɛ duu maa nyɛla kuɣ' gahindili. (The stone they used to build the room is a special stone.)

Gahiŋgoligu tia maa bala pam, di dɔɣu tooi chɛra. (The gahiŋgoligu tree is very soft, its wood breaks easily.) Bɛ zaŋdi gahiŋgoligu dɔri n tibiri dɔriti. (They use gahiŋgoligu wood to treat illnesses.)
See: galli.  Noo maa nye la gala anu asiba. (The hen laid five eggs this morning.) Gala maa be koligu ni, ti zaŋ ti daa. (The eggs are in the basket, we'll take them to market.)

Colloc: tum gala. Syn: taali.  O tum la gala ka o ba je o. (He committed an offence and his father is angry with him.) Pa gala ka o niŋ maa, di nyɛla yɛlimaŋli. (What he did is not an offence, it's the truth.)
O yɛri la galagala, so bi baŋdi o ni yɛri shɛli. (He speaks foolishly, no one understands what he's saying.) Di niŋ galagala ka o zo n chaŋ. (He ran meaninglessly for no reason.)
Kurimbuni, tɔbbihi daa gbiri la galakanje n tuhira. (In the past, soldiers carried battle-axes to fight.) Galakanje maa nyɛla kuriti din tibsa. (The battle-axe is heavy iron.)

english: Galba Naa (th wife of Yaa Naa)
example: See: naa.  Galba Naa maa nyɛla paɣa so ŋun mali yɛm pam. (The Galba Naa is a very wise woman.) Bɛ piigi o la Galba Naa dama o nyɛla ŋun mali anfaani. (They appointed her Galba Naa because she is useful.)
O nyɛla ninvuɣ' galɔŋ, o bi wumdi so saɣisigu. (He is a stubborn person, he doesn't listen to anyone's advice.) Bihi galɔŋ maa bi wumdi bɛ karimba. (The rebellious children don't obey their teacher.)

From: (Eng.).  O da la galɔŋ kpam daa ni n ti duɣi bindirigu. (He bought a gallon of oil in the market for cooking.) Galɔŋ maa pali kom, ti tooi nyu di zaa. (The gallon container is full of water, we can drink it all.)

See: ga; sagali.  O diri la bindir' gali dama ʒiɛri maa na bi duɣi. (He eats dry food because the soup isn't cooked yet.) Saɣim gali bi viɛla ŋubu, a bɔri la ʒiɛri. (Dry t.z. is not good to eat, you need soup.)

O pa o wahu gali ka ba li. (He saddled his horse and rode it.) Gali maa nyɛla din bala, wahu maa bi niŋ wahala. (The saddle is soft, the horse doesn't struggle.) O da gali palli daa ni n ti o wahu. (He bought a new saddle in the market for his horse.) Colloc: pa gali.

Sim: ŋarinli.  Biɛla maa tɔhi la gaya mɔɣu ni n diri. (The children picked monkey-guava fruits in the bush and ate them.) Gaya maa nyɛɣisa pam, n bɔri ni n lan tɔhi. (The monkey-guavas are very sweet, I want to pick more.)

Sim: mia, gabiga.  O zaŋ gali ʒee n lo o nuu maa. (She tied a red thread on her hand.) Gali maa chihiya, o tu ni o taɣi li. (The thread broke, she needs to replace it.) Paɣaba maa mali gali n wuɣiri chinchini. (The women use thread to weave cloth.)
Wahu maa gali ya dama bɛ bi ba ri li saha shɛli. (The horse became wild because they don't ride it often.) O bi lahi tooi ba wahu gali maa. (He can no longer ride the wild horse.)

english: to keep vigil, pass sleepless night; to fail to germinate
example: Colloc: gali gom.  Ti gali la gom yuŋ maa zaa dama ti bi tooi gbihi. (We stayed awake all night because we couldn't sleep.) O gali gom waawaayili dama o tɛhiri la o biɛhigu yɛla. (He often passes sleepless nights thinking about his life.) Sima maa gali ya dama saa bi mi. (The groundnuts failed to germinate because there was no rain.)

english: to revive, bring back to life; to survive
example: Syn: galiŋ. Ant: kpi.  Bɛ ti o la tim ka o gali. (They gave him medicine and he revived.) A bia maa ni gali, di bi yɛn kpi. (Your child will survive, he won't die.) Saa maa nyaaŋa, tihi maa zaa gali ya. (After the rain, all the trees revived.)

From: (Eng.) < (Latin) < (Grk.).  Bɛ zaŋ galibanom n niŋ tulaale maa ni ka di nyɔm viɛla. (They put galbanum in the perfume to make it smell nice.) Galibanom nyɛla din yɔɣira daa ni. (Galbanum is sold in the market.)

See: galikuli.  O zaŋ galiga n niŋ wahu maa noli ni ka tooi su li. (He put the bit in the horse's mouth and could control it.) Galiga maa bi viɛla, wahu maa bi wumdi li. (The bit is not good, the horse doesn't obey it.)

See: gali.  O nyu galiga ka gali, din daa yɛn kpi. (He took reviving medicine and came back to life, he was about to die.) Galiga maa nyɛla timalana ni ti o shɛli. (The reviving medicine was given to him by a herbalist.)

See: gali.  O timsim galigbaŋa maa ka wahu maa bi damdi. (He tightened the saddle-girth so the saddle wouldn't move.) Galigbaŋa maa nyɛla gbanzaba ni shɛ shɛli. (The cinch was sewn by a leatherworker.)
See: galige; gbɔŋ.  O gbibi la galigbɔŋ maa n suuri wahu maa. (He holds the reins to steer the horse.) Galigbɔŋ maa chihiya ka wahu zo n chaŋ. (The reins broke and the horse ran away.)

See: galikuli. Sim: langami.  O lo la galige wahu maa noli ni ka di bi lahi zo. (He put the bridle on the horse's mouth so it wouldn't run.) Galige maa chihiya, o tu ni o taɣi li. (The bridle broke, he needs to replace it.)
See: gali; jɛni.  O zaŋ galijɛni n niŋ gali maa ni ka di bi gabi. (She put the thread on a reel so it wouldn't tangle.) Galijɛna maa zaa ʒiya teebuli zuɣu. (All the reels are on the table.)

Sim: lɔɣibuya, nyɔtaɣirigu.  Kurimbuni, tɔbbihi daa gbiri la galikua n taɣiri bɛ maŋa. (In the past, soldiers carried shields to protect themselves.) Galikua maa nyɛla dɔɣu ni gbaŋ. (The shield is made of wood and leather.)

Syn: chɛbili.  O zaŋ o naba n niŋ galikuli ni ka ba wahu maa. (He put his foot in the stirrup and rode the horse.) Galikuli maa yi lu, o ku tooi ba wahu maa. (If the stirrup falls, he can't ride the horse.)
See: gali.  Wahu maa galim ya dama bɛ bi ba ri li saha shɛli. (The horse became wild because they don't ride it often.) O bi lahi tooi ba o. (He can no longer ride it.)

english: to offend, be guilty; to blame, reprimand; to disconcert
example: A galmiya, a tu ni a suhi gaafara. (You are guilty, you should apologize.) O galim o zo maa ni o bi tum tuma maa. (He blamed his friend for not doing the work.) Di galim a mi, ti ni tooi niŋ li. (Don't be disconcerted, we can do it.)

See: 'galiŋ.  Ninvuɣ' galima maa bi tumdi tuma, bɛ kul gindi la. (The worthless people don't work, they just roam around.) O nyɛla ninvuɣ' galiŋ, so bi jɛri o. (He is a worthless person, no one likes him.)

See: galim. Colloc: ka galimi.  O tuma maa puuni, o ka galimi. (In his work, he is blameless.) O yi deeg' o galimi, ti ni lahi sɔŋ o. (If he admits his fault, we will still help him.) Bɛ zaŋ taali maa galimi ʒili o. (They put the blame on him.)

See: gampiliga.  Galinʒiɛɣu tia maa waɣila, di mahim gili la yaɣili maa zaa. (The ficus tree is tall, its shade covers the whole area.) Bɛ ʒiya galinʒiɛɣu gbinni n vuhira. (They are sitting under the ficus tree resting.)

See: galinʒiɛɣu; laa.  Galinʒiɛlaa tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, biɛla tooi diri di wala. (The ficus tree is in the bush, children often eat its fruits.) Galinʒiɛlaa wɔla pam yuuni ŋɔ. (The ficus tree has fruited a lot this year.)

See: galinʒiɛɣu; -nyaŋ.  Galinʒiɛnyaŋ tia maa nyɛla din mali anfaani, bɛ zaŋdi di vari n tibiri dɔriti. (The ficus tree is useful, they use its leaves for medicine.) Bɛ ʒiya galinʒiɛnyaŋ gbinni n diɛmda. (They are sitting under the ficus tree playing.)
O nyɛla ninvuɣ' galiŋ, o bi bɔri tuma. (He is a worthless person, he doesn't want to work.) Yiya ayi baa nyɛla ba' galiŋ, o bi wumdi so. (A dog of two homes is stubborn, it doesn't obey anyone.)

english: to revive, come back to life
example: Syn: gali.  O ni daa ti kpi, bɛ ti o tim ka o galiŋ. (When he was dying, they gave him medicine and he revived.) A bia maa ni galiŋ, di zo dabiɛm. (Your child will recover, don't be afraid.)
Sim: viligi. Cpart: kaai, mili.  O galiŋ la mia maa ka di yɛligi. (He unwound the rope and it stretched out.) Galiŋmi li ka ti labi mili. (Unwind it so we can rewind it properly.)
See: galli; piɛlli.  O zaŋ galipiɛlli n pahi bindirigu ni ka di mali alaafee. (She added egg white to the food to make it healthy.) Galipiɛlli maa piɛla pam. (The egg white is very white.)

english: to be big, large, great; to be plentiful
example: Sim: bara, zooi. Cpart: pɔra.  O puu maa galisiya pam, o kori la bindirigu pam. (His farm is very big, he grows a lot of food.) Saa maa ni daa mi, kom maa galisiya mɔɣili ni. (When the rain fell, the water became plentiful in the dam.) Ti daalahichi galisiya, ti tu ni ti taɣi. (Our sins are many, we must change.)

english: to reach out and give, hand over
example: O galisi o liɣiri maa n ti o ma. (He handed over the money to his mother.) Galisim li n-ti ma, m borila li. (Reach out and give it to me, I need it.)

See: galisi.  Yaa Naa mali paɣaba pam, bɛ galisim ŋɔ zuɣu bɛ mali la duuri pam. (The Yaa Naa has many wives, because of their number they have many rooms.) Di galisim zuɣu, ti bi tooi ʒi di zaa. (Because of its size, we can't carry it all.) Alizanda galisim nyɛla din gari ti tɛha. (Heaven's greatness is beyond our thinking.)
See: galli; wɔɣu.  O zaŋ galiwɔɣu n bahi, di bi lahi mali anfaani. (She threw away the eggshell, it's no longer useful.) Galiwɔɣu maa nyɛɣira di yi ti lu tiŋa. (The eggshell breaks when it falls.)
See: galli.  O zaŋ galiʒiɛm n niŋ bindirigu ni ka di ʒee. (She added egg yolk to the food to make it yellow.) Galiʒiɛm maa mali alaafee pam n-ti bihi. (Egg yolk is very healthy for children.)

Noo maa nye la galli Asibiri kam asiba zaŋ piligu. (The hen lays an egg every Saturday early morning.) Bɛ bi ʒiri gala n-chani daa ni dama di nyɛɣira. (People don't carry eggs to market because they break.) O da la gala pia daa ni n ti o ma. (He bought ten eggs in the market for his mother.) Colloc: nye gala 'lay eggs'

See: galiŋ.  O nyɛla galuŋ, o bi taɣiri o biɛhigu. (He is stubborn, he doesn't change his ways.) Galuŋ bi wumdi saɣisigu, dinzuɣu niriba bi jɛri o. (A stubborn person doesn't listen to advice, so people don't like him.)

english: to be in excess; to be excessively sth.
example: Colloc: gam chɔri, gam sariya.  Saa maa mi o mibu maa gam sariya, ti bi tooi yi yiŋ. (The rain fell excessively, we couldn't go out.) Niriba ban daa laɣim daa gam la sariya, ti bi tooi kali ba. (The people who gathered were too many, we couldn't count them.) Paɣ' so ŋun viɛlim gam nyɛla ŋun ʒi tiŋa ŋɔ ni. (An extremely beautiful woman lives in this town.)

english: to walk freely; to swagger; to go without something
example: O ʒiri la binshɛɣu dama o bi gamda o chandi ni. (He is carrying something so he doesn't walk freely.) O gamdi la o chandi ni kamani o nyɛla bundana. (He swaggers as he walks like he's a rich person.) O chaŋ daa ni ka gamdi kunna, o bi nya o ni bɔri shɛli. (He went to market and came back empty-handed, he didn't find what he wanted.)
See: gam; zuɣu.  O chaŋ o gamazuɣu, so bi doli o. (He went alone, no one followed him.) O bi bɔri sɔŋsim, o bɔri ni o niŋ o tuma o gamazuɣu. (He doesn't want help, he wants to do his work alone.)
Gambanda goli maa puuni, ti puhiri la chuɣu. (During Gambanda month, we celebrate a festival.) Gambanda nyaaŋa ka Bandacheena goli paana. (After Gambanda, Bandacheena month comes.)

english: bad gate (for faithful wives of deceased chief)
example: See: gamli; biɛ. Colloc: gambee gooni.  Bɛ zaŋ gambee n yihi paɣaba maa naai, dinzuɣu bɛ bi lahi kpɛri. (They used the bad gate to bring out the wives, so they don't enter again.) Gambee gooni maa bi waɣa, di nyɛla din bi waɣa. (The bad gate wall is short, it's not tall.)

See: gamli; biɛɣu. Colloc: gambeeyirigu bia.  O nyɛla gambeeyirigu, o bi yi gambee ni kamani paɣaba maa. (She is a faithful wife, she didn't go out through the bad gate like the others.) Gambeeyirigu bia nyɛla ŋun mali jilima tiŋa ni. (A faithful wife's child has honour in the town.)

O labi la gambo n wuɣiri chinchini viɛnyɛla. (He throws the shuttle weaving cloth very well.) Gambo maa chani yomyom, o naan tooi wuɣi chinchini pam. (The shuttle moves quickly, he can weave a lot of cloth.)
See: gamu.  Gambu n-lu ti zuɣu, ti bi lahi mi ti ni yɛn niŋ bɔ. (Disaster has fallen on us, we don't know what to do.) Ti gu ti maŋa viɛnyɛla ka chɛ gambu. (We protect ourselves well from disaster.)

From: (Ha.). Sim: dunoli.  O zaŋ gamli n pɔbi dunoli ka biɛɣu maa bi kpe. (He used a mat to close the doorway so the day wouldn't enter.) Gamli maa chɛhiya, o tu ni o taɣi li. (The door mat tore, he needs to replace it.)

Sim: nɔsuɣu.  O zaŋ noo maa n niŋ gampiliga ni ka di bi zo. (He put the fowl in a crate so it wouldn't run away.) Gampiliga maa viɛla, noo maa be di ni ni suhudoo. (The crate is good, the fowl is in it peacefully.)

See: galinʒiɛɣu.  Gampiliga tia maa waɣila, di mahim viɛla pam. (The ficus tree is tall, its shade is very good.) Naawuni n-zooi guŋa, gampiri' suma di jɛri suli. (God made the kapok grow, good ficus trees shouldn't be angry.)

See: gampiliga; laa. Syn: gampiliga.  Gampililaa tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, bɛ biɛla tooi diri di wala. (The ficus tree is in the bush, children often eat its fruits.) Bɛ ʒiya gampililaa gbinni n vuhira. (They are sitting under the ficus tree resting.)

See: gampiliga; -nyaŋ.  Gampilinyaŋ tia maa nyɛla din mali anfaani, bɛ zaŋdi di dɔri n mɛri yiya. (The ficus tree is useful, they use its wood to build houses.) Gampilinyaŋ wɔla pam yuuni ŋɔ. (The ficus tree has fruited well this year.)

Colloc: tu gamu, nya gamu.  O tu la gamu o chandi ni, o naba gɔhi kpe. (He met with a mishap on his journey, a thorn pricked his foot.) Ti yi di daa wum saɣisigu, ti naan ku nya gamu shɛli. (If we had listened to advice, we would have avoided this mishap.)
See: gamu.  Gamukatari zuɣu, ti mini o laɣim la daa ni. (By coincidence, we met him in the market.) Di nyɛla gamukatari n nya o luɣu shɛli. (It was a coincidence I saw him somewhere.)
O nyɛla gandammoli tiŋa ŋɔ ni, sokam mi o. (He is a hero in this town, everyone knows him.) Ti nyɛla gandamoya, ti di nasara tobu ni. (We are champions, we won in battle.)

See: gandamoli.  O nyɛla gandɔɣu tiŋa ŋɔ ni, niriba zaa mali o jilima. (He is a distinguished person in this town, everyone respects him.) Gandari maa zaa laɣim ya n ti tɔɣisi tiŋa yɛla. (All the distinguished people gathered to discuss town matters.)

english: response to challenge ("I take you up")
example: Correl: findi.  A yɛli findi, ka n yɛli gandi. (You said "challenge" and I said "I accept".) Gandi! n ni tooi niŋ li n gari a. (I accept! I can do it better than you.)

O noo maa nyɛla gandimgandim, di mali nimgbina pam. (His fowl is spotted, it has many colours.) Bɔm dɔɣiri gandamgandam soya ka di bi lahi pam. (Find a method of spaced-out births so they are not too many.) Tampina zuɣu gandimgandim wuhirimi ni a mali yaa. (Walking proudly on ridges shows you are strong.)

Bɛ diri la gando nayili ni yuŋ kam saha shɛli. (They eat supper at the palace every night at a certain time.) Gando maa saha paai, sokam ʒiya n guli bindirigu. (Supper time has come, everyone is sitting waiting for food.)
See: gaŋ.  O nyɛla ninvuɣ' gani, o tooi niŋ binshɛɣu pam. (He is a powerful person, he can do many things.) O bi' gani n-nyɛ o, o ka tuzɔ. (He is his only child, he has no siblings.)

english: to put on (clothing around waist & below)
example: Syn: so. Gen: ye. Cpart: ganigi.  O zaŋ tanchili n-gani n chaŋ daa ni. (She put on a loincloth and went to market.) A taanchili viɛla, a gani li viɛnyɛla. (Your loincloth is nice, you put it on well.)
Cpart: gani, so.  O kpe duu ni n ganigi o kpalaŋa. (He entered the room to take off his trousers.) O ganigi o kpalaŋa n ti o tuzɔ. (He took off his trousers and gave them to his younger brother.)

Colloc: kpe ganyina, kpɛhi X ganyina.  O kpe la ganyina ni dama o bi wum saɣisigu. (He got into trouble because he didn't listen to advice.) Di kpɛhi a maŋa ganyina ni, biɛla ni a nya barina. (Don't get yourself into trouble, you might get hurt.)

english: to discriminate, select, choose out; to pick on sb.
example: Syn: gahim, pihi.  Pumpɔŋɔ ti baŋya ni ti bi simdi ni ti gaŋdi niriba. (Now we know we shouldn't discriminate against people.) Yi ni tooi gaŋ niriba ka yihi ban tum tuumbiɛri? (Can you pick out those who did wrong?) Bɛ daa gaŋ o ka yɛli ni ŋuna n tum taali. (They picked on him and said he did wrong.)

See: gaŋ. Colloc: yino gaŋa.  O bi' gaŋa n-nyɛ o, o ka tuzɔ. (He is his only child, he has no siblings.) Zaŋm' a bigaŋ' Yisahaku so a ni yuri pam la chaŋ daa. (Take your only son Isaac, whom you love much, to the market.) Ka ti nya o tuma shɛli din nyɛ o gaŋa tuma. (We saw his work which is his alone.)

Gaŋguligu tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, bɛ zaŋdi di dɔri n mɛri yiya. (The Cussonia tree is in the bush, they use its wood to build houses.) Gaŋguligu tia maa nyɛla din waɣi pam. (The Cussonia tree is very tall.)

Sim: dani, faai. Cpart: bari.  O gaŋsi ya dama o bi diri viɛnyɛla. (He has become thin because he doesn't eat well.) Naɣ' kɔri bɛn kul gaŋsigaŋsi maa bi mali yaa. (The thin and skinny cows are not strong.) Saha shɛli kum ni, niriba gaŋsira pam. (During famine, people become very thin.)

Sim: suɣili.  O sa gara din du pam o puu ni n guli bindirigu. (He made a tall shelter in his farm to guard the food.) Ka sa gara zali puu maa sunsuuni n ti vuhi. (He put up a shelter in the middle of the farm to rest.) O daa sa la gara n-kpe n-ʒi di mahim. (He put up a shelter and sat in its shade.)

Binyɛra maa be la garagara, ti tu ni ti tali ba. (The things are in a jumble, we need to arrange them.) O yɛltɔɣa nyɛla garagara, so bi baŋdi o ni yɛri shɛli. (His speech is confused, no one understands what he's saying.)

From: (Eng.). Colloc: zani garɛntii.  O zani la garɛntii n-ti o zo ka o tooi deei samli. (He stood guarantee for his friend so he could get a loan.) Garɛntii maa nyɛla gbaŋ din mali yaa. (The guarantee is a strong paper.)

english: to astonish, surprise; to confuse, disturb
example: Sim: alahaziba.  O tuma maa gari ti zaa, ti bi tɛhi ni o tooi niŋ li. (His work astonished us all, we didn't think he could do it.) O ni daa yɛli shɛm maa daa gari niriba maa. (What he said surprised the people.) Lala yɛltɔɣa maa gari o suhu. (That matter disturbed his mind.)

Syn: gabi.  O zaŋ bindira maa gari gari ka di bi lahi viɛla. (He mixed up the food and it's no longer good.) A ma yi biɛra, a bi gari o tim ni bindirigu. (If your mother is sick, don't mix her medicine with food.) Bɛ bɔrila yaɣiri ka gari li ni duɣu ŋmaŋ. (They get clay and mix it with broken pot pieces.)

english: to pass by; to move on; to overtake; to surpass
example: Colloc: Yaɣigari Chuɣu 'Passover'.  O garitila n sani Asibiri kam, ka m bi nyar' o. (He passes by me every Saturday, but I don't see him.) M gar' o zuɣu ka ti paai yiŋa tuuli. (I overtook him and we reached home first.) O nyɛla ŋun gari o zo viɛnyɛla. (He is better than his friend.) Colloc: gaarima! 'oh, do come along!'
See: garo; -bila.  Bɛ daa zaŋ o sɔŋla garibil' zuɣu n ti sɔɣi. (They laid him on a bier to bury him.) Garibila maa zuɣu, bɛ ʒi o n chaŋ kagili ni. (On the bier, they carried him to the cemetery.)

From: (Eng.).  Bɛ zaŋ biŋkɔbiri maa n niŋ gariche ni yuŋ. (They put the animals in the kraal at night.) O sa gariche gili o puu ni ka biŋkɔbiri bi kpe. (He made a fence around his farm so animals don't enter.) Gariche maa ni, o ko la bindira pam. (In the garden, he planted a lot of food.)

english: to answer, reply, respond; to inform; to intervene
example: O bi garigi shɛli, o ʒiya mi ka yɛli yɛltɔɣa. (He gave no answer, he just sat quietly.) Subee daa bi garigi Shitobu shɛli, ka o labi yiŋa. (Subee gave Shitobu no reply, so he went home.) Bɛ ni ti garigi a yaba ni o kana. (They will inform your grandfather to come.) Mabiligu zabira, a di garigi. (If family members fight, don't intervene.) Colloc: garigi kuli 'announce a death'
See: gari. Colloc: suhugarigu, daɣugarigu.  Suhugarigu gbaagi o dama o bia kpiya. (Sadness caught him because his child died.) Andunia garigu pam be ti ni. (There is much worldly distress among us.)

Syn: bingarili. See: ga; garivoli.  O daliya maa garili lu ya, o tu ni o tɔɣi li. (His shirt's button fell off, he needs to sew it back.) O zaŋ garili n ga o daliya. (He used a button to button his shirt.)

See: kuligarili.  Garili maa bili la gooni maa zuɣu ka di viɛla. (The creeper grew on the wall and it's beautiful.) Garili maa vari nyɛla tim, bɛ zaŋdi li n tibiri dɔriti. (The creeper's leaves are medicine, they use it to treat illnesses.)
Sim: gom.  Garili gbaagi o nahu maa, di ku tooi lahi tum. (Sleeping-sickness caught his cow, it can no longer work.) Garili nyɛla dɔro din kuri nihi pam. (Sleeping-sickness kills many cattle.)

O mali garili o puli ni, o tu ni o nyu tim. (He has a tapeworm in his stomach, he needs to take medicine.) Garili maa nyɛla din ni tooi kpi nira di yi bi tibi li. (Tapeworm can kill a person if not treated.)
Loori mini chɛche garim taba soli zuɣu. (A lorry and a bicycle collided on the road.) Bindirigu maa garim o kukoli ni ka o tiɣi. (The food stuck in his throat and he choked.)

Gen: wahu.  O chaŋ mɔɣu ni ka gariŋga yi dari maa ni na. (He went to the bush and a viper came out of the sticks.) O nyɛla gariŋga, o yɛltɔɣa diri niriba. (He is a viper, his words bite people.) Din be gariŋga zuɣu ni dina m-be lɛkakuli kpali ni. (What's on the viper's head is also on the axe's butt.)
Limon maa nyɛla din garisa, di bi viɛla ŋubu. (The lemon is tart, it's not nice to eat.) Bindirigu ŋɔ garisa pam, m bi tooi di li. (This food is too astringent, I can't eat it.)

Colloc: saan' garito.  O nyɛla saan' garito, o bi ʒini yɔɣi. (He is a passing stranger, he doesn't stay long.) N leela saan' garito tiŋa ŋɔ ni, niriba bi mi ma. (I have become a passing stranger in this town, people don't know me.)
