See: garili; voli.  O zaŋ garili n niŋ garivoli ni ka di ga. (He put the button in the buttonhole and buttoned it.) Garivoli maa chɛhiya, o tu ni o taɣi li. (The buttonhole tore, he needs to fix it.)

See: ʒiɛɣu.  Gariʒiɛɣu maa be la mɔɣu ni, bɛ zaŋdi di vari n tibiri dɔriti. (The Combretum shrub is in the bush, they use its leaves for medicine.) Gariʒiɛri pam be tiŋa ŋɔ ni. (Many Combretum shrubs are in this area.)

From: (Ha.).  O do la o garo zuɣu n gbihira asiba zaŋ piligu. (He is sleeping on his bed early in the morning.) Dɔnim' a garo zuɣu n vuhi, a yi ti gbarigi. (Lie on your bed and rest, if you are tired.) O da o garo palli daa ni Asibiri dali. (He bought a new bed in the market on Saturday.)
See: garo; yaɣili.  O ʒiya o garoyaɣili n guli o ba. (He is sitting at his father's bedside watching him.) Garoyaɣili maa, o zaŋ la firila ka di neeri. (At the bedside, he put a lamp to give light.)

Colloc: gawuɣila dam.  O zaŋ gawuɣila n maai dam ka di nyɛɣisa. (She used dried gaa-fruits to make drink and it's sweet.) Gawuɣilli maa kuui, ti tooi zaŋ li maai dam saha kam. (The dried gaa-fruit is dry, we can use it to make drink anytime.)

See: gali.  Gaya maa nyɛɣisa pam, biɛla maa tɔhi ba n diri. (The monkey-guava fruits are very sweet, the children picked them and are eating.) O zaŋ gaya n ti o ma. (He gave monkey-guavas to his mother.)

See: gali.  Gaya maa zaa be dundɔŋ ni, ti pa ba n ti yuri maa. (All the saddles are in the courtyard, we'll put them on the horses.) O pa la gaya n-ti yuri maa n chaŋ daa. (He put saddles on the horses and went to market.)

See: gali.  Gaya maa zaa be koligu ni, ti zaŋ ti kpalua. (All the threads are in the basket, we'll take them to the weaver.) O zaŋ gaya n wuɣi chinchini viɛnyɛla. (She used threads to weave cloth beautifully.)

A yi chaŋ daa, n gba ni chaŋ. (If you go to market, I will also go.) O nyɛla pukpara, o bia gba nyɛla pukpara. (He is a farmer, his child is also a farmer.) N di la saɣim, n nyu la kom gba. (I ate t.z., I also drank water.)
Gbaagba! a tuma maa naai ya. (Well done! your work is finished.) Gbaagba! a wa waa maa viɛnyɛla. (Well done! you danced well.)

See: gbaai; gbini.  O lo la gbaagbini o kpalaŋa ni ka di bi lu. (He tied the cord on his trousers so they wouldn't fall.) Dagɔrili kpalaŋa di ka bingarili, di mi ka ziifu, di naɣi malila gbaagbini. (The womaniser's trousers have no zip or button, only a drawstring.)

english: to take hold of, hold, grip; to catch, capture; to begin; to decide; to hit target
example: Serial: bɔbili gbaai, gbaai yihi. Colloc: gbaai kirikiri, gbaai maŋa, gbaai saawara, gbaai dabiɛm, dɔro gbaai X, gbaai azichi, gbaai kpamli. Sim: bɔbi, chaɣi, gɔhi, gbibi.  O gbaai la o nuu ka o yi. (He grabbed his hand and he left.) Tɛha na bi gbaa' ma n yɛn niŋ shɛm. (I've not yet decided what to do.) Dɔr' shɛli gbaag' o, o chaŋ asibiti. (Some disease caught him, he went to hospital.) O gbaaya tobu maa ni. (He hit the target in the shooting.) N gbaala mmaŋ' ni so' biɛɣu kam dolibu. (I avoid following every bad way.) Colloc: gbaai X napɔŋ n-ti Y 'to implore'
See: gbani.  O gbaani o yidan' tooni n suhi gaafara. (He knelt before his master to ask forgiveness.) Gbaanimi tiŋa n-ti Naawuni. (Kneel down to God.)
See: gbibi.  O gbabiri la o ba kurigu viɛnyɛla. (He looks after his old father well.) Gbabimi bihi maa ka bɛ bi ti barina. (Look after the children so they don't get hurt.)

english: to seize with pincers/tongs; to hold in beak
example: O gbabi la buɣim sala ni gbabo. (He seized the hot coal with tongs.) Noo maa gbabi la bindirigu o noli ni. (The hen held food in its beak.) O gbabi la nimdi maa o nyina ni. (He gripped the meat between his teeth.)

english: to snatch, wrench, wrest from sb.
example: See: gbabi.  Bia maa gbabigi la bindirigu maa n yi o tuzɔ nuu ni. (The child snatched the food from his younger sibling's hand.) O gbabigi la liɣiri maa n zo n chaŋ. (He snatched the money and ran away.)
InvNinst: gbabi.  O zaŋ gbabo n gbabi buɣim sala maa. (He used tongs to hold the hot coal.) Gbabonima maa be daaŋa ni. (The tongs are in the kitchen.)
Gbafuɣa maa be la mɔɣu ni. (The animal is in the bush.) O gbaai la gbafuɣa n ti o ma. (He caught an animal and gave it to his mother.)

english: long basket for kola nuts; animal-cradle; load; capital
example: Colloc: lɔ gbaɣa, ku gbaɣa, yɛligi gbaɣa.  O zaŋ gbaɣa n ʒi guli daa ni. (He used a long basket to carry kola nuts to market.) O lɔ o la gbaɣa n ti o tuma. (He gave him capital for business.) O ku o gbaɣa dama o da laɣiri pam. (He depleted his capital because he spent too much money.)
O gbaɣi la tia maa gbiri. (He sliced off the tree's root.) Tipɔɣu ku kɔŋ gbaɣibu. (Tree bark won't refuse to be sliced off.)

english: to shake out; to dispose of under financial pressure; to shake head; to have mild illness
example: Serial: zaŋ gbaɣiri.  O gbaɣiri o binyɛrigu ka yihi taŋkpaɣu. (He shook out his garment to remove dust.) Bɛ yi kul gbaɣiri gɔhi maa, di wala lurila. (If they just shake the thorn tree, its fruits fall.) Bia maa ningbuŋ gbaɣiriya, ti yɛn zaŋ o chaŋ asibiti. (The child has a slight fever, we'll take him to hospital.)

english: to nod, shake head; to change tone, weaken, slacken
example: Sim: waai, waɣisi.  O gbaɣisi o zuɣu n wuhi ni o bi saɣi ti. (He shook his head to show he doesn't agree.) Naawuni gbaɣisi n tɔbu ka filim ma. (God has unstrung my bow and weakened me.) Bɛ gbaɣisi tɔbu maa ka di bi lahi ŋmɛra. (They unstrung the bow so it no longer shoots.)
O nyɛla ninvuɣ' gbaɣu, o bi lahi tooi tum tuma. (He is a weak old person, he can no longer work.) Dokuri' gbaɣu sola ŋun kani. (Having a weak old elder is better than having none.)
See: gbaɣa.  O zaŋ gbaɣu n ʒi nyuya. (He used a basket to carry yams.) Gbaɣu maa pali bindira. (The basket is full of food.)

Bɛ gbahi la zahim pam mɔɣili ni. (They caught many fish in the dam.) Saha shɛli tɔbu ni, bɛ gbahi la tɔbbihi pam. (During the war, they captured many soldiers.) Dabiɛm gbahi o dama yuŋ biɛla. (Fear seized him because it was dark.) N gbahi a napɔŋ, m bɔri sɔŋsim. (I beg you, I need help.)
O gbahiŋ maa wumiya, ti zaa gbaai alizama. (His long talk is heard, we all started a conversation.) Gbahiŋ bi viɛla, niriba tooi je li. (Too much talking is not good, people often dislike it.)
O tum tuma maa gbajaɣa, dinzuɣu di bi viɛla. (He did the work carelessly, so it's not good.) Gbajaɣa chandi ni tooi tahi barina na. (Careless walking can bring danger.)
O zaŋ gbaka n ʒi kom yiŋa na. (She used a large calabash to carry water home.) Gbaka maa yi lu tiŋa, di nyɛɣira. (If the large calabash falls to the ground, it breaks.)
See: gballi.  Gbala maa be kagili maa ni. (The graves are in the cemetery.) Bɛ sɔɣi la bɛ banima gbala ni. (They buried their ancestors in the graves.)
See: gballi.  Bɛ yɛrigi la gbala n ti saamba maa. (They spread mats for the guests.) Gbala maa zaa be dundɔŋ ni. (All the mats are in the courtyard.)

english: thigh; leg/back leg of quadruped; wheel; roots
example: Cpart: bɔɣu. Gen: nabili, napɔŋ. Spec: gbalipini. Colloc: gbali gɔŋ.  O gbali biɛri o dama o lu ya. (His thigh hurts because he fell.) A yi ka bua shibu shee, bua gbali yi ti barigi, bɛ ku bɔh' a. (If you're not at goat-skinning, they won't ask about lost thigh.) Chɛchɛbuŋ' gbaya maa gindi viɛnyɛla. (The chariot wheels are turning well.)

See: gbali; kpiŋ; -lana. Sim: bɔɣukpimalana.  O nyɛla gbalikpimalana, o ku tooi chaŋ katiŋa. (He has paralysed legs, he can't walk far.) Dɔɣite maa tibi la gbalikpimalana maa ka o gbali ti viɛla. (The doctor treated the man with paralysed legs and his leg improved.)

english: a lot, plenty; too much, excessively
example: Colloc: yɛtɔɣ'gbaliŋ. Cpart: biɛla.  O mali la liɣiri gbaliŋ, o nyɛla bundana. (He has plenty of money, he is rich.) Ti daa nyɛla ninvuɣ' gbaliŋ salima ni. (We were many people at the festival.) Nir' kul ni tɔɣisiri yɛtɔɣ' gbaliŋ ŋɔ, niriba ku tooi gbibi li. (If someone talks excessively, people can't bear it.)
See: gbali. Syn: gbalipuɣili. Sim: nyɔɣu.  O zaŋ gbalipaɣa n ti saamba maa. (He gave the haunch to the guests.) Nimdi viɛlla ni, gbalipaɣa n-nyɛ di puuni. (Among good meat, the haunch is the best.)

See: gbali. Gen: gbali. Sim: sakpuɣu, gbalikpuɣili. Colloc: gbalipini kɔbili.  O gbalipini biɛri o dama o zo n gbaai. (The flesh of his thigh hurts because he ran and caught it.) Tummi tuma n-gari jinjɛm' muɣi gbalipini. (Working is better than having big thighs that can't enter shorts.) Zaŋm' a nuu niŋ n gbalipina lɔŋ ni. (Put your hand under my thighs.)
Syn: gbalipaɣa. See: bingbalipuɣili.  O zaŋ gbalipuɣili n ti o ma. (He gave the thigh to his mother.) Nimdi viɛla nyɛla gbalipuɣa mini gaba. (Good meat is thighs and shoulders.)

Syn: kagili. Sim: daboli, siliga. Sit: sɔɣi, kuli.  Bɛ sɔɣi o la gballi ni kagili maa ni. (They buried him in a grave in the cemetery.) O daa kul bela gbala maa shee n vuhira. (He was just sitting near the graves resting.) Pololi yi ʒi shɛlikam, o mi la lɔŋ gballi. (If the toad knows nothing, it knows the frog's grave.)

InvMat: sampaa. Sim: sɔŋ. Mat: gbiŋgbaŋ.  Bɛ yɛrigi la gballi n ti saamba maa n gbihi. (They spread a mat for the guests to sleep.) Gballi maa chɛhiya, ti tu ni ti taɣi li. (The mat tore, we need to replace it.)

O zaŋ omaŋ' wuhi niriba gbaluŋ. (He showed himself to many people.) N ti chɛ ka zuliya gbaluŋ yi a zuliya maa ni na. (I will make many nations come from your nation.) Zom' yɛtɔɣ' gbalin' chɔɣima tɔɣisibu. (Avoid talking long-winded rubbish.)

See: bigbamdigu.  Bia maa gbamdi la tiŋa, o na bi tooi chaŋ. (The child crawls on the ground, he can't walk yet.) Binnɛma bɛn gbamdi tiŋa maa be mɔɣu ni. (The creeping creatures are in the bush.) Bɔtaa, n chaŋ chaŋ ti lan gbamda? (I've walked and walked, should I also crawl?)

english: weir fixed across stream to catch fish
example: Sim: tɔɣu.  Bɛ zaŋ gbambiɛɣu n gbahi zahim mɔɣili ni. (They used a weir to catch fish in the dam.) Gbambiɛɣu maa be mɔɣili maa ni. (The weir is in the stream.)
See: gbɔŋ; -bila.  O da la gbambila n kpe deeba maa ni. (He bought a ticket to enter the durbar.) Gbambila maa liɣiri nyɛla din galisi. (The ticket price is high.)

Syn: Silimiŋa, Nasara. Cpart: gbansabinli.  Gbampiɛlli maa yi la England na n ti ʒini Tamale. (The European came from England to live in Tamale.) Gbampiɛla pam be Ghana tiŋgbɔŋ ni. (Many Europeans are in Ghana.)

english: small hide pieces protecting drum skin
example: See: gbɔŋ; guŋgɔŋkuɣra.  O zaŋ gbanchiriga n gu guŋgɔŋ maa ka di bi chɛhi. (He used hide pieces to protect the drum so it wouldn't tear.) Gbanchiriga maa chihiya, o tu ni o taɣi li. (The hide piece broke, he needs to replace it.)
Bɛ ko la gbandaa maa ka bindirigu zooi pam. (They farmed the fertile virgin land and food grew a lot.) Gbandaa maa puuni, ti mali ti puu. (In that zone, we have our farm.)

english: thick strings (animal hide) for drum heads
example: See: gbɔŋ; -laa; guŋgɔŋ.  Bɛ zaŋ gbandaa n lo guŋgɔŋ maa gbana. (They used thick hide strings for the drum heads.) Gbandaa maa gbibi gbaŋ maa. (The strings hold the skin.)

See: guŋgɔŋ; darigi.  O zaŋ gbandarigra n darigi guŋgɔŋ maa ka di kum viɛla. (He used leather strips to tighten the drum so it sounds good.) Gbandarigra maa chihiya, o tu ni o taɣi li. (The leather strips broke, he needs to replace them.)
Sim: sɔɣiso.  O zaŋ gbandɔrigu n paɣi o maŋa. (He used a loofah to wash himself.) Gbandɔrigu maa bala pam. (The loofah is very soft.)
See: gbɔŋ.  Gbandi maa zaa be dundɔŋ ni. (All the skins are in the courtyard.) Bɛ zaŋ gbandi n mɛ suɣili. (They used skins to make a shelter.)

english: eczema; disease of yams/animals; insoluble problem
example: Gbani gbaagi o, o chaŋ asibiti n ti nya tim. (Eczema caught him, he went to hospital to get medicine.) Gbani gbaagi o nahu maa, di bi diri viɛnyɛla. (Disease caught his cow, it's not eating well.) Di niŋ gbani, ti bi mi ni ti yɛn niŋ bɔ. (It's an insoluble problem, we don't know what to do.) Colloc: niŋ gbani.
O ŋubi la gbani maa, di tɔ amaa di mali alaafee. (He ate the gristle, it's tough but healthy.) Gbani maa nyɛla din mali yaa. (The sinew is strong.)
Di nyɛla gbani n-ti ti, ti bi tooi nya di naabu. (It's an insoluble problem for us, we can't find its end.) Gbani maa zuɣu, ti bi tooi chaŋ tooni. (Because of the problem, we can't go forward.)

Sim: shɛlɔrigu, balati, nyolɔrigu.  O lo la gbani o shee ni n gbibi o kpalaŋa. (He wore a leather belt on his waist to hold his trousers.) Gbani maa chihiya, o tu ni o taɣi li. (The belt broke, he needs to replace it.)

english: to bend down, be bent double; to kneel
example: Etym: OV *GBAN.  O gbani tiŋa n ti o kpɛma. (He knelt down to his elder.) Yɛntɔri daa ti gbani la o ba tooni. (Yentori knelt before her father.) O daa du di zuɣu ti gbani o duna zuɣu. (He climbed up and knelt down.)
Sim: kɔri, pari.  O gbani la o maŋa dama o nyɔri. (He scratched himself because he was itching.) Noo maa gbani la tiŋa n ti nya bindirigu. (The hen scratched the ground to find food.)

Sim: gbanyani, gbuluɣu, lɔŋ, saka, soo.  Gbanjaŋa maa be kom ni. (The striped frog is in the water.) Gbanjaŋa ŋmani gbanyani amaa di mali nimgbina. (The striped frog looks like a frog but it has colours.)
Colloc: ŋme gbanjɔŋ.  Bɛ ŋme la gbanjɔŋ daa ni Asibiri kam. (They hold an auction in the market every Saturday.) Gbanjɔŋ maa ni, bɛ kɔhi la nɛma pam. (At the auction, they sell many things.)
O zaŋ gbanjein n ʒi tandi n ti mɛ duu. (He used a tipper truck to carry sand for building.) Gbanjein maa ʒiya soli yaɣili. (The tipper truck is by the roadside.)

english: topknot, tuft of hair left on front of head
example: O chɛ la gbanjua o zuɣu ka di viɛla. (He left a topknot on his head and it's beautiful.) Gbanjua maa nyɛla nachiinsi. (The topknot is decoration.)

See: gbaŋkɔɣu.  Gbankɔɣu gbaagi o bia, o mali fiiba. (Measles caught his child, he has fever.) Di yi lee ka bɛ chibi bia gbankɔɣu chihiro-chihiro, bia maa ni tooi gu o maŋa. (If a child is vaccinated against measles, he can protect himself.)

See: guŋgɔŋkuɣra.  Gbankuɣra maa gbibi gbaŋ maa ka guŋgɔŋ maa ŋmɛra. (The drum seat holds the skin so the drum can be played.) O zaŋ gbankuɣra n mali guŋgɔŋ palli. (He used a drum seat to make a new drum.)

english: last weeding of farm; doing sth. in a rush
example: Colloc: wobi gbankurugu. See: gbɔŋ; kurugu.  Bɛ wobi la gbankurugu puu maa ni ka naai. (They did the last weeding of the farm and finished.) O wobi la gbankurugu n niŋ tuma maa, dinzuɣu di bi viɛla. (He did the work in a rush, so it's not good.)
See: gbaŋŋmara.  Gbanŋmara maa sabi la gbaŋ n ti naa maa. (The secretary wrote a letter for the chief.) Gbanŋmara maa nyɛla ŋun mi sabbu viɛnyɛla. (The secretary knows writing well.)

Colloc: gbansabila tinsi. Cpart: gbampiɛlli, Silimiŋa, Nasara.  Ti zaa nyɛla gbansabila, ti ʒi la Ghana. (We are all black people, we live in Ghana.) Gbansabila tinsi pam be Afrika. (Many African countries are in Africa.)

Syn: gbaŋŋmara, takada. See: gbɔŋ; sabi.  O nyɛla gbansabira, o sabiri la gbana Asibiri kam. (He is a scribe, he writes letters every Saturday.) Gbansabira maa mi sabbu viɛnyɛla. (The scribe knows writing well.)

english: yellow and black striped toad; bed-wetter (fig.)
example: Sim: pololi.  Gbansaŋa maa be kom ni. (The striped toad is in the water.) O nyɛla gbansaŋa, o duni la gbɛri ni. (He is a bed-wetter, he urinates in bed.) Nimmira bi diri nam. Nimmira yi diri nam gbansaŋa naanyi di Kɔnkɔn. (If big eyes made chiefs, the toad would be chief of Konkon.)

english: farm in fourth year after yams; abandoned farm
example: O ko la gbansɔɣu maa ka nyuya maa wɔla. (He farmed the fourth-year field and the yams produced well.) Gbansɔɣu maa kpalim ya, bɛ bi lahi kɔri li. (The farm has been abandoned, they no longer farm it.)
See: gbɔŋ.  O zaŋ gbantalima n lo o binshɛɣu maa. (He used a strap to tie his thing.) Gbantalima maa chihiya, o tu ni o taɣi li. (The strap broke, he needs to replace it.)
See: gbɔŋ; ti.  Gbantira maa laɣim la liɣiri daa ni Asibiri kam. (The market-collector gathers money at the market every Saturday.) Gbantiriba maa tumdi viɛnyɛla. (The market-collectors work well.)
See: gbantalima.  Gbantolima maa chihiya, o tu ni o taɣi ba. (The straps broke, he needs to replace them.) O zaŋ gbantolima n lo o. (He used straps to tie him.)
Sim: buluɣu, gbanjaŋa, gbuluɣu, lɔŋ, saka, soo.  Gbanyani maa be kom ni. (The frog is in the water.) Gbanyani maa kuli la yuŋ. (The frog is croaking at night.)

See: gbɔŋ; zabi.  O nyɛla gbanzaba, o shɛri la gurima mini saba. (He is a leather worker, he sews talismans and amulets.) Gbanzaba maa tumdi viɛnyɛla, o tooi shɛri namdi. (The leather worker works well, he can sew shoes.)
See: gbanzaba; gbɔŋ.  Gbanzabigandi maa be dundɔŋ ni. (The hides are in the courtyard.) O zaŋ gbanzabigandi n mali gurima. (He used hides to make talismans.)

See: gbanzaba; goo.  Gbanzabigoo tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, bɛ zaŋdi di dɔri n tibiri gbana. (The tanning acacia is in the bush, they use its wood to treat hides.) Gbanzabigoo mali gɔhi pam. (The tanning acacia has many thorns.)
See: gbanzaba.  Gbanzabigu nyɛla tuma kurili tiŋa ŋɔ ni. (Leather working is an old craft in this town.) O bɔhim la gbanzabigu o ba sani. (He learned leather working from his father.)
Bɛ zaŋ gbanzaliŋga dɔri n mɛ duu maa. (They used Burkea wood to build the room.) Gbanzaliŋga tia maa waɣila pam. (The Burkea tree is very tall.)

english: title of oldest wife of Ya Naa; her house
example: Gbanzaluŋ maa nyɛla paɣa so ŋun mali jilima nayili ni. (The Gbanzaluŋ is a woman with honour at the palace.) O be la gbanzaluŋ maa ni. (She is in the Gbanzaluŋ's house.)
Gbanzigɔɣu maa zooi pam mɔɣu ni. (The tall grass grows a lot in the bush.) Gbanzigɔɣu be soli yaɣili. (The tall grass is by the roadside.)

english: short period of heat after rainy season
example: See: ʒiɛɣu.  Gbanʒiɛɣu maa paai, tula duya pam. (The post-rain hot period arrived, the heat increased greatly.) Gbanʒiɛɣu nyɛla din yuura. (The post-rain hot period lasts long.)
O zaŋ gbaŋ n ʒi kom. (He used a rubber tube to carry water.) Gbaŋ maa nyɛla din teera. (The rubber tube is stretchy.)
See: gbɔŋ.  Gbaŋ maa be nahu maa zuɣu. (The skin is on the cow.) O zaŋ gbaŋ n mali gurima. (He used skin to make talismans.)

english: fowl's comb; mohican, crest of hair
example: Sim: gbanjua.  Nolɔɣu gbaŋa maa ʒeɛ. (The cock's comb is red.) O chɛ la gbaŋa o zuɣu ka di viɛla. (He left a crest of hair on his head and it's beautiful.)
Gbaŋgu maa ni, biŋkɔbiri pam be ni. (In the thick bush, there are many animals.) O kpe gbaŋgu ni n ti gbaai waɣi. (He entered the thick bush to catch a snake.)
See: gbankɔɣu.  Sapaɣu dimi gbaŋkɔɣu bara, sapaɣu je komahim. (A cobra bit a measles patient; the cobra dislikes cold water.) Gbaŋkɔɣu - Yim - Chira awei nyaaŋa. (Measles - Once - After  months.)
O zaŋ gbaŋkurigu n tuui buɣim. (He used dried guinea corn leaves to start a fire.) Gbaŋkurigu maa kuui, di ni tooi taai buɣim. (The dried leaves are dry, they can catch fire easily.)
Colloc: wɔbi gbankurugu.  Bɛ wɔbi la gbaŋkurigu puu maa ni. (They did the last weeding of the farm.) Gbaŋkurigu maa nyaaŋa, bɛ guui li. (After the last weeding, they left it.)
See: gbɔŋlana.  Gbaŋlana maa di o ba yili. (The regent succeeded his father.) Gbaŋlana maa ʒiya nam gbandi zuɣu. (The regent sits on the skin of office.)

Syn: gbaŋsabira, takada. InvNact: ŋma. Colloc: ŋma gbɔŋ.  Tiŋ' maa gbaŋŋmara daa gooi salo maa. (The town clerk settled the people.) O boli gbaŋŋmariba na n ti sabi gbaŋ. (He called the scribes to write a letter.)
Gbaŋpali maa nyɛli o, o ku tooi chaŋ viɛnyɛla. (The sand-crack hurts him, he can't walk well.) O mali gbaŋpali o napɔŋ ni. (He has a sand-crack on his foot.)

Syn: gbaŋŋmara. See: gbɔŋ; sabi.  Liɣiri din be adaka maa ni zooi, naa gbaŋsabira kanina nti kal' li. (When the money in the box was much, the king's secretary came to count it.) Gbaŋsabira maa sabi la gbaŋ. (The secretary wrote a letter.)

english: trap of leather and wood (for big animals)
example: O zaŋ gbara n gbaai wɔbigu. (He used a trap to catch an elephant.) Gbara maa nyɛla gbanzaba ni mali shɛli. (The trap is made by leatherworkers.)
Sim: je, gbarigi, wum, ŋɔhigi, buɣi.  O gbari ya tuma maa ni, o bɔri vuhigu. (He is tired from the work, he needs rest.) N gbari pam, n ku tooi lahi tum. (I'm very tired, I can no longer work.)

english: to hobble front legs; to limp
example: Sim: ba.  O gbari o wahu maa naba ka di bi zo. (He hobbled his horse's legs so it wouldn't run.) O gbariri la dama o naba biɛri o. (He limps because his foot hurts.)

english: to sputter, spatter; to bubble and plop
example: Saɣim maa gbariri la di yi ti duɣira. (The t.z. sputters when cooking.) Kom maa gbariri la di yi ti mii. (Water sputters when boiling.)
See: gbarigu; jaaŋgbee.  O nyɛla gbaridaangbee, o bi tooi chaŋ. (He has polio, he can't walk.) Gbaridaangbee gbaagi o bia, o chaŋ asibiti. (Polio caught his child, he went to hospital.)
Bindirigu maa leei la gbarigbari, di bi viɛla ŋubu. (The food became thick, it's not good to eat.) Nimdi maa nyɛla gbarigbari, ti ku tooi di li. (The meat is stinking, we can't eat it.)
O zaŋ gbarigbuni n zahim chi maa. (He used a bowl to measure the millet.) Gbarigbuni maa pali bindira. (The bowl is full of food.)
Bɛ jɛmdi la Gbarigi mɔɣili maa ni. (They worship Gbarigi at the river.) Gbarigi nyɛla buɣili. (Gbarigi is a shrine.)

english: to be very weary; to be weak; to make weak; to be run-down
example: Sim: chaɣisi, chɔɣim, kabisi. Cpart: kpɛma.  Di wumdi bipala ka bɛ gbarigira. (Even youths grow tired and weary.) Bɛ yi zaŋ tɔbimi' pala zaɣ' maha dib' ayopɔin lo ma, di ni chɛ ka n gbarigi. (If tied with seven fresh bowstrings, I'll become weak.) Dɔriti ŋɔ gbarigiri ti ka ti bi tooi tumdi tuma. (These diseases weaken us so we can't work.)
See: gbarigi.  Gbariginsim gbaagi o, o bi tooi kpuɣi binshɛɣu. (Weakness caught him, he can't lift anything.) A mi di chɛ ma bahi n gbariginsim saha. (Don't abandon me in my weakness.)

Colloc: gbaridɔɣirili. Sim: wɔbiga, napɔmbara.  O sɔŋ la gbarigu maa n du lɔri maa ni. (He helped the lame man to get on the lorry.) Mani n-daa nyɛ zoomba nina, ka nyɛ gbariti napɔna. (I was eyes to the blind and legs to the crippled.) A yi bɔri ni a baŋ tiŋ' daashili, nyin' bɔhimila gbarigu. (To know a town's secrets, ask a cripple.)
O gbarim la kpam o maŋa ni. (He smeared oil on himself.) O binyɛrigu gbarim ya, o tu ni o paɣi li. (His garment is soiled, he needs to wash it.)
Cpart: kpɛma, yaa, paŋ. See: gbarigi.  O nyɛla ninvuɣ' gbariŋ, o bi tooi tum tuma. (He is a weak person, he can't work.) Ti gba nyɛla ninvuɣ' gbarima saha shɛŋa. (We are also weak people sometimes.)
See: gbarigu.  Gbariti maa zaa ʒiya mɔɣuni. (All the lame people are at the lorry station.) O ti la gbariti maa bindirigu. (He gave food to the lame people.)
Gbarizam maa gbaai la noo. (The large hawk caught a fowl.) O nyɛla gbarizam, o bi tooi kpuɣi binshɛɣu. (He is a light person, he can't lift anything.)
See: jɛrigu.  O kparila gbataajɛrigu n chaŋ salima ni. (She wore batik cloth to the festival.) Gbataajɛrigu maa viɛla pam. (The batik cloth is very beautiful.)
N nintam maa chɛ ka n dufɛli kul mahigi gbati. (My tears made my pillow all wet and soggy.) Bindirigu maa leei la gbati dama kom pam be ni. (The food became soggy because there's too much water.)
Dunoli maa yooi gbau ka ti kpe. (The door opened wide and we entered.) Soli maa nyɛla gbau, ti tooi chaŋ viɛnyɛla. (The path is wide, we can walk well.)
See: gbali.  O gbaya biɛri o dama o zo pam. (His thighs hurt because he ran a lot.) Chɛchɛbuŋ' gbaya maa gindi viɛnyɛla. (The chariot wheels are turning well.)

See: gbɔɣino; ʒi.  O daa kuhi boli nachimbil' so ŋun nyɛ o gbɔɣino ʒira. (He shouted to call the young man who was his armourbearer.) Gbɔɣinɔʒira maa ʒiri la gbɔɣino. (The armourbearer carries the armour.)

Sim: galikua, lɔɣibuya, nyɔtaɣirigu, bintaɣirigu. See: gbɔɣinɔʒira.  O ye la gbɔɣino n chaŋ tobu ni. (He wore armour and went to war.) Bɛ yi di kariti binyɛr' biɛri, nira naan ku yɛ gbaɣino. (If people threw away dirty clothes, no one would have a war-dress to wear.) O ʒiɛyimtali nyɛla gbaɣinɔ mini din taɣiri nira. (His faithful promises are armour and protection.)

english: skin (of living body); hide or leather; chieftaincy; paper, card; book; letter
example: Etym: OV *GBAN. Colloc: nam gbandi, ʒini nam gbandi zuɣu, gbɔŋ bia/bihi, saɣim gbɔŋ, ŋma gbɔŋ, talim gbɔŋ, dihi gbɔŋ. Sim: buku, litaafi, kundi, tiɣiti. Syn: washiika.  O niŋgbuŋ gbaŋ maa mɔrita. (The skin of his body is swelling.) N yɛn zaŋla piɛ' gbɔŋ sɔŋ kabɔɣili ni. (I will put a sheepskin on the threshing floor.) Naawuni ŋɔ daa zaŋ biŋkɔbiri gbana n-she n-ti Adam. (God took animal skins and sewed them for Adam.) O ʒini Izraɛl nam gbandi zuɣu. (He is king of Israel.) M bi bɔri ni n zaŋ tadabo sab' li pa gbaŋ zuɣu. (I don't want to write it on paper to you.) O zaŋ gbɔŋ maa ti ma. (He gave me the book.) Bɛ daa ŋma gbaŋ fiɣisi Judanima. (They wrote a letter accusing the Judahites.)
See: mɔɣu; yɔɣu.  Gbɔŋgu maa ni, biŋkɔbiri pam be ni. (In the thick bush, there are many animals.) O kpe gbɔŋgu ni. (He entered the thick bush.)
See: gbɔŋ; kɔɣu.  O kurigi pam, o gbɔŋkɔɣu mɔrita. (He is very old, his wrinkled skin is loose.) Gbɔŋkɔɣu maa be o niŋgbuna ni. (The wrinkled skin is on his body.)

Colloc: ʒili X gbɔŋ. Cpart: pakpɔŋ. Sim: zuu. See: gbɔŋ; -lana.  Gbɔŋlana maa di o ba yili. (The regent succeeded his father.) Bɛ ʒili o la gbɔŋ ka o leei gbɔŋlana. (They appointed him as regent.) Aku! Gbɔnalana di o ba yili. (Ho, ho! the regent has succeeded his father.)
See: gbaŋŋmara.  Gbɔŋmara maa sabi la gbaŋ. (The secretary wrote a letter.) Gbɔŋmara maa nyɛla ŋun mi sabbu. (The secretary knows writing.)

english: to get control of, exhaust sth.
example: Bɛ tum tuumbiɛri ka di gbɔrigi ba. (They have sinned so much they are exhausted by it.) O gbɔrigi la o liɣiri zaa. (He exhausted all his money.)

english: to spend the night; to lie with sb.
example: Sim: gbihi, dɔni. Colloc: gbɛb' shee.  Ka o daa kpe vokabili puuni zoli maa zuɣu nti gbe nimaani. (He entered a cave on the mountain and spent the night there.) So yi tum taali, yin' di chɛ ka o gbe tia maa zuɣu. (If someone commits an offence, don't leave them hanging on the tree.) Abu mini o paɣa n gbɛra. (Abu sleeps with his wife.)
See: baɣigbee, kulagbɛɣu.  O zaŋ gbee n ko o puu. (He used a three-pronged hoe to farm.) Gbee maa nyɛla din mali anfaani. (The three-pronged hoe is useful.)

english: to take part of quantity, draw water; to cut small piece; to chip off
example: Syn: ŋooi, tooi, ŋɔŋ.  O gbeei la kom maa kobiliga ni. (He drew some water from the well.) O gbeei la nimdi biɛla n ti o bia. (He cut a small piece of meat for his child.) O gbeei la gaɣili maa. (He chipped the calabash.)

english: to violate, disregard taboo; to cease to be taboo
example: Colloc: gbeei chihili.  O gbeei la chihili maa, dinzuɣu o kpe daɣiri ni. (He violated the taboo, so he incurred impurity.) Chihili maa gbeei ya, ti tooi di li. (The taboo has ceased, we can eat it.)
See: gbɛli.  O gbeeli ma dama m bi tum o ni yɛli shɛli. (He looked askance at me because I didn't do what he said.) Gbeelindi niriba bi viɛla. (Looking askance at people is not good.)
See: doo.  O nyɛla Gbengbɛdoo, o kɔhiri la nɛma daa ni. (He is a Hausa man, he sells goods in the market.) Gbengbɛdɔbba maa zaa laɣim ya. (All the Hausa men gathered.)
See: paɣa.  O nyɛla Gbengbɛpaɣa, o kɔhiri la bindira daa ni. (She is a Hausa woman, she sells food in the market.) Gbengbɛpaɣiba maa mali baŋsim pam. (The Hausa women have much skill.)

Naa Gbewaa daa dɔɣila bidibisi ata: Gbewaa mini Namʒishɛli ni Nyaɣili. (Naa Gbewaa begat three sons: Gbewaa, Namzisheli and Nyagili.) Naa Gbewaa bi'shɛba bɛn kpalim maa bɛn daa dila nama m-be Dagbɔŋ tinsi ni. (The rest of Naa Gbewaa's sons took chieftaincies in Dagbong towns.) Colloc: Naa Gbewaa.
Gbɛba maa be mɔɣu ni. (The small shrub is in the bush.) Bɛ zaŋdi gbɛba vari n tibiri dɔriti. (They use the shrub's leaves for medicine.)
See: 'dibiga.  Gbɛdibiga maa mali yaa pam. (The male waterbuck is very strong.) O tɔhi la gbɛdibiga. (He shot a male waterbuck.)

Etym: OV *TIN/TUN. Colloc: gbɛri kpa taaba, che gbɛɣu.  O gbɛɣu zuɣu mali dalima. (He has marks on his forehead.) Gbɛɣu bi ʒia ka bɛ turi kpali. (You don't insult the back of the head before the forehead.) O mini bɛn fiɣisir' o maa gbɛri kpa taba. (He confronted his accusers face to face.)
Sim: kikaa.  O piɛbi la gbɛɣu salima ni. (He is blowing a horn at the festival.) Gbɛri maa kum wumiya katiŋa. (The horns' sound is heard far away.)
Gbɛɣu maa be mɔɣu ni. (The waterbuck is in the bush.) O gbaai la gbɛɣu n ti o ma. (He caught a waterbuck and gave it to his mother.)

english: to treat sacrificial meat with medicine; to break charcoal from log
example: O gbɛhi la nimdi maa pɔi ka bɛ di li. (He treated the meat with medicine before they ate it.) O gbɛhi la buɣim datee. (He broke charcoal from the burning stick.)
O nyɛla gbɛli, o tooi kpuɣi binshɛɣu pam. (He is strong, he can lift many things.) Naɣilaa gbɛli ku ʒɛ diɣili. (A strong bull won't gore an anthill.)

english: to look askance at, stare angrily
example: O gbɛli ma dama m bi tum o ni yɛli shɛli. (He looked askance at me because I didn't do what he said.) Jɛrigu ʒi gbɛligu naɣla zukɔɣili. (A fool doesn't know an angry look without a knock on the head.) O gbeelindi ma mi. (He looks askance at me.)
Whole: wahu.  Wahu maa gbɛm maa viɛla pam. (The horse's mane is very beautiful.) Nyini n-zaŋ gbɛm tam o nyiŋgoli zuɣu? (Did you put the mane on his neck?)

See: gbimisi.  Bɛ gbɛmdi la gɔhi maa vari n ti buhi. (They nip off the thorn tree leaves to feed the goats.) Taruŋ nyɛla baŋli ka sokam gbɛmda. (A simple person is like the creeping pea plant that everyone plucks.)
See: jɛŋgbɛyɔɣirili.  Gbɛngbɛɣu maa yi la Kano na n ti ʒini Tamale. (The Hausa person came from Kano to live in Tamale.) Gbɛngbɛri pam be tiŋa ŋɔ ni. (Many Hausa people are in this town.)
O yɛri la Gbɛngbɛhili viɛnyɛla. (He speaks Hausa well.) Gbɛngbɛhili nyɛla balli din galisi. (Hausa is a widely spoken language.)

english: Hausa customs; long tribal marks on face
example: O doli la Gbɛngbɛri o biɛhigu ni. (He follows Hausa customs in his life.) O mali Gbɛngbɛri o tapaɣa zuɣu. (He has Hausa tribal marks on his cheeks.)
See: gbɛɣu.  Bɛ gbɛri zaa mali dalima. (All their foreheads have marks.) Gbɛri maa kpa taba. (Their foreheads faced each other.)
See: gbɛɣu.  Bɛ piɛbiri gbɛri salima ni. (They are blowing horns at the festival.) Gbɛri maa kum wumiya. (The horns' sound is heard.)
See: gbɛɣu.  Gbɛri maa be mɔɣu ni. (The waterbucks are in the bush.) O gbaai la gbɛri ayi. (He caught two waterbucks.)
See: gbe.  O kpe o du' gbɛrili ni n ti gbihi. (He entered his bedroom to sleep.) Polɔya waɣi m-pali bɛ nanim' dugbɛra ni. (Toads multiplied and filled their kings' bedrooms.)

Correl: gbi zamba bɔɣa. Sim: gaai.  O gbi voli n ti sɔɣi saɣiri. (He dug a hole to bury rubbish.) A gbir' amaŋmaŋ' gballi kpe. (You dig yourself a grave.) Timbuŋ' gbi voli ka shɛlinli kpe. (An aardvark digs a hole and a porcupine enters.) Zamba bɔɣili, di gbirimi ka di yɛlima. (If digging a pit for someone's downfall, dig it wide.)
Kuruchu bɔbigu daa ʒe zol' luɣili ni gbiri binyara ŋubira. (A herd of pigs were on the hillside rooting for food.) Nahu maa gbi o la. (The cow butted him.)

english: to grasp in hand; to control; to care for; to keep up; to accept, obey; to support
example: Colloc: vili n-gbibi, gbibi so taali, zaligu gbibi, gbibi noli. Sim: bɔbi, chaɣi, gbaai, gbabi, gbahi, kpibi. Caus: gbibili.  O gbibi la sua o nuu ni. (He held the knife in his hand.) Nyin' ka n vili gbibi. (You are the one I cling to.) A yi bi gbibi so bini, a ku tooi gbibi amaŋmaŋa bini. (If you can't care for another's things, you can't care for your own.) Gbibim a ba saɣisigu. (Obey your father's advice.)

english: to put in hand for holding; to hand over
example: InvCaus: gbibi.  Ka zaŋ kaɣili gbibili o nudirigu ni. (They made him hold a stalk in his right hand.) O daa zaŋ liɣiri maa gbibli o. (He handed over the money to him.)
Sim: gbilim.  A lɛ' gbiɣu so la "Ŋmami ka ti ma." (Your dull axe is better than "Cut and give me.") Sua gbiɣu ku tooi ŋmaai nimdi. (A blunt knife cannot cut meat.)

english: to sleep, be asleep; to clot, coagulate, freeze
example: Serial: dooni n-gbihi. Nact: gɔgbihira. Sim: dɔni, gbe, gom, ʒeei. Cpart: neei. Colloc: daa gbihi 'price has fallen'.  O do la o garo zuɣu n gbihira. (He is sleeping on his bed.) N chaŋ nti gbihi. (I'm going to sleep.) N ku gbihi bee n-ʒeei gom. (I will not sleep or doze off.) Kawana daa gbihiya. (The price of maize has fallen.)
Sim: gbili, bili. See: gbili.  O gbihi la duɣu maa ka kom kpaai. (He tilted the pot and water spilled.) Di gbihi garo maa. (Don't tilt the bed.)

english: to tip over, roll over, tilt; to lie aside; to be covered in
example: Sim: biligi, bili, gbihi, gbiligi.  Di gbiliya, a tu ni a tuhi li. (It's not straight, you need to straighten it.) Ŋun ʒiri ka di gbili sola ŋun gamda. (A carrier with tilted load is better than one walking free.) Nira yi je a ka a min' o duɣiri kom, ŋun yɛli ni a niŋdi taŋkpaɣu gbiligu niŋdi o nin' ni. (If someone doesn't like you and you swim with him, he says you're making dust to cover his eyes.)

english: to tip over, roll over, tilt
example: Serial: gbiligi lihi. Sim: biligi, bili, gbihi, gbili. See: gbili; gbilisi.  O gbiligi n-lu tiŋa. (He tipped over and fell.) Kpahimmi Naawuni tuma; o yi niŋ shɛli ka di gbiligi, ŋuni n-lee ni tooi mal' li zali dɛde? (Consider God's work; who can straighten what he has made crooked?) Paɣa yi bɔbi bɔbiga n zaŋ gbiligi o tapaɣa, di wuhirimi ni o nyɛla ninchini lana. (If a woman wears a headscarf that covers her cheeks, it shows she is disrespectful.) Ka Gallio bi gbiligi lihi ba gba. (And Gallio took no notice of them.)
Sim: gbaliŋ.  Tii maa vari daa viɛli pam ka di kul wali gbilim. (The tree's leaves were very beautiful and it fruited abundantly.)
Sim: gbiɣu. Ant: di.  Sua maa gbilim ya, di ku tooi ŋmaai binshɛɣu. (The knife is blunt, it can't cut anything.) A chɛ ka o takɔbi noli gbilim. (You let his sword become blunt.)
O ko la gbiliŋgbi o pɔŋ ni. (He planted gbiliŋgbi yams.) Gbiliŋgbi maa wɔla pam. (The gbiliŋgbi yams produced well.) Naa maa nyɛla Gbiliŋgbi. (The chief is a king.)

english: to lend an ear, pay attention
example: Colloc: gbilisi tibili. Sim: gbiligi.  Yi daa zaɣisiya ni yi ku gbilisi tibili wum. (You refused to pay attention and listen.) N ni tooi lan gbilisi tibili wum baan dɔbba mini baan' paɣiba yila. (I can listen carefully to the songs of male and female musicians.) Niriba ŋɔ zaa nyɛla bɛn ni wuhi shɛba ka bɛ ni tooi gbilisi tibili n-wum a yɛlimuɣisira. (All these people are educated and can listen carefully to your problems.)

Etym: OVN *GBIL, KPIL.  Bɛ laɣim la tiŋa n niŋ gbilli. (They gathered earth into a heap.) N daa pirigi ba gbilligbilli ʒii buyi. (I divided them into two sections.) Saŋmarisi bɛn laɣim gbilligbilli. (The stars gather in constellations.)

See: gbilli. Colloc: saŋmarisi bɛn laɣim gbilligbilli.  N ni yihi n niriba Izraɛlnim maa zaa Ijipti tiŋgbɔŋ maa ni gbilligbilli. (I will bring my people Israel out of Egypt in orderly ranks.) Bɛ laɣimmi gbilligbilli ʒini momaha zuɣu. (They sat in rows on the green grass.) Saŋmarisi bɛn laɣim gbilligbilli be zuɣusaa ŋɔ ti ku ne. (The stars of heaven and their constellations will not show their light.)
Sim: balim balim, baalim, biɛla biɛla.  Di ʒiya gbim. (It is quiet.) O ʒiya gbim ka bi yɛli yɛltɔɣa. (He sat quietly without saying anything.)

Sim: kpaɣi, kparim, muɣisi, shɛbi, muɣi.  Gbim be soli maa zuɣu, niriba bi tooi gari. (There is congestion on the road, people can't pass.) Gbim maa zuɣu, bɛ bi tooi kpe duu maa ni. (Because of the congestion, they couldn't enter the room.)

english: to be impoverished, make sb. poor
example: O daabiligu maa zuɣu, o gbimisi ya. (Because of his business, he became impoverished.) Lala yɛla maa gbimisi o. (Those matters made him poor.)

english: to nip off, prune; to shed feathers
example: See: gbɛm.  O gbimisi la tia maa vari. (He pruned the tree's leaves.) Noo maa gbimisiri la di kɔbiri. (The fowl is shedding its feathers.)
Bɛn' gbimisi ka lara, bɛn' mi bɛ yɛlli. (Those who whisper together and laugh know what it's about.) Bɛ gbimisi la ni bɛ ti o barina. (They plotted secretly to harm him.)
See: gbini.  Bɛ gbina zaa be garo maa zuɣu. (All their bottoms are on the bed.) Di gbina maa zaa dii viɛla. (All its meanings are clear.)
Sim: dindaluŋ, dukpimbaɣinda, napaɣisuli, sakɔɣu, toliga.  Gbinafarinako maa be mɔɣu ni. (The grasshopper is in the bush.) Gbinafarinako pam be puu ni. (Many grasshoppers are in the farm.)
See: binakpaa.  Gbinakpaa maa be tia gbinni. (The millipede is under the tree.) Gbinakpaa mali naba pam. (The millipede has many legs.)
See: gbini; chibi.  Dɔɣite maa ti o la gbinchibili. (The doctor gave him an enema.) Gbinchibili maa sɔŋ o ka o tooi nye bindi. (The enema helped him to defecate.)

english: nakedness, raggedness, lack of sufficient clothes
example: See: gbini; fini.  O daa chani gbinfini, ka ti ti o sitira. (He was going about naked, and we gave him clothes.) Gbinfini bi viɛla, nira tu ni o ye sitira. (Nakedness is not good, a person should wear clothes.)
Gbingbamba maa gbibi o niŋgbuna ni binyɛra. (The membrane holds things inside his body.) Gbingbamba maa chɛhiya. (The membrane tore.)

Colloc: tiri gbini, su gbini, doli X gbini.  Bɛ yi tabiri a gbini, a durila yom. (If they support your bottom, you climb quickly.) Jaŋa lari juɣu zuɣu polo ka ŋun jaŋ' gbina fa? (The monkey laughs at the vulture's bald head, but what about his own bottom?) Naawuni m-mi ŋun gbina piɛla. (God knows whose bottom is white.) Di gbinni maa nyɛla din zilim. (Its meaning is deep.) O gbini maa do polo ni. (His secret is exposed.)
Syn: nooti. See: gbini; mɔgbini.  O gbini la mia maa ni. (He made a knot in the rope.) Gbina maa be mia maa ni. (The knots are in the rope.)

english: to join two pieces of rope; to tie a knot; to become knotted
example: Sim: lo, tuɣi, vili. Cpart: gbinigi. See: gbaagbini.  O gbini la mi' ayi maa taba. (He joined the two ropes together.) N gbindila mia. (I am tying a knot in the rope.) Bɛ gbini la taba. (They are knotted together - referring to mating dogs.)
Cpart: gbini. Sim: lɔrigi. See: gbini.  O gbinigi la mia maa. (He untied the rope.) Gbinigim li ka ti labi gbini. (Untie it so we can retie it.)
See: gbinni; liri.  O mali gbinliriga o daabiligu ni. (He has a means of profit in his business.) Gbinliriga maa zuɣu, o nyari la liɣiri pam. (Because of his trade secret, he makes a lot of money.)

english: underneath, beneath, under, behind; near, at; East
example: Colloc: gbin' na, gbinna. Cpart: zuɣusaa. Sim: lɔŋni.  O ʒini la tia ŋɔ gbinni n vuhira. (He sat under this tree resting.) Zaŋmiya li n labisi bɔhigunim' naŋ do gbunni ŋɔ. (Use it to answer the questions below.) Tua lan nyɛ din tiri ti mahim ka di gbuni ʒinibu mali suhudoo. (The baobab gives us shade and it's peaceful sitting under it.) Bɛ yi ti ka sara maligu bimbini gbinna. (When they approach the sacrifice-altar.) Alɔbo saa kum gbinni, o yɛn bula nyɛviliŋmahinima. (If a terrible thunderbolt sounds in the east, it will kill those with short lives.)

See: gbini. Colloc: X ka gbinni.  Pita chihi ni o bi baŋ a ni yɛli shɛm maa gbinni. (Peter denied knowing the meaning of what he said.) Sokam zahindi daa bela di ko, ka mali di gbinni. (Each dream had its own meaning.) Sana ku tooi baŋ tiŋ' gbinni. (A stranger can't know the secrets of a village.) Nolɔɣu zo n-nyɛ pɔhim, pɔhim mi n-viiri nolɔɣu gbinni. (The wind is the cock's friend, and reveals its bottom/secret.)

SynD: zinyaɣili (E.D.). Sim: anyinsi, vinyaɣili, wawuluŋ.  O gbinnyaɣili zuɣu, o bi tumdi tuma. (Because of his laziness, he doesn't work.) Gbinnyaɣili bi viɛla, di chɛ ka nira fara. (Laziness is not good, it makes a person poor.)

See: gbini; vii.  O gbinviili zuɣu, niriba zaa baŋ o yɛla. (Because of the exposure of his secret, everyone knows his matters.) Gbinviili bi viɛla, di tooi tahi barina na. (Exposing secrets is not good, it can bring trouble.)
See: gbini; voli. Sim: nyiriga.  O gbinvoli biɛri o. (His anus hurts.) Pan' kurili so gbini voli. (An old vagina is better than an arsehole.)
See: gbinyaɣu; -bila.  Gbinyabila maa doli la o ma. (The duckling follows its mother.) Gbinyabihi maa be kom ni. (The ducklings are in the water.)

Syn: dondira, kpayuma, vinyaɣilana.  Nyin' bi' biɛ' gbinyaɣilan' ŋɔ! (You bad, lazy person!) Bɛ nyɛla gbinyaɣinima, dina n-chɛ ka bɛ yɛli, 'Chɛ ka ti chaŋ ti mali sara.' (They are lazy, that's why they say, 'Let us go and make sacrifices.')
See: gbinyɛlɔɣu.  Gbinyaɣu maa be kom ni. (The duck is in the water.) Ʒiɛmani ŋɔ gbinyari bihi n-chani tooni ka bɛ manim' doli. (Today's children are like ducks; the little ones lead their mothers.)

english: drake, male duck, teal, spur-winged goose
example: See: gbinyaɣu; lɔɣu.  Gbinyɛlɔɣu maa be kom ni. (The drake is in the water.) Gbinyɛlɔɣu maa mali viɛlim. (The drake is beautiful.)
O mali gbinzim pam. (He has much wealth.) Gbinzim zuɣu, o tooi sɔŋdi niriba. (Because of his wealth, he can help people.)
See: mɔgbini; gbiŋgbaŋ; nyugbuŋ.  O ŋma la gbiŋ mɔri. (He cut a clump of grass.) Gbiŋ maa be mɔɣu ni. (The clump of grass is in the bush.)

InvMat: gballi. Sim: baŋlari, chimli, gumachuɣuzuli.  Gbiŋgbaŋ maa be mɔɣu ni. (The reed is in the bush.) Mɔɣu ni biŋkɔb' shɛb' bɛn be gbiŋgbaŋ ni la. (The wild animals in the reeds.) Di nyɛla yi silim yi zuɣuri tiŋa kaman gbiŋgbama la? (Is it that you should bow your heads like reeds?) Tantee wuɣ' gballi ka gbuŋgbaŋ ŋuna? (The short tantee grass makes a mat, but what about the tall elephant grass?)
O zaŋ gbiŋgbarinliŋa n niŋ bindirigu. (He put food on a metal plate.) Zaŋmi li kuri gbiŋgbarinlinsi. (Take them and forge them into sheets.)

See: luŋa.  Bɛ ŋmɛri la gbiŋgbarinluŋa salima ni. (They play cymbals at the festival.) M bemi kaman dawule bee gbiŋgbarinlun' shɛŋa bɛ ni mali ŋmɛri taba. (I would be like a gong-gong or cymbals that they bang together.)
Sim: baɣiri, batibati, bayaɣiti.  O firi la gbiŋgbɛli ni. (He sank in the mud.) Gbiŋgbɛli maa be soli zuɣu, ti ku tooi gari. (Mud is on the road, we can't pass.)

Gen: noo.  Gbiŋgbiŋ maa be dundɔŋ ni. (The short-feathered hen is in the courtyard.) Saŋmar' shɛba bɛn laɣim taba ka bɛ booni ba 'Gbiŋgbiŋ min' o bihi la'. (The constellation they call 'the short-feathered hen and her chicks' - the Pleiades.)

english: to drown sb.; to hold under water until full; to force-feed
example: Syn: gbirigi.  O gbiri la o maŋa kom ni. (He drowned himself in water.) O gbiri la siŋa maa kom ni hal ka di pali. (He held the pot under water until it was full.) O gbiri la bia maa bihim. (She force-fed the child milk.)
Gbirifo maa gbi la bɔɣili. (The mole dug a hole.) Gbirifo be mɔɣu ni. (The mole is in the bush.)
Gbiriga tia maa be la mɔɣu ni. (The Combretum tree is in the bush.) Bɛ zaŋdi gbiriga dɔri n mɛri yiya. (They use Combretum wood to build houses.)

Sim: dabiga, kaɣalom, kɔnyurili, kpamvɔɣu, naɣiyihim, suiin, wɔrikariwana, wɔrimɔri, yihim. See: gbirikɔɣu.  O zaŋ gbiriga n di o wahu. (He used horse-grass to feed his horse.) Gbiriga maa zooi pam. (The horse-grass grows a lot.)
O zaŋ gbirigbiri n ʒi kom. (She used a long-necked gourd to carry water.) Gbirigbiri maa yi lu, di nyɛɣira. (If the long-necked gourd falls, it breaks.)

english: to boil, come to a rolling boil; to unsettle
example: Sim: fuɣi, fuɣiri, kpeei, tam.  Kom maa gbirigiri la duɣu ni. (The water is boiling in the pot.) O chɛ ka kɔbulli ni kom gbirigiri. (He made the depths churn like a boiling cauldron.)

english: to hold under water until full
example: Syn: gbiri. See: gbiri.  O gbirigi la siŋa maa kom ni hal ka di pali. (He held the pot under water until it was full.) Gbirigim siŋa maa. (Hold the pot under water.)
See: gbiriga.  O zaŋ gbirikɔɣu n di o wahu. (He used hay to feed his horse.) Gbirikɔɣu maa kuui. (The hay is dry.)
Sim: tigburigu, mundili.  O ŋma la tia maa ka chɛ gbirili. (He cut the tree and left a stump.) Wulipolli ni ti puhi yi Jɛsi gbirili ni na. (A new shoot will come up from the stump of Jesse.)

Soli maa nyɛla gbirimagbirima. (The road is rough and bumpy.) O kukoli nyɛla gbirimagbirima dama o ŋubi la sima. (His voice is hoarse because he ate groundnuts.) N ni mali luɣ' shɛŋa din be gbirimagbirima la ka di niŋ polipoli. (I will make the rough places smooth.)

Sim: tariga, naŋgbaŋgbiriŋ. See: gbiriŋkaba.  Bɛ ʒiya kom maa gbiriŋ. (They are sitting at the water's edge.) Bɛ daa yiɣisi Sukɔt n-chaŋ ti dɔni Itam tingban' nɛl' naŋgbaŋ' gbiriŋ. (They set off from Succoth and stopped at Etam on the edge of the desert.)
See: gbiriŋ; 'kabili.  Bɛ sɔɣi la gbiriŋkaba ni. (They hid in the caves.) Gbiriŋkaba maa be mɔɣili yaɣili. (The undercut banks are by the stream.)
Yuŋ maa nyɛla gbiti. (The night is dark.) Gbiti be duu maa ni. (Darkness is in the room.)
Sim: voli.  O kpe la gbiuŋ ni. (He entered the cave.) Gbiuŋ maa be zoli maa ni. (The cave is in the mountain.)

english: to be slightly ill, have a slight fever
example: O gbɔra, o ku tooi chaŋ tuma ni. (He is slightly ill, he can't go to work.) Bia maa gbɔra, o mali fiiba biɛla. (The child has a slight fever.)

english: to keep, hold with hand, hold back, sustain
example: See: gbibi.  O gbubi la o nuu ni. (He held it in his hand.) Gbubimi li viɛnyɛla. (Hold it well.)
O ninni nyɛla gbufu. (His face is dirty.) Gbufu bi viɛla. (Dirty face is not good.)

english: to be common, plentiful (prices come down)
example: Bindira maa gbuɣi ya, di daa booi. (Food is plentiful, its price has come down.) Sima gbuɣi ya yuuni ŋɔ. (Groundnuts are plentiful this year.)
See: gbuhi.  Nɛma maa gbuɣi ya daa ni. (The goods remained unsold in the market.) Gbuɣi maa zaa kpalim ya. (All the remainder is left.)

english: to vomit voluntarily, spit out; to chew the cud; to prise out; to come out
example: Sim: ti, tuhi. See: gbuɣili.  O gbuɣi la bindirigu maa. (He vomited the food.) Baa ni yi gbuɣi, ka lan labi ti dir' li. (A dog returns to its vomit.) Biŋkɔb' shɛb' bɛn gbuɣiri ŋubira maa. (Animals which chew the cud.) O gbuɣi la kuɣili maa. (He prised the stone out.)
See: gbuɣi.  Nahu maa gbuɣiri la gbuɣili. (The cow is chewing the cud.) Gbuɣili maa be o puli ni. (The cud is in its stomach.)
SynR: jaŋgbimbila. Sim: gbuɣimpolli. See: -bila.  Gbuɣimbila maa ŋmɛri la diɛma. (The lion cub is playing.) Gbuɣimbihi maa doli bɛ ma. (The lion cubs follow their mother.)
Gen: gbuɣinli. Sim: gbuɣimbila, gbuɣindibiga. See: 'polli.  Gbuɣimpolli maa mali yaa pam. (The young male lion is very strong.) Gbuɣim' pola min ti kɔŋ bindirigu. (Even young lions will lack food.)

Gen: gbuɣinli. Cpart: gbuɣinnyaŋ. Sim: gbuɣindibiga, gbuɣinpolli. See: -laa.  Gbuɣindaa maa guli la o bihi. (The male lion guards his cubs.) Bɛ nina ni daa ŋmanila gbuɣindahi nina ni. (Their teeth were like male lions' teeth.)
Sim: gbuɣimpolli. See: 'dibiga.  Gbuɣindibiga maa ŋmɛri la diɛma. (The lion cub is playing.) Gbuɣindibihi maa be mɔɣu ni. (The lion cubs are in the bush.)

Colloc: gbuɣinli kumsi, ŋum. SynR: gbuŋgburugu, jaŋgbini, yɔnaa, zuɣigbilli.  Gbuɣinli maa ŋum la mɔɣu ni. (The lion roared in the bush.) O fa Daniɛl gbuɣima nuu ni bahi. (He saved Daniel from the lions.) Gbuɣim daa kumsi ni yirigi tiŋa. (The lion roared and the land was in an uproar.)
Gen: gbuɣinli. Cpart: gbuɣindaa. See: -nyaŋ.  Gbuɣinnyaŋ maa guli la o bihi. (The lioness guards her cubs.) Gbuɣin' nyama ayi daa yi tibɔŋ ni na. (Two lionesses came out of the forest.)

english: to remain unsold (glut, drug on market)
example: See: gbuɣi.  Nɛma maa gbuhi ya daa ni. (The goods remained unsold in the market.) Bindira gbuhi ya dama di zooi pam. (Food is unsold because it's too plentiful.)
See: gbili.  O gbuli la duɣu maa. (He tilted the pot.) Di gbuli garo maa. (Don't tilt the bed.)
See: gbiligi.  O gbuligi n-lu tiŋa. (He tilted and fell.) Gbuligim li ka ti tuhi. (Tilt it so we can straighten it.)

english: to be dirty, catch the dirt; to swarm, pile up; to accumulate; to do together
example: Colloc: gbulim chaŋ, gbulim yo. Sim: laɣim, gubi, bali.  O binyɛrigu gbulim ya. (His garment is dirty.) Zɔhi maa gbulim la nimdi maa zuɣu. (Flies swarmed on the meat.) O gbulim la liɣiri pam. (He accumulated a lot of money.) Ti gbulim chaŋ daa ni. (We went together to the market.)
See: gbilli.  O mali duɣu gbulli. (He has a round pot.) O nyɛla ninvuɣ' gbulli. (He is a huge person.)

Sim: gbanjaŋa, gbanyani, lɔŋ, saka, soo.  Gbuluɣu maa be kom ni. (The frog is in the water.) Gbuluɣu bi firiti ni mia. (A frog with a string tied to it cannot sink.) Gbuluɣu noli n-kuri o baa ni. (The frog's mouth kills it in a marshy place.)
See: gbili; gbuli.  O garo maa nyɛla gbuluŋ. (His bed is tilted.) Gbuluŋ bi viɛla. (Being tilted is not good.)
See: gbim.  Kom maa ʒiya gbum. (The water is very calm.) Gbum be yaɣili maa. (There is coolness over there.)
Mɔri maa gbum ya. (The grass is crowded.) Bindirigu maa gbum ya puu ni. (The crop is crowded in the farm.)
Etym: onomat. + duu.  Gbumgbum duu maa be Tamale. (The power-station is in Tamale.) Gbumgbum duu maa tiri ti la nɛma. (The power-station gives us power.)
See: gbimisi.  O daabiligu maa gbumisi o. (His business impoverished him.) Gbumisi maa zuɣu, o bi lahi mali liɣiri. (Because of impoverishment, he no longer has money.)
See: gbimisi.  O gbumisi la tia maa vari. (He pruned the tree's leaves.) Gbumisim tihi maa ka di wali viɛnyɛla. (Prune the trees so they bear fruit well.)
See: gbinfini.  O gbunfuni zuɣu, niriba lara o. (Because of his raggedness, people laugh at him.) Gbunfuni bi viɛla. (Raggedness is not good.)

english: thin tissue in diaphragm (for children's drums)
example: Bɛ zaŋ gbungbamba n mali bihi guŋgɔna. (They use thin tissue to make children's drums.) Gbungbamba maa chɛhirimi. (The thin tissue tears easily.)
O zaŋ gbungbunlɔɣiri n lo o wahu zuɣu. (He put saddlebags on his horse.) Gbungbunlɔɣiri maa pali bindira. (The saddlebags are full of food.)

See: gbiŋgbiŋ.  Gbungbuŋ maa be dundɔŋ ni. (The short-feathered hen is in the courtyard.) Saŋmar' shɛba bɛn laɣim taba ka bɛ booni ba 'Gbungbuŋ min' o bihi la'. (The constellation they call 'the short-feathered hen and her chicks'.)
See: gbuŋgburugu.  Gbungburiga maa ŋum la. (The lion roared.) Gbuŋgburugu gbaŋ yi bi lo saɣimbu, piɛm nyɔɣirila o zuɣu ni. (If the lion doesn't want its hide spoiled, an arrow pierces its head.)
See: gbini.  O gbuni maa be garo maa zuɣu. (His bottom is on the bed.) Di gbuni maa nyɛla din zilim. (Its meaning is deep.)
See: gbini.  O gbuni la mia maa ni. (He made a knot in the rope.) Gbuni maa chihiya. (The knot came undone.)
See: gbinliriga.  O mali gbunliriga o daabiligu ni. (He has a means of profit in his business.) Gbunliriga maa zuɣu, o nyari la liɣiri pam. (Because of his trade secret, he makes a lot of money.)
See: gbinnyaɣili.  O gbunnyaɣili zuɣu, o bi tumdi tuma. (Because of his laziness, he doesn't work.) Gbunnyaɣili bi viɛla. (Laziness is not good.)
See: gbinviili.  O gbunviili zuɣu, niriba zaa baŋ o yɛla. (Because of the exposure of his secret, everyone knows his matters.) Gbunviili bi viɛla. (Exposing secrets is not good.)
See: gbinyaɣu.  Gbunyaɣu maa be kom ni. (The duck is in the water.) Gbunyaɣu maa mali viɛlim. (The duck is beautiful.)
See: gbiŋ.  O ŋma la gbuŋ mɔri. (He cut a clump of grass.) Gbuŋ maa be mɔɣu ni. (The clump of grass is in the bush.)

See: gbiŋgbaŋ.  Gbuŋgbaŋ maa be mɔɣu ni. (The reed is in the bush.) Tantee wuɣ' gballi ka gbuŋgbaŋ ŋuna? (The short tantee grass makes a mat, but what about the tall elephant grass?) Gbungbaŋ nyɛlim gooni ku du tia. (The reed grass hangs over the wall but can't climb a tree.)
SynR: gbuɣinli.  Gbuŋgburugu maa ŋum la. (The lion roared.) Gbuŋgburugu gbaŋ yi bi lo saɣimbu, piɛm nyɔɣirila o zuɣu ni. (If the lion doesn't want its hide spoiled, an arrow pierces its head.)

english: to drown; to hold under water; to force-feed
example: See: gbiri.  O gburi la o maŋa kom ni. (He drowned himself in water.) O gburi la siŋa maa kom ni. (He held the pot under water.) O gburi la bia maa bihim. (She force-fed the child milk.)
Gburiga maa be mɔɣu ni. (The plant is in the bush.) Bɛ zaŋdi gburiga n tibiri dɔriti. (They use the plant for medicine.)
See: gbirigi.  Kom maa gburigi la duɣu ni. (The water is boiling in the pot.) Gburigim li ka di gbiri. (Stir it so it boils.)
See: gbirigi.  O gburigi la siŋa maa kom ni. (He dipped the pot in the water.) Gburigim li kom ni. (Dip it in the water.)
See: gbiriga.  O zaŋ gburigu n di o wahu. (He used grass to feed his horse.) Gburigu maa zooi pam. (The grass grows a lot.)
See: gbirikɔɣu.  O zaŋ gburikɔɣu n di o wahu. (He used hay to feed his horse.) Gburikɔɣu maa kuui. (The hay is dry.)
See: gbirimagbirima.  Soli maa nyɛla gburimagburima. (The road is rough and bumpy.) O kukoli nyɛla gburimagburima. (His voice is hoarse.)
A ni nya ka bihim maa niŋ gburisim ka timsa. (You will see that the milk thickens and has more substance.) Kom maa gburisim ya. (The water has thickened.)
Gburo! di chaŋ nimaani. (Hey! don't go there.) Gburo! zanimi. (Hey! stop.)
See: gbiriŋ.  Bɛ ʒiya kom maa gburuŋ. (They are sitting at the water's edge.) Gburuŋ maa be mɔɣili yaɣili. (The edge is by the stream.)
See: gbiriŋkaba.  Bɛ sɔɣi la gburuŋkaba ni. (They hid in the caves.) Gburuŋkaba maa be mɔɣili yaɣili. (The undercut banks are by the stream.)
O teeŋa nyɛla gbuu. (His beard is bushy.) Gbuu bi viɛla. (Being too bushy is not good.)

Sim: ye, so, gani. Colloc: gɔbi chinchini.  O gɔbi la chinchini palli n chaŋ salima ni. (He put on a new wrapper and went to the festival.) Bɛ gɔbi o la chinchini ka o leei toondana. (They put a cloth on him and he became a leader.)
See: gɔbili.  O mali gɔbidola nahu din mali yaa pam. (He has a crossbred cow that is very strong.) Gɔbidola maa ni tooi ko puu viɛnyɛla. (The crossbred cow can plough the farm well.)

Cpart: nudirigu. Syn: nuzaa.  O zaŋ o gɔbiga n gbibi sua maa. (He used his left hand to hold the knife.) Gɔbiga maa nyɛla wɔbigu, tɔhibihi booni o lala. (The 'left-handed one' is the elephant, hunters call it that.) O nyɛla gɔbiga, o sabiri la o nuzaa. (He is left-handed, he writes with his left hand.)

See: -lana.  O nyɛla gɔbigalana, o zaŋdi la o nuzaa n tumdi tuma. (He is left-handed, he uses his left hand to work.) Gɔbisinima pam be tobu ni, bɛ tooi tuhira viɛnyɛla. (Many left-handers are in battle, they fight well.)

See: gɔbiga. Colloc: chaŋ gɔbili, niŋ gɔbili.  O chaŋ la gɔbili, di tu ni o chaŋ nudirigu. (He went the wrong way, he should have gone right.) O niŋ la gɔbili o tuma ni. (He did his work the wrong way.)

See: gɔbidola.  O mali gɔbili nahu din mali gurigu. (He has a zebu cow with a hump.) Gɔbili maa mali yaa pam. (The zebu is very strong.) O nyɛla gɔbili, o zuɣu gɔɣiya. (He is a hunchback, his back is bent.)
See: gɔbigalana.  Gɔbisinima maa zaa be tobu ni n tuhira. (All the left-handers are in the battle fighting.) Bɛ mi ni bɛ nyɛla gɔbisinima. (They know they are left-handed.)

See: gom; gali.  Gɔgaliga gbaagi o, o bi tooi gbihi yuŋ. (Insomnia caught him, he can't sleep at night.) A gogaliga dali ka a baŋdi a zɔsimo. (On your sleepless night you know your true friend.) Kunduŋ me yili tabili baa, baa gɔgaliga. (Hyena builds next to dog, dog can't sleep.)
See: gom; gbihi.  Nyin' gɔgbihira, yiɣisima n ti tum tuma! (You sleeper, wake up and work!) Gɔgbihiriba gbihirila yuŋ, bɛ bi tumda. (Sleepers sleep at night, they don't work.)
Serial: pɔri gɔɣi. Sim: gbani, gɔŋ, pɔri, silim, kurim.  O kurigi pam, o pɔri gɔɣi. (He is very old, he is bent over.) Dɔro maa chɛ ka o pɔri gɔɣi. (The sickness made him bend over.)
Bɛ gɔɣi la waa Gurunli ni salima ni. (They danced the Grunshi dance at the festival.) O mi gɔɣibu viɛnyɛla, o tooi wari li. (He knows the Grunshi dance well, he can dance it.)
Sim: gingaani.  O gɔɣiri la o chandi ni kamani o nyɛla bundana. (He walks arrogantly like he is a rich person.) O gɔɣi dama o di nasara tobu ni. (He flaunts because he won in battle.)
O kparila gɔɣili palli n chaŋ daa. (He wore a new sleeveless garment to the market.) Bɔ n-niŋ ka a lɛbi a gɔɣili yɛ? (Why are you wearing your smock inside out?)

Sim: gurugulana, gɔbili. See: gɔɣi; gɔɣu.  Bɛ booni o la gɔɣiluŋ dama o zuɣu gɔɣiya. (They call him humpy because his back is bent.) O nyɛla gɔɣiluŋ, o ku tooi teegi o maŋa. (He is a humpy person, he can't straighten up.)
See: gɔɣi.  Paɣ' gɔɣu n-nyɛ o, o ku tooi ʒini tuhi. (She is a bent woman, she can't sit straight.) O nyɛla gɔɣu, o nina lihiri tiŋa. (He is hunched, his eyes look at the ground.)

Tɔbbihi maa zani bɛ gɔɣu ni n guhi dima. (The soldiers stood in their battle line waiting for the enemy.) Saha shɛli bɛ ni daa maani shili ni bɛ chaŋ tɔb' gɔɣ' ni. (When they were preparing to go to the battle line.)
See: goo.  Gɔhi maa chibi o naba ka ʒim yi. (The thorns pricked his foot and blood came out.) Gɔhi pam be soli maa zuɣu, di tu ni ti ŋmaai ba. (Many thorns are on the path, we need to cut them.)

See: goo.  Gɔhi maa pam be mɔɣu ni, a yi chaŋ ni a ni kpe. (Many thorn trees are in the bush, if you go there you'll get into them.) O kpe gɔhi ni ka o naba gɔhi. (He got into the thorns and his foot got pricked.)

english: to catch (flying/thrown); to catch hold, snatch, seize
example: Sim: gbaai.  O gɔhi la bɔli maa ka di bi lu. (He caught the ball and it didn't fall.) O va n-gɔh' o binyɛrigu tiŋli, ka di cheei. (He caught hold of his cloak and it tore.) O dii gɔh' o nuu ka o yi. (He suddenly grabbed his hand and he left.)
Syn: ʒe.  Nahu maa gɔhi o la dama o miri li. (The cow gored him because he went near it.) Naɣikpɛma maa gɔhi la o tɔ ka o zo. (The big bull gored his opponent and he ran.)
See: gahi.  Bɛ zaŋ kuriti n gɔhi dapuhi maa taba. (They used iron to hook the poles together.) Gɔhimi ba taba viɛnyɛla. (Hook them together well.)

Colloc: bolli gɔnaate. Syn: gɔni.  O nyɛla bolli gɔnaate, o ŋmɛri bɔli viɛnyɛla. (He is an expert footballer, he plays football well.) Gɔnaate! a tuma maa naai viɛnyɛla. (Bravo! your work finished well.)

See: gɔŋ.  Soli maa nyɛla gɔnagɔna, di bi tuhi. (The path is sinuous, it's not straight.) O yɛltɔɣa nyɛla gɔnagɔna, so bi baŋdi o ni yɛri shɛli. (His speech is crooked, no one understands what he's saying.)
O ko la gɔndili tihi o pɔŋ ni. (He planted pawpaw trees in his farm.) Gɔnda maa nyɛɣisa pam, biɛla maa diri li. (The pawpaws are very sweet, the children are eating them.)
Syn: gonaate.  Gɔni! a yila maa viɛla pam. (Bravo! your songs are very good.) Gɔni! Naawuni ti ti alaafee. (Glory be! God gave us health.)
O nyɛla gɔni o tuma ni, so bi gari o. (He is an expert at his work, no one surpasses him.) Shɛshɛri' goni ku she gooni din chɛhi. (An expert sewer cannot sew a cracked wall.)
See: goonje.  O ŋmɛri la gɔnje salima ni. (He is playing the fiddle at the festival.) Gɔnje maa kum nyɛɣisa, niriba wumdi li. (The fiddle's sound is sweet, people hear it.)

See: gɔŋ. Colloc: gbali gɔŋ. Sim: 'golima, golimgolim, ŋmaliŋ, shɛliŋ.  Ti dolila so' gɔŋ n chaŋ daa. (We followed a crooked path to market.) Ʒiri din tuhi n-gari yɛlimaŋ' gɔŋ. (A straight lie is better than a crooked truth.)

english: to become crooked; to bend or curve
example: Serial: pɔri gɔŋ. Sim: golim, shɛlim, pɔri, gɔɣi. Cpart: tuhi.  Binshɛli din gɔŋ ku tooi lan teei tuhi. (What is crooked can't be made straight.) Jɔab daa tim ni bɛ cham' ti gɔŋ Abinɛr labisi na. (Joab sent to make Abner come back.) Dɔro ni daa mali paɣ' so m-pɔri gɔŋ o yuun' pisaayika. (A woman was bent over for  years.)
Sim: gɔni, gɔnaate.  Gɔŋga! a tuma maa viɛla. (Well done! your work is good.) Gɔŋga! a wa waa maa viɛnyɛla. (Wonderful! you danced well.)
See: gom; ŋmaa.  O gbihi la gɔŋmaa wuntaŋa ni. (He took a short nap in the afternoon.) Gɔŋmaa maa nyaaŋa, o yiɣisi ni yaa. (After the nap, he woke up strong.)
O zaŋ gɔpɔhiga n tibi o zuɣu doro. (He used scented root medicine to treat his headache.) Gɔpɔhiga maa nyɔm viɛla. (The scented root medicine smells good.)

See: goo; piɛliga.  Gɔpiɛliga tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, di gbaŋ piɛla. (The white-barked thorn tree is in the bush, its bark is white.) Gɔpiɛliga mali gɔhi pam. (The white-barked thorn tree has many thorns.)
See: gɔɣu.  Tɔbbihi maa zani bɛ gɔri ni n guhi tobu. (The soldiers stood in their battle lines waiting for war.) Gɔri maa ni, bɛ daa tuhi pam. (In the battle lines, they fought hard.)
See: go.  O gɔri maa ni, o nya la o zo maa. (During his roaming, he saw his friend.) Gɔri maa daa yuui, o labi na zaawuni. (The roaming took long, he came back in the evening.)
Bia maa gɔri la o buku ni. (The child is scribbling in his book.) O gɔri la gbaŋ maa zuɣu ni pɛn. (He is scribbling on the paper with a pen.)

Bɛ zaŋ niɣi maa n niŋ gɔrigu ni yuŋ. (They put the cows in the pen at night.) Naɣ'gɔrigu maa waɣila, niɣi maa tooi gindi di ni. (The cattle kraal is wide, the cows can move around in it.) Colloc: naɣ'gɔrigu. Sim: naɣikpaa, gariche.
O zaŋ gɔrigu n che chi maa. (He used a sickle to harvest the millet.) Gɔrigu maa nyɛɣisa, di tooi che viɛnyɛla. (The sickle is sharp, it harvests well.)
O duɣi la gɔrili n ti bihi maa. (She cooked boiled bean leaves for the children.) Gɔrili nyɛla bindirigu din mali alaafee. (Boiled bean leaves are healthy food.)
Syn: gɔrimdima.  O gɔrimgaŋda maa daa sɔŋ o pam o chandi ni. (The people he met on his journey helped him much.) Gɔrimgaŋdiba maa zaa daa paɣi o. (All the people he met greeted him.)

N'a gɔrimnima! yi yi paai na. (Greetings, travelers! you have arrived.) O gɔrimnima maa daa ti o la bindirigu. (The people he met on his journey gave him food.) Colloc: N'a gɔrimnima! Syn: gɔrimgaŋda.

Colloc: vuhi gosima. Sim: gom.  Gom gbaagi o, o vuhi la gosima. (He fell asleep, he is snoring.) Gosima mali o pam dama o bi gbihi viɛnyɛla yuŋ. (He is very sleepy because he didn't sleep well at night.)
See: goo; tia. Syn: goo.  Gɔtia tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, di mali gɔhi pam. (The acacia tree is in the bush, it has many thorns.) Paɣiviɛligakushihikom ni ʒe gotihi sunsuuni. (Like a lily among thorns.)
From: (Eng.).  O ko la gɔva tihi o pɔŋ ni. (He planted guava trees in his farm.) Gɔva wala maa nyɛɣisa pam. (The guava fruits are very sweet.)
See: gom.  Gɔyuunsi gbaagi o tuma ni, o ku tooi tum. (Drowsiness caught him at work, he can't work.) O bi tooi kari o maŋa gɔyuunsi ni. (He can't keep himself from drowsiness.)
See: -lana.  Buɣim datee, baɣa bɔ m-be gɔyuunsilana ni? (Does a burning stick care about a half-asleep person?) Gɔyuunsilana maa bi wumdi yɛltɔɣa. (The drowsy person doesn't hear speech.)
See: goo; ʒee. Syn: nagtibichɛriga.  Gɔʒee tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, di gbaŋ ʒeɛ. (The red-barked acacia is in the bush, its bark is red.) Gɔʒee mali gɔhi pam. (The red-barked acacia has many thorns.)
From: (Heb.).  O yo la anzinfa gɛra pishi daa ni. (He paid twenty gerahs of silver in the market.) Gɛra pishi nyɛla anzinfa din galisi. (Twenty gerahs is a lot of silver.)
Ghana tiŋgbani ŋɔ puuni, ti kɔri la bindira pam. (In this country of Ghana, we farm a lot of food.) O yo la Ghana kɔbigu n da li. (He paid 00 Ghana Cedis to buy it.)

See: gili.  O gihibu tiŋa maa ni daa yuui, o labi na zaawuni. (His going round the town took long, he came back in the evening.) Gihibu maa ni, o nya la o zo kurigu. (During his rounds, he saw his old friend.)

O ŋmahi la bindirigu maa gilagila n ti bihi maa. (He cut the food into chunks and gave to the children.) N daa ŋmahiŋmahi m mam maa gilagila n zaŋ ti ba. (I cut my concubine into pieces and gave to them.)
O puli pali gilata dama o di pam. (His stomach is completely full because he ate a lot.) Kɔringa daa yɛli ni nyaaŋa booi, fiɛɣu gilata. (The partridge said its back is wet but its gizzard is full.)

english: to go round, travel; to surround; to be around
example: Serial: go n-gili. Sim: go.  Bɛ gindi la tiŋa maa ni n kɔhira. (They go round the town selling.) Gilimiya dunia ŋɔ zaa n ti nya ti dɔɣiriba. (Go all over the world to see our relatives.) O daa gili o maa nyaaŋa n gu o. (He went around behind his mother to protect her.) A ʒiɛyimtali gilila a luɣili kam. (Your faithfulness surrounds you everywhere.) Colloc: viini n-ginda 'wander off-course'
O ŋmɛri la giliga viɛnyɛla salima ni. (He plays the xylophone well at festivals.) Giliga maa kum nyɛɣisa, niriba zaa wumdi li. (The xylophone's sound is sweet, everyone hears it.)

Ti giligi taba daa ni dama ti bi saɣi ti yɛltɔɣa. (We broke with each other at the market because we didn't agree.) O mini o zo giligi ya, bɛ bi lahi laɣinda. (He and his friend have broken up, they don't associate anymore.)

english: to overbalance, tumble; to upset sth.
example: Sim: lɛbigi.  O giligi n-lu tiŋa dama o naba gɔhi. (He overbalanced and fell because his foot got caught.) Di giligi duɣu maa, kom maa ni kpaai. (Don't upset the pot, the water will spill.)

english: to surround; to go round; to give last price
example: Colloc: yuuni giligi. See: gili.  Bɛ giligi o niŋ sunsuuni ka o bi tooi yi. (They surrounded him so he couldn't escape.) Ka yuuni lan gilgi na, ti lahi puhi chuɣu. (And the year came round again, we celebrate the festival again.) O giligi daa maa n naai n da li. (He gave the last price and bought it.)

english: solemn royal processional step; drum rhythm for it
example: Colloc: gam gingaani. Sim: gam, yɔɣi.  Naa maa chaŋ la nayili ni ni gingaani. (The chief walked to the palace with solemn procession.) Jaŋa gamdi gingaani dama o bi nya baa. (The monkey struts proudly because it sees no dog.) Kpakpili bɔri giŋgaani ka baɣiri kani. (The tortoise wants to walk majestically but has no arms.)

Gingaɣifiŋa gbaagi o bia, o bi tooi chaŋ shikuru. (Chickenpox caught his child, he can't go to school.) Gingaɣifiŋa nyɛla dɔro din pɔra, di bi kpiri nira. (Chickenpox is a minor disease, it doesn't kill a person.)

See: goo; giŋgaɣiri.  Gingaɣigoo tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, di mali gɔhi pam. (The strychos spinosa shrub is in the bush, it has many thorns.) Bɛ zaŋdi gingaɣigoo vari n tibiri dɔriti. (They use its leaves for medicine.)

See: giŋgaɣiri. SynD: gingaɣiri (W.D.).  Kum saha, niriba diri la gingaɣili ka gali. (In famine time, people eat gingaɣili leaves to survive.) Bɛ daa wɔbiri la giŋgaɣiri mini tuturi vari. (They stripped leaves of hunger-bush and shrubs.)

english: snail; facial paralysis; juju causing facial paralysis
example: Gingalanyɔɣu maa chana balim balim o garo zuɣu. (The snail walks slowly on its bed.) Gumachuɣu turi gingalayɔɣu ni o naŋgbaŋ ŋmaliŋ. (The chameleon insults the snail about its crooked mouth.) O mali gingalanyɔɣu, o nina bi neeri viɛnyɛla. (He has facial paralysis, his eyes don't open well.)

SynD: gingaɣili (E.D.).  Giŋgaɣiri nyɛla bindirigu kum saha, niriba diri li. (Giŋgaɣiri is food in famine time, people eat it.) Bɛ daa wɔbiri la giŋgaɣiri mini tuturi vari. (They stripped leaves of hunger-bush and shrubs.)
See: gingalanyɔɣu.  Giŋgalayɔɣu maa chana balim balim, di bi zɔra. (The snail walks slowly, it doesn't run.) Giŋgalayɔɣu yi ti nya kom, di yirina. (When the snail sees water, it comes out.)

See: gingalanyɔɣu. Syn: gingalanyɔɣu.  O mali giŋgalayonifu, o nini yini bi neeri. (He has facial paralysis, one of his eyes doesn't open.) Giŋgalayonifu nyɛla dɔro din tooi gbaai ninkura. (Facial paralysis can affect old people.)
From: (Fr.). Sim: kilo.  O zahim la sima giram kɔbigu. (He weighed 00 grammes of groundnuts.) Giram pihinu nyɛla din pɔra. (Fifty grammes is a small amount.)

english: to familiarise self, get used to; to be fed up
example: Colloc: giri lɔŋ.  O giri la Tamale biɛhigu dama o ʒini ni yuun' ata. (He is familiar with life in Tamale because he stayed three years.) Naa suhu daa ti giri zaŋ chaŋ o bia kum maa polo. (The king got over his child's death.) Daanbiɛɣu bini gir' lɔŋ, n ku lahi guhi o. (I've given up hope of a gift from a parasite.)
Girigiri be tiŋa maa ni, niriba zaa zɔra. (Violence is in the town, everyone is running.) Bɛ kana nti liri ti n-tahi girigiri niŋbu na. (They came to attack us and cause mayhem.)

english: to stunt growth, stop, stifle, prevent
example: Ka gɔhi zooi n-girim li, bindirigu maa bi lahi zo. (Thorns grew and stifled it, the food plant no longer grew.) Gɔhi maa girimgirim li, di ku lahi wali. (The thorns completely choked it, it will no longer bear fruit.)

From: (Eng.).  O girisi o chɛchɛ maa ka di bi lahi ŋmɛri vuri. (He greased his bicycle so it doesn't make noise.) Girisim moto maa viɛnyɛla ka di chaŋ viɛnyɛla. (Lubricate the motorcycle well so it runs well.)
O zani la giti, o bi damdi. (He stood firmly, he doesn't shake.) Di nyɛla giti, di bi taɣira. (It is firm, it doesn't change.)

Serial: go n-gili. Colloc: go mɔɣu, go tɔhigu, go kɔhigu, go pagɔrisigu.  O go la mɔɣu ni n ti gbaai waɣi. (He went hunting in the bush to catch a snake.) Gɔ-n-yuui mali tɛha pam. (One who travels long has many ideas.) O go la kɔhigu n chaŋ tinsi pam. (He went trading to many towns.) Colloc: n gɔya (response to 'welcome')

Sim: chirili.  A chaŋ goli dahin yini, di tu ni a gu. (You went on the first day of the month, you should be careful.) Goli yi peei bia yiŋa, kpɛma chanimi n ti diɛm. (When the moon lights a child's house, adults go to play.) Nyini n-zali wuntaŋ' mini goli. (You established the sun and moon.) O lo la goli o nyiŋgɔya ni. (She wears a crescent pendant on her neck.)

english: to hang by hands/feet; to hook
example: Colloc: goli loori. Sim: yili.  Zɔŋ gɔndi la tia zuɣu yuŋ. (The bat hangs on the tree at night.) O goli la loori n chaŋ Tamale. (He took a lorry to Tamale.) O zaŋ kuriti n goli bansi maa ni. (He used iron to hook into the rings.)

Syn: laɣim, nyɛbi, bo.  Bɛ bi simdi ni bidibila mini paɣasaribila goli taba pɔi ka bɛ naai kpuɣi taba. (Boys and girls shouldn't have sex before marriage.) A golidi a paɣa koŋko. (You should only have sex with your wife.) Colloc: niriba ni golindi taba video 'pornographic video'

See: golimuni.  Yuŋ kam goli muni yi ti neei, ti ni nya neesim. (When the full moon shines at night, we'll have light.) Goli muni maa viɛla pam, di neeri kamani wuntaŋa. (The full moon is very beautiful, it shines like the sun.)

See: goli; wulana.  Goli wulana maa neela zaawuni, ti mi ni yuŋ yɛn paai. (The evening star shone in the evening, we know night will come.) Biɛla maa lihiri goli wulana. (The children are looking at the evening star.)
Gen: goli.  Golichaɣu maa neela, di pala goli muni. (The half moon shone, it's not full moon.) Golichaɣu nyɛla din viɛla yuŋ. (The half moon is beautiful at night.)

english: to be relevant; to disentangle, unhook
example: Colloc: X yɛm goligi.  A yɛltɔɣa maa bi goligi ti yɛla. (Your remark is not relevant to our matter.) Goligim mia maa bahi. (Untie the rope.) Bɛn zaa yɛm goligi ka bɛ tooi baŋ shɛli. (All whose minds can understand.)

english: to branch, bend, curve round, return; to twist, deform
example: Ti chaŋ ni ka goligi labina. (We went there and turned back.) Bɛ dii goligi o nam maa n ti o yuli palli. (They changed his chieftaincy title and gave him a new name.)
Syn: golinli.  Koligu maa goligu chihiya, n tu ni n taɣi li. (The bag's handle broke, I need to replace it.) O zaŋ goligu n gbibi koligu maa. (He used the strap to hold the bag.)
Gen: goli. See: kabi.  Golikabli maa dali, ti pili goli palli. (On moonbreak day, we begin the new month.) Golikabli nyɛla din niŋda goli bahigu. (Moonbreak happens at month's end.)

english: to become crooked; to turn off
example: Colloc: golim golimi. Sim: gɔŋ, shɛlim.  Dɔɣu maa golim ya, di bi lahi tuhi. (The wood became crooked, it's no longer straight.) Dagbanli, di gɔlimgɔlimi. (Dagbani is difficult, everything is the wrong way round.) O daa zaŋ ba golimgolim soya. (He took them by winding paths.)

Sim: golimgolim, gɔŋ, ŋmaliŋ, shɛliŋ.  Bɛn mali taali soli nyɛla so' golima. (The way of the guilty is devious.) Yinim' bɛn ka yɛda mini ʒiɛman' golimanim' ŋɔ. (You unbelieving and perverse generation!)
O duɣi la golima n ti bihi maa. (She cooked food from gourd seeds for the children.) Golima nyɛla bindirigu din mali alaafee. (Gourd seed food is healthy.)
See: golinli.  Koligu maa golima zaa chihiya. (All the bag's handles broke.) Golima maa mali viɛlim, bɛ lo ba viɛnyɛla. (The handles are beautiful, they are well made.)
Ʒim golimbu o niŋgbuna ni viɛla, o mali alaafee. (Blood circulation in his body is good, he is healthy.) Golimbu biɛɣu ni tooi tahi barina na. (Poor circulation can bring danger.)

Sim: 'golima, gɔŋ, ŋmaliŋ, shɛliŋ.  Soli maa nyɛla golimgolim, di tu ni ti tuhi li. (The path is crooked, we need to straighten it.) O yɛltɔɣa nyɛla golimgolim, so bi baŋdi li. (His speech is crooked, no one understands it.)
Gen: goli. See: 'muni.  Piɛbimi kikaa goli maa yi ti niŋ golimuni. (Blow the trumpet when the moon is full.) Golimuni maa neela pam yuŋ. (The full moon shone brightly at night.)
See: goli.  Bɛ golinda bi viɛla, di tooi tahiri dɔriti na. (Their fucking is not good, it can bring diseases.) Golinda ni tooi chɛ ka paɣa niŋ puwa. (Fucking can make a woman pregnant.)
Syn: goligu.  Koligu maa golinli chihiya, n tu ni n taɣi li. (The bag's handle broke, I need to replace it.) O zaŋ golinli n gbibi koligu. (He used the handle to hold the bag.)
Biɛla maa ŋmɛri la golinli mɔɣu ni. (The child is playing with a long gourd in the bush.) Golinli maa yi lu, di nyɛɣira. (If the long gourd falls, it breaks.)

Soli maa mali golinma pam, di bi tuhi. (The path has many meanders, it's not straight.) Golinma maa zuɣu, ti bi tooi nya luɣushɛli ti ni chana. (Because of the meanders, we can't see where we're going.)
See: golinli.  Koligu maa goliŋga chihiya. (The bag's handle broke.) Goliŋga maa gbibi koligu maa. (The handle holds the bag.)
Gen: goli. See: palli. Sim: gol' paliga.  Piɛbimiya kikaa goli palli dali. (Blow the trumpet on new moon day.) Golipalli maa dali, ti pili chira palli. (On new moon day, we begin the new month.)

english: to strut along, show off; to decorate, embellish
example: Syn: gam, viili.  O golisiri la o chandi ni kamani o nyɛla toondana. (He struts along like he is a leader.) Bɛ golisila duu maa ni nɛma viɛla. (They decorated the room with beautiful things.)

Colloc: gom gbaai X, gom mali X.  Gom gbaagi o, o kpe duu ni n ti gbihi. (Sleep caught him, he entered the room to sleep.) Ŋun guli bihi maa pala gom ni gbaari so. (The one watching the children doesn't fall asleep.) Gom mali ma, n yɛn chaŋ n gbihi. (I'm sleepy, I'm going to sleep.) Gom beni? (response to morning greeting - "Is there sleep?" i.e., "Did you sleep well?")
Gomachɛma tia maa be la mɔɣu ni, di mali gɔhi pam. (The Mimosa tree is in the bush, it has many thorns.) Gomachɛma wɔla yuuni kam. (The Mimosa tree fruits every year.)
Gomancheen tia maa waɣila, di dɔri viɛla mɛbu ni. (The acacia tree is tall, its wood is good for building.) Bɛ zaŋdi gomancheen dɔri n mɛri yiya. (They use acacia wood to build houses.)
O labi la gombo n wuɣiri chinchini viɛnyɛla. (He throws the shuttle weaving cloth well.) Gombo maa chani yomyom, o naai yom. (The shuttle moves quickly, he finishes quickly.)
Gomli maa nyɛla kpɛma nayili ni, o sɔŋdi naa maa. (The Gomli is an elder at the palace, he helps the chief.) Bɛ boli Gomli na n ti tɔɣisi yɛltɔɣa. (They called the Gomli to discuss matters.)
From: (Eng.). Colloc: Simpa Gomna.  Gomna maa kana ti nya tiŋa maa. (The governor came to see the town.) Gomna maa kpaŋsiri la Simpa waa viɛnyɛla. (The leader encourages the simpa dance well.)

From: (Eng.). Colloc: gɔmanati tuntumda.  Gomnanti ti la pukpariba tim ni bindira. (The government gives farmers medicine and food.) Soli bɛni ni gɔmnanti mini ʒɛngama zuɣunima yina n ti sɔŋ ti. (There's opportunity for government and NGO leaders to come and help us.)

See: gomnanti.  O nyɛla gomnanti tumtumda, o tumdi la ofiisi ni Asibiri kam. (He is a civil servant, he works in an office every Saturday.) Gomnanti tumtumdiba pam be Tamale. (Many civil servants are in Tamale.)

Bɛ zaŋ gomparigu n pili gooni maa ka di taɣiri saa. (They used thatch covering for the wall to protect from rain.) Gomparigu maa kuui, ti tu ni ti taɣi li. (The thatch covering is old, we need to replace it.)

Sim: bintɔrigu, biŋgoo, yum.  O mali goo o naba ni, o ku tooi chaŋ. (He has a boil on his foot, he can't walk.) Goo maa puhi ya, o chaŋ asibiti n ti wuhi dɔɣite. (The abscess swelled, he went to hospital to show the doctor.)

Jɛrigu yi ŋahi ŋahili, di ŋmanila nir' ni nyu dam kuli ka bɔri ni o ŋooi goo o nuu ni la. (A fool quoting a proverb is like a drunk trying to remove a thorn.) A yi chɛ soli ka dari mɔɣu, goo yi ti kuhi a, a ni labina. (If you leave the path and wander, a thorn will make you return.) Yuɣimpini bili gɔhi n-tiligi kurili ku tiligi lɛbila. (The hedgehog grows quills to save itself from hand blows, but not from a club.)

Syn: gɔtia, akasia.  Suɣulolana n-duri goo ka goo niŋ gɔ' salli. (A patient person climbs a thorn tree and it becomes smooth.) Shinshingoorɔnim' tɛri bɛ tɛri gɔhi tihi zuɣu. (Storks build their nests on thorn trees.)

english: to restrain, stop; to tie something round
example: Sim: taɣi, kaɣili, gu.  N ni chaŋ nti gooi ba dama bɛ zabira. (I will go and separate them because they are fighting.) Dagbam' kpamba yɛliya ni niriba ayi yi zabira, a goori ba mi. (Dagomba elders say if two fight, you separate them.) A yi gooi ma ʒili, n ku di yi bindirigu. (Even if you detain me, I won't eat your food.) O zaŋ sariga n-gooi o shee. (She tied a veil around her waist.)
See: goonje.  Bɛ ŋmɛri goojinima salima ni. (They play fiddles at the festival.) Goojinima maa kum nyɛɣisa, niriba wumdi li. (The fiddles' sound is sweet, people hear it.)
See: goo; mia.  O zaŋ goomia n gbahi zahim. (He used a fishing line to catch fish.) Goomia maa chihiya, o tu ni o taɣi li. (The fishing line broke, he needs to replace it.)

See: gooni; -laa.  Bɛ daa duhila bɛ yee tahiri pampam, ka tiŋ' maa goondaa maa dii kabi ti lu tiŋa. (They shouted and the city wall broke and fell.) O ni ti kpe tiŋ' shɛŋa bɛ ni me goondahi gili ni. (He will enter walled cities.)
See: gooni; gɔŋ.  Goongɔŋ maa ni, bɛ ʒiya n guri saamba. (In the corner, they are sitting waiting for guests.) Goongɔŋ maa puuni, bɛ sɔɣi la liɣiri. (In the corner, they hid the money.)

Sim: dukpini, dikili. Colloc: gambee gooni.  A yi labi pololi gooni a bi lahi dol' o. (If you throw a toad over a wall, you don't go to see where it falls.) N daa lihi nya ka voli be gooni ni. (I saw a hole in the wall.) Gbungbaŋ nyɛlim gooni ku du tia. (Reed grass hangs over the wall but can't climb a tree.)

Sim: biɛɣu, mɔɣilo. Sit: zaabia.  Ŋmɛmiya goonjinim' ka piɛbi yuhi salima ni. (Play fiddles and blow whistles at the festival.) Di simdi ni ti ŋme goonji mini mɔɣilo. (It's good to play fiddle and harp.) N naan chɛbis' a ni suhupiɛlli, ka ŋme goonjinim' ka chaɣisi sayɛya. (I would farewell you joyfully, playing fiddles and rattles.)
See: goonje; ŋma. Syn: goonje.  Goonjɛŋma maa ŋmɛri la goonje viɛnyɛla. (The fiddle-player plays the fiddle well.) Goonjɛŋmariba maa zaa mali baŋsim. (All the fiddle-players have skill.)
See: goonje.  O ŋmɛri la goonji. (He is playing the fiddle.) Goonji maa kum nyɛɣisa. (The fiddle's sound is sweet.)
See: gooni.  Bɛ zaŋ goonpariga n pahi gooni maa zuɣu ka di viɛla. (They put wall-plates on top of the wall to make it beautiful.) Goonparisi maa viɛla pam. (The wall-plates are very beautiful.)
See: goongɔŋ.  Gooŋgɔŋ maa ni, bɛ ʒiya n diɛmda. (In the corner, they are sitting playing.) O sɔɣi la gooŋgɔŋ ni. (He hid in the corner.)
From: (Eng.). Colloc: grɛp tihi. Syn: wain tiwalli.  O ko la grɛp tihi o pɔŋ ni. (He planted grapevines in his farm.) Grɛp wala maa nyɛɣisa pam. (The grapes are very sweet.)

O ni kpaai tulaale ŋɔ niŋ ma maa, o niŋ li mi gu n sɔɣibu. (She poured perfume on me to prepare for my burial.) Maalim shili n-gu o. (Get ready and wait for him.) O ʒiya n gu o zo. (He is sitting waiting for his friend.) Colloc: maali shili gu. Syn: guhi, guli, yuli.

english: to obstruct, block; to protect against
