example: Colloc: gu soli. Sim: ŋari, taɣi, gooi.  Di gu ti soli, ti bɔri ni ti gari. (Don't block our way, we want to pass.) O gu omaŋa saa ni ni kɔbiko. (He protected himself from rain with an umbrella.) Jɛrɔbɔam daa chɛ ka tɔbbi' shɛb' lɔɣi gili la Judanim' maa nyaaŋa nti sɔɣi dɔni gu ba. (Jeroboam set an ambush to wait for the Judahites.)
See: gɔva.  O diri la guabe. (He is eating guava.) Guabe maa nyɛɣisa. (The guava is sweet.)

Serial: gubi kpuɣi. Syn: laɣim.  Bɛ zaa gubi n kpuɣi dakuɣu maa. (They all united to lift the log.) Daŋ buni gubi ʒira ku ʒi nin' yino. (Family wealth is carried together, not by one.) A ni bɔri dokuɣu shɛli, di salinsahi n-gubiri a. (The ants on the log you want unite against you.)
See: gubi.  Bɛ gubili maa zuɣu, bɛ naai tuma maa yomyom. (Because they came together, they finished the work quickly.) Gubili viɛla tuma ni. (Unity is good in work.)
Sim: doli, guliŋguɣu, jambɔŋ.  O gbibi la guɣu o nuu ni n chaŋ mɔɣu. (He held a club in his hand going to the bush.) Guɣu maa nyɛla dɔɣu din tibsa. (The club is a heavy piece of wood.)

english: to pluck a fowl; to lose hair
example: O guhi la noo maa ka yihi di kɔbiri. (He plucked the fowl and removed its feathers.) Di chɛ ka bɛ guhir' a ka a yiɣira. (Don't let them pluck your wings when you're trying to fly.) Doo zabiri yi guhi, o nyɛla ŋun mali zuɣupalo. (If a man loses hair, he is bald.)

english: to wait for; to delay; to become poor
example: Colloc: guhi je. Syn: gu, guli, yuli. Caus: guligi.  Yin guhimi kpɛ n-lihira. (You wait here and watch.) Ti guhim' ba biɛla ka bɛ paa' na. (Let's wait a bit for them to arrive.) Subee daa bi garigi Shitobu shɛli hali ka Shitobu ti guhi o je. (Subee gave no answer until Shitobu waited in vain.) N ku guhi, n ni niŋ yomyom. (I won't delay, I'll act quickly.)
Sim: lu.  Ŋmaɣu maa guhigiya, di bi tooi kpe. (The arrow fell down, it didn't penetrate.) O ni labi kuɣili maa, di guhigiya. (The stone he threw fell down.)
Sim: paɣili, gulisigu.  Guhigu be yaŋgbani ni asiba. (Fog is in the air in the morning.) Guhigu zuɣu, ti bi tooi nya katiŋa. (Because of the fog, we can't see far.)

english: to bend head, nod; to walk stoopingly; to steal away furtively
example: Colloc: guhim zuɣu. Syn: baɣisi, momsi, puɣisi.  A yi baŋ a zo a guhindila a zuɣu. (If you understand your friend, you nod.) A yi bi zura, a bi guhinda. (If you're not a thief, you don't walk stoopingly.) A yi bi zura ka guhimda a nyɛla tayiɣa. (If you're not a thief but sneak around, you're still a thief.)

Colloc: dansarika gula. Sim: waasimani.  O nyɛla gula n-ti niɣi maa. (He is a herdsman for the cows.) Kum ni nyɛ bɛ gula. (Death will be their shepherd.) Bɛ piigi o la gula n-ti tiŋa maa. (They appointed him as watchman for the town.)
Gulanaa maa nyɛla kpɛma nayili ni. (The Gulanaa is an elder at the palace.) Bɛ boli Gulanaa na n ti tɔɣisi yɛltɔɣa. (They called the Gulanaa to discuss matters.)
Sim: chɛrili.  Guli maa dimi o ka o kuhi. (The black ant bit him and he cried.) Guya pam be mɔɣu ni. (Many black ants are in the bush.)

Etym: OVN *GU-DI/A. Colloc: kpari guli.  O da la guli pam daa ni. (He bought a lot of kola nuts in the market.) Guli yuurila bɛn tibigi li noli ni. (Kola nuts last longer in the mouth of those who value them.) Doo ŋɔ jɛmdi la paɣa ŋɔ niriba ni liɣiri mini guli. (The man pays attentions with money and kola.)

english: to look after, keep watch; to wait for
example: Syn: yuli, guhi, gu. Caus: guligi.  O gulila o ma biŋkɔbiri. (He looks after his mother's animals.) Ti yi guli binkɔbiri yinsi ka bi maani bɛ bɛhigu shee maa, bɛ bina maa ni wɔɣi zɔhi pam. (If we keep animals and don't clean their places, their dung breeds flies.) O ʒi n-gul' o. (He sat and waited for him.)
See: gula.  Guliba maa guli la nayili maa. (The guards guard the palace.) Guliba maa zaa gbibi la bidibbina. (All the guards hold weapons.)
InvCaus: gu, guhi, guli.  N zaŋ la so guligi n ti o. (I entrusted it to someone.) O guligi o bihi maa n-ti o ma. (He entrusted his children to his mother.)
Sim: paɣili, guhigu, gulisigu.  Duu maa ni nyɛla guliguli dama bɛ nyo la buɣim. (The room is smoky because they made a fire.) Guliguli be yaɣili maa. (It's misty over there.)
Gulilu maa be kom ni. (The tadpole is in the water.) O zaŋ gulilu n niŋ gali maa ni. (She put the thread on a reel.)

Sim: sambani. Colloc: gulinyaaŋa polo 'north'.  O ʒiya gulinyaaŋa n vuhira. (He is sitting in the back yard resting.) Di simdi ka a yiŋ' sambani ni mini gulinyaaŋa zaa mali m-be nyam. (It's necessary that your front and back yards are kept clean.) Gulinyaaŋa polo nyɛla maasim. (The north is cool.)
Sim: chɛrili.  Cham' ti nya guliŋguhi biɛhigu. (Go and see how the ants live.) Guliŋgua maa ʒiya tia gbinni. (The ant is under the tree.)
Sim: doli, guɣu.  O gbibi la guliŋguɣu o nuu ni. (He held a club in his hand.) Guliŋguɣu maa nyɛla dɔɣu kpɛma. (The club is a big stick.)
Sim: paɣili, guhigu.  Buɣim mini sakuɣa ni gulisigu. (Fire, hail and vapour.) O chɛ ka gulisigu be kaman taŋkpaɣu la n-gili luɣili kam. (He scatters frost like ashes all around.)
See: guli.  Guliyili maa waɣila. (The ant-hill is tall.) Guliyiya pam be mɔɣu ni. (Many ant-hills are in the bush.)
See: guli; biŋgulli.  O niŋdi la gulli, o mali niɣi mini buhi. (He does animal rearing, he has cows and goats.) Gulli nyɛla tuma din mali nyɔri. (Animal rearing is profitable.)
From: (Eng.).  O zaŋ gulu n di tabili. (He used glue to stick it.) Gulu maa gbibi li viɛnyɛla. (The glue holds it well.)
See: gulilu.  Gululu maa be kom ni. (The tadpole is in the water.) Gululunima pam be kuliga maa ni. (Many tadpoles are in the well.)
See: guliŋgua.  Guluŋgua maa ʒiya tia zuɣu. (The ant is on the tree.) Guluŋgua maa gbiri la bɔɣili. (The ant is digging a hole.)
See: guliŋguɣu.  O gbibi la guluŋguɣu. (He holds a club.) Guluŋguɣu maa nyɛla dɔɣu kpɛma. (The club is a big stick.)
Guluŋguŋ maa be la mɔɣu ni. (The Nauclea shrub is in the bush.) Bɛ zaŋdi guluŋguŋ vari n tibiri dɔriti. (They use its leaves for medicine.)

Aliguufulana baa gbaarila gumachuɣu. (A dishonest person's dog catches a chameleon.) Gumachuɣu yi wari waa ka dɔɣi o bia, o gba zaŋdila waa yiɣisi zani. (If a chameleon dances giving birth, the child will also jump up and dance.)
See: gumachuɣu; zuli.  Bɛ zaŋ gumachuɣuzuli n wuɣiri gbala. (They use gumachuɣuzuli grass to weave mats.) Gumachuɣuzuli maa zooi pam. (The grass grows a lot.)

english: kapok seeds; soup from kapok seeds
example: See: guŋa. Sim: guŋkaa.  Bɛ duɣiri la gumbɔɣu ʒɛri n-tooi li tari tiŋ ni kpamba. (They cook kapok-seed soup and throw it for the elders.) Guŋa wɔndi la guŋguma ka bɛ puɣiri guŋguma maa n-yihiri gumdi ka chɛ gumbɔɣu. (The kapok tree has pods, they take out the kapok and leave the seeds.)
N yi daɣiri ni, ka paligi laɣilaɣi gari gumbɔrigu. (I come out of dirt and become whiter than a cotton-boll.) Gumbɔra maa be guŋa maa zuɣu. (The cotton-bolls are on the kapok tree.)

See: gumʒim, guntɔbu, gummira.  Di bi tumda, di mi bi miri gumdi. (They don't work or spin cotton.) Bɛ yi bo la gumdi ŋɔ n-yɛbisi li m-bu li ka naanyi zaŋ gummua m-milisili. (They get cotton, disentangle it, beat it, then use a distaff to spin it.) Ninvuɣ' shɛb' bɛn mali yaa ni ŋmanila gumdi, ka bɛ tuma be kaman chɛbili la. (The mighty become tinder, their work a spark.)
InvSit: wahu.  O zaŋ gumiika n lo o wahu maa. (He put a martingale on his horse.) Gumiika maa gbibi wahu maa. (The martingale holds the horse.)

See: gumdi; mi.  Gummiri' nyɛ la ninvuɣi so ŋun maani gumdi n-tiri kpalua. (A cotton-spinner makes yarn for the weaver.) Gummira maa mi gumdi milibu viɛnyɛla. (The spinner knows how to spin cotton well.)
See: gumdi.  O zaŋ gummiriga n yɛbisi gumdi maa. (She used a gin to disentangle the cotton.) Gummiriga maa nyɛla dɔɣu. (The gin is made of wood.)

See: gumdi. Sim: gummiriga.  O gbibila gummua o nuu ni, ka mali o nubihi wahiri jɛni. (She holds the distaff and spins the spindle with her fingers.) Bɛ yi bo la gumdi ŋɔ n-yɛbisi li m-bu li ka naanyi zaŋ gummua m-milisili. (They get cotton, disentangle it, beat it, then use a distaff to spin it.)
See: guŋa; guŋgumli; gumbɔɣu.  O zaŋ gumpanyaaŋa n duɣi ʒɛri. (She used kapok leaves to cook soup.) Gumpanyaaŋa ʒɛri maa nyɛɣisa. (The kapok-leaf soup is tasty.)
See: gumdi; tari. Syn: jɛntarigu.  O zaŋ gumdi n niŋ gumtarigu ni. (She put cotton in the receptacle.) Gumtarigu maa pali gumdi. (The receptacle is full of cotton.)
See: gumdi; ʒim.  O zaŋ gumʒim n pɔbi o daŋa. (He used cotton-wool to bandage his wound.) Gumʒim maa be asibiti ni. (Cotton-wool is in the hospital.)

english: piled up, in heaps, lofty, towering
example: Bɛ laɣim la binyɛra gunaguna. (They piled things up in heaps.) Tihi maa zoya gunaguna. (The trees are towering.)
See: guŋgombila.  Bɛ ŋmɛri la gungombihi salima ni. (They play tambourines at the festival.) Gungombihi maa kum nyɛɣisa. (The tambourines' sound is sweet.)

Bɛ daa kana ni bɛ ti guni bii maa. (They came to circumcise the child.) Wanzam maa guni la nachimbila maa. (The circumciser circumcised the youth.) Filistianim' bɛn bi gundi ŋɔ. (These uncircumcised Philistines.)
Syn: jɛntarigu. See: gumdi; tari.  O zaŋ gumdi n niŋ guntarigu ni. (She put cotton in the calabash.) Guntarigu maa be o nyaaŋa. (The calabash is behind her.)
See: gumdi; tɔbu.  O zaŋ guntɔbu n tɔbi gumdi. (He used a bow to card cotton.) Guntɔbu maa nyɛla dɔɣu ni mia. (The bow is made of wood and string.)
Colloc: che gunu, maali gunu.  Bɛ daa che gunu ka naai. (They consulted and finished.) O maali gunu ni o zo. (He made an agreement with his friend.)
Sim: wanzam.  Na' so n-daa zaŋ wɔnzam so niŋ o yili gunu. (A chief got a circumciser as his barber.) Gunu maa su la sariya. (The barber judges.)

See: yɔɣu; guŋa. Sim: mɔɣu.  Dina n-nyɛ bɛ varisi o ka o guui nti sɔɣi gunyɔɣu ni. (Because they terrified him, he ran and hid in the kapok bush.) O chaŋ gunyɔɣu ni n ti kabi dari. (He went to the kapok bush to collect firewood.)

Etym: OV *GUM. Syn: kantɔŋ.  Naawuni n-zooi guŋa, gampiri' suma di jɛri suli. (God made the kapok grow; good ficus trees shouldn't be angry.) Bɛ sari la guŋa, amaa ka shɛŋa binda di tolitoli. (They plant kapok, but some grow on their own.) Guŋa wɔndi la guŋguma. (The kapok tree has pods.)
Bɔ m-be duu ni ka guŋgɔni bi mi? (What's in a room that the raised platform doesn't know?) O do la guŋgɔni zuɣu. (He sleeps on the raised platform.)
Colloc: kpe gungɔŋ.  Bɛ kpe la guŋgɔŋ kuli maa ni. (They did the gun-salute at the funeral.) Guŋgɔŋ maa ŋmɛbu wumya katiŋa. (The gun-salute was heard far away.)
Di zaa zaɣ' yiniyini daa naan tooi deei ko' guŋgɔna ayɔbuyɔbu. (Each one could hold six water-cans.) O zaŋ guŋgɔŋ n niŋ kpam. (He put oil in a metal drum.)

Colloc: vooi guŋgɔŋ. Part: chahiriga.  Bila biŋkumda n-nyɛ: guŋgɔŋ, yuhi, nti pahi lunsi zuɣu. (The Bila instruments: bass drum, flutes, hourglass drums.) Guŋgɔŋ kpiŋ sola nupuɣu. (A worthless drum is better than hand-clapping.) Tarimba yɛri guŋgɔna ka naa yɛli dini? (Poor people speak drums, what will the chief speak?)
See: guŋgɔŋ; lunkuɣra.  O zaŋ gbaŋ n pili guŋgɔŋkuɣra maa. (He put skin on the drum's seat.) Guŋgɔŋkuɣra maa gbibi gbaŋ maa. (The drum's seat holds the skin.)

See: guŋgɔŋ; -bila. Colloc: guŋgombila din mali chaɣisa.  Yilimiya yila ka ŋmɛm' guŋgombihi. (Sing songs and beat tambourines.) Bɛ daa gbibi gungombihi din mali chaɣisa. (They held tambourines with jingles.)
Syn: guŋguŋkpilli.  O ko la guŋgumbo o pɔŋ ni. (He planted guŋgumbo yams.) Guŋgumbo maa wɔla pam. (The guŋgumbo yams produced well.)

See: guŋa.  Guŋguma yi saazuɣu n-lu tiŋa na di ku lan labi. (If a kapok pod falls, it never goes back on the tree.) Guŋa wɔndi la guŋguma ka bɛ puɣiri guŋguma maa n-yihiri gumdi. (The kapok tree has pods, they open them to get kapok.)
See: gumdi; guŋa.  O zaŋ guŋgundi n niŋ difiɛli ni. (She put kapok in the pillow.) Guŋgundi maa bala pam. (The kapok is very soft.)
Guŋguni maa be kom ni. (The mound is in the swamp.) Bɛ ʒiya guŋguni zuɣu. (They are sitting on the mound.)
Sim: kikaa.  Guŋgunlɛrigu be yaɣili maa. (Harmattan haze is over there.) Guŋgunlɛrigu zuɣu, ti bi tooi nya katiŋa. (Because of the haze, we can't see far.)
See: guŋgumbo; guŋguŋkpilli; salli.  O ko la guŋgunsalli. (He planted guŋgunsalli yams.) Guŋgunsala maa wɔla pam. (The guŋgunsalli yams produced well.)
Syn: guŋgumbo. See: guŋgumbo; guŋgunsalli; kpilli.  O ko la guŋguŋkpilli o pɔŋ ni. (He planted guŋguŋkpilli yams.) Guŋguŋkpila maa nyɛɣisa. (The guŋguŋkpilli yams are tasty.)

See: guŋa. Sim: gumbɔɣu.  Guŋkaa nyaɣisim zuɣu chɛ ka Dagbamba ŋahiri di ŋahili. (Because of kapok-soup's taste, Dagomba have a proverb about it.) Guŋkaa bi mi ni biri kpam nyaɣisa. (Kapok-soup doesn't know that rosella stew is tasty.)
O zaŋ guŋkpani n tɔbi gumdi. (He used a cotton bar to gin cotton.) Guŋkpani maa nyɛla kuriti. (The cotton bar is made of iron.)
See: guli; ŋmani.  O da la guŋmani guli. (He bought a package of 00 kola nuts.) Guŋmani maa liɣiri nyɛla din galisi. (The price of a 00-kola package is high.)
See: guŋa; guŋgumli; gumbɔɣu.  O zaŋ guŋpanyaaŋa n duɣi ʒɛri. (She used kapok leaves to cook soup.) Guŋpanyaaŋa maa nyɛɣisa. (The kapok-leaf soup is tasty.)
O zaŋ guraaʒe n niŋ kooko maa ni. (She put lumps in the porridge.) Guraaʒe maa nyɛɣisa. (The lumps are tasty.)

english: to tie up in a bundle; to carry in bundle; to tighten bowstring; to fold limbs; to wedge
example: Bɛ zaŋdi bɛ tuumbiɛri maa guri yɛlimaŋli soli. (They suppress truth by their wickedness.) O gur' o naba n' o yiɣi. (He flexed his legs to jump.) Naawuni ni sah' o takɔbi; ka gur' o tɔbu. (God will sharpen his sword and string his bow.)

english: to untie, release bow or trap
example: Cpart: guri.  Dɔɣim kpanjɔɣu ku gurigi zino. (A stranger cannot open the family inheritance box.) O gurigi tɔbu maa. (He unstrung the bow.)
Colloc: gurigu lana. Sim: toozo.  Shee biɛrigu ni nam guriga. (Backache can cause a hunchback.) O mali gurigu o zuɣu. (He has a hump on his back.)
Sim: chɛrili.  Gurigurilo maa ʒiya tia zuɣu. (The ant is on the tree.) Gurigurilo pam be mɔɣu ni. (Many ants are in the bush.)

Daantaliga ʒiri yɔɣira ka ʒiri gurilo ka ʒiri mɔri. (The roof-pole carries the rafters, the grass plait, and the thatch grass.) Bɛ zaŋ gurilo n gbini yɔɣira maa. (They used grass plait to tie the rafters.)

Sim: sabili.  Gbaŋ ŋɔ tuma yi naai, o ni tooi zaŋ li she dufiɛli, gurim, bee sabili. (After tanning, leather can make a pouffe, talisman or amulet.) Gbanzabigu nyɛla ŋun shɛri gurima mini saba. (Leatherworkers sew talismans and amulets.)
O gurim ma ʒili. (He has made me wrinkled.) O binyɛrigu gurim ya. (His garment is wrinkled.) Buɣum bi n-gurim' o. (The fire shrank it.)
See: gurim.  O lo la gurima o shee ni. (He wore talismans on his waist.) Gurima maa gu o la barina ni. (The talismans protect him from harm.)
See: gurim.  O ninni nyɛla gurimagurima dama o kurigi. (His face is wrinkled because he is old.) Gbaŋ maa leei la gurimagurima. (The skin became wrinkled.)
See: gurim.  O gurimbu nyɛla din galisi. (His wrinkling is great.) Gbaŋ maa mali gurimbu. (The skin has wrinkles.)
O nyɛla Guringa, o yi la Bɔlga na. (He is a Frafra, he came from Bolga.) Gurunsi pam be Tamale. (Many Frafra people are in Tamale.)
O gurisiri la o chandi ni dama o naba biɛri o. (He walks slowly because his foot hurts.) O gurisi n kana yiŋa. (He came home slowly in pain.)
O mali gurugua pala. (He has a new small car.) Gurugua maa chaŋ yomyom. (The small car goes fast.)

See: -lana. Syn: gɔbili.  Do' so ŋun napɔŋ bee o nuu mali daŋa bee gurugulana bee diɣili. (A man with a wound or a hunchback or a dwarf.) A bi luhi gurugulana ka bɔri polilana vuri. (You haven't thrown a hunchback and want to fight a hernia person.)
Nact: wahu.  Wahu maa chani la guruma. (The horse is ambling.) Guruma nyɛla wahu chandi din nyɛɣisa. (Ambling is a comfortable horse gait.)

english: man from Upper Region; boy 'bought' to avert bad fate
example: See: Guringa; doo.  O nyɛla Gurundoo. (He is a Frafra man.) Bɛ zaŋ o leei Gurundoo n ti o. (They made him a 'bought' boy for him.)
See: Guringa; Guruŋ.  O yɛri la Gurunli. (He speaks Frafra.) Gurunli nyɛla balli din mali anfaani. (Frafra is a useful language.)

english: woman from Upper Region; girl 'bought' to avert bad fate
example: See: Guringa; paɣa.  O nyɛla Gurunpaɣa. (She is a Frafra woman.) Bɛ zaŋ o leei Gurunpaɣa n ti o. (They made her a 'bought' girl for her.)

english: land of the Grunsi people (U.E. Region)
example: Colloc: Guruŋ bua. See: Guringa; Gurunli.  Guruŋ' piɛɣu yi ʒi shɛli amaa o leei mi ŋun da o. (If a Gurunsi sheep doesn't know anything, it knows its buyer.) O chaŋ Guruŋ ni n ti nya o dɔɣiriba. (He went to Gurunsi land to see his relatives.)
O nyɛla ti' guruŋga. (It is a stunted tree.) Bia maa nyɛla guruŋga, o bi zooi viɛnyɛla. (The child is stunted, he doesn't grow well.)
See: guli; salli.  O diri la gusalli. (He is eating sticky kola nut.) Gusalli maa nyɛɣisa. (The sticky kola nut is sweet.)
See: guli; sɔrigu. InvNinst: so.  O zaŋ gusɔrigu n sɔri guli. (He used a grater to grate kola nuts.) Gusɔrigu maa nyɛla kuriti. (The grater is made of iron.)
See: guli.  O bahi la gutuɣiri maa. (He threw away the kola pulp.) Gutuɣiri maa be tiŋa. (The kola pulp is on the ground.)

Nabstr: gutulunsi.  Gutulu ku sooŋa, n-tam wɔbi' kura yɛla. (An ungrateful man killed a hare and forgot the elephant-sharer.) Gutulu ka simli. (A selfish person has no friend.) A mini nir' gutulu yi mira mia, mia chihuŋ nyɛ li. (If you make rope with an untrustworthy person, it's a broken rope.)
InvNabstr: gutulu.  O gutulunsi bi viɛla. (His ingratitude is not good.) Gutulunsi chɛ ka niriba je o. (Ingratitude makes people dislike him.)

english: to run away fast; to run fast
example: O guuya. (He ran away.) Sisɛra daa sheeg' o chɛchɛbuŋ' zuɣu na guui. (Sisera got down and ran.) A wɔhu yi guura a ni tooi kari gbaai pɔhim? (If your horse runs fast, can you catch the wind?)

english: to remain, be left; to leave over
example: Syn: che.  Zaɣ' yini guuya. (One is left.) Bɛ daa laɣisi bɔrobɔro chɛhi mini zimburisa din guui. (They collected leftover bread and fish.) O guuri shɛli n-kɔhira. (He is keeping something to sell.) Tomiya ba piɛma, di tɔnya ka guui peen' gaŋa. (Shoot, spare no arrows.)
Sim: baaji, bɔkati.  N dana, a ka guuka, ka kobiliga ŋɔ mi zilim pam. (Sir, you have no bucket and this well is deep.) O zaŋ guuka n ʒi kom. (He used a rubber bucket to carry water.)
See: guli; zuɣu.  O ti o la guzuɣu. (He gave him a large kola nut.) Guzuɣuri maa nyɛɣisa. (The large kola nuts are sweet.)
O zaŋ guzuli n niŋ o adiiku ni. (He put provisions in his travel bag.) Guzuli maa saɣiri o chandi maa ni. (The provisions will be enough for his journey.)

A yi paai, puhimi bɛn daŋ ŋɔ ha. (When you arrive, greet those who arrived there before you.) Di daa niŋ daba ayi ha ka o ti labi na. (After two days, he then returned.) Yi bilim ni ha ka o pun zooi lala. (Since your childhood, he has been like that.) Colloc: katiŋ' ha 'far away there'

english: to breathe out through the mouth
example: Cpart: fu.  O ha la pɔhim o noli ni. (He breathed out air through his mouth.) Ha pɔhim naai, ka fu pɔhim. (Breathe out, then breathe in.)
Haa! di chaŋ nimaani. (Boo! don't go there.) Bɛ niŋ o haa ka o zo. (They went boo! at him and he ran.)
Variant: haai, hayi. Sim: haya. From: (Eng.). See: haya.  O haayi la lɔri n ʒi di daabiligu. (He hired a lorry for his business.) Duu maa haayibu liɣiri nyɛla din galisi. (The room's rent is high.)
O yo la o hachi. (He paid his due.) Hachi nyɛla binshɛɣu din tu ni a yo. (A due is something you must pay.)
Colloc: niŋ hada. From: (Ha.).  O niŋ la hada ka tooi karim. (He memorized and was able to read.) Hada nyɛla din mali anfaani bɔhimbu ni. (Memory is useful in learning.)
From: (Ar.). See: yɛda.  N hadi o mi dama o nyɛla yɛlimaŋlilana. (I trust him because he is truthful.) Di hadi niriba ni yomyom. (Don't trust people too quickly.)
Haiya! a yɛn chaŋ ya? (Hey! are you going?) Haiya, n yɛn yɛli a yɛltɔɣa. (Now then, I will tell you a story.)
From: (Ha.) < (Ar.).  Haka ka ti tu ni ti niŋ. (This is how we should do it.) Di nyɛla haka, pa lala. (It is thus, not like that.)

english: very, very much; until, as far as; even; even though
example: Variant: hali. Colloc: hal wayo, hali ni zuŋɔ, zaŋ hal ni, hal ti yɛli ni bo m-bala, hal...gba, hal baabiɛla, hal...zaa yoli. From: (GNG) < (Ha.) < (Ar.).  O chaŋ hal Tamale. (He went as far as Tamale.) Di zaa ni ti wurim hal ka di kuɣ' yini gba ti ku lan pa di kpee zuɣu. (It will all be destroyed, even one stone will not remain on another.) Hal ŋɔɣu yi du n-tam di zuɣu, o ni daai li luhi. (Even if a fox climbs on it, he will push it down.) O daa bi biɛhim Naawuni daalikauli hal baabiɛla. (He didn't doubt the promise one bit.)

See: halli; 'biɛɣu. Colloc: yihi X halibiɛɣu. Sim: biɛm.  O halibiɛɣu zuɣu, niriba bi jɛri o. (Because of his bad character, people don't like him.) Yi halibiɛri zuɣu ka yɛla bi viɛla. (Because of your bad character, things are not good.)

See: -nima. Sim: barina(-lana), bibiee, dingalingam(-lana), kpandaalana, ninvuɣubiɛɣu, 'yariga, yɔŋa.  Halibiɛrinima maa bi tumdi tuun suma. (People of bad character don't do good works.) Bɛ nyɛla halibiɛrinima, yɛla bi viɛla bɛ sani. (They are people of bad character, things are not good with them.)
See: halli; kpɛma.  Halikpɛmanima maa bi chɛri taali. (Unforgiving people don't forgive offences.) Bɛ ni shɛb' ni nyɛla niŋkuunsinima mini halikpɛmanima. (Some of them are cruel and unforgiving.)
From: (Heb.). Sim: aleluyani.  Haliluya! ti di nasara. (Halleluya! we have won.) Bɛ yiini la haliluya yila. (They sing halleluya songs.)

english: to the farthest end; the highest tribunal; how far?
example: Colloc: chaŋ haliya. Sim: danigi, haya, katiŋa, laligi, vinvimni. See: hal; haya.  Di yi be haliya, o nina ni nya li. (However far it is, his eyes will see it.) Yisa yuli daa du n-chaŋ haliya. (The fame spread far and wide.) Haliya ka a yiŋa be? (How far is your home?)
See: haliya.  O chaŋ Haliya n ti nya Naa. (He went to Yendi to see the chief.) Haliya nyɛla Dagbɔŋ tinŋzuɣu. (Yendi is the capital of Dagbong.)

Colloc: yihi halli. Sim: biɛm, alizini, biɛhigu, fihiga. From: (Ha.) < (Ar.). See: halibiɛɣu; halibiɛrinima; halikpɛmanima; haliyoyanima.  O halli zuɣu, niriba zaa mi o. (Because of his character, everyone knows him.) A halli viɛla, a sɔŋdi niriba. (Your character is good, you help people.) O halli biɛɣu zuɣu, o mini so bi be. (Because of his bad character, he doesn't get along with anyone.)

Variant: ankali, haŋkali. Colloc: mali haŋkali, niŋ haŋkali, saɣim haŋkali. From: (Ha.) < (Ar.).  Niŋmi hankali a chandi ni. (Be careful on your journey.) O mali haŋkali pam, o bi damdi yomyom. (He is very cautious, he doesn't act quickly.) Di saɣim a haŋkali, ti ni tooi niŋ li. (Don't lose your calm, we can do it.)
Haŋ! a yɛri lala? (Huh! you're saying that?) Haŋ! n bi saɣi ti lala. (Huh! I don't agree with that.)
See: aŋko.  Haŋko biɛɣu n-nyɛ li. (It is a bad omen.) Haŋko viɛlli wuhirimi ni bindirigu yɛn zooi. (A good omen shows that food will be plentiful.)

english: prohibited thing, thing one has no right to
example: Variant: haramu. Colloc: di haram(u). From: (Ha.) < (Ar.).  Di nyɛla haram n-ti ti. (It is prohibited for us.) O di la haram, dinzuɣu bɛ gbaai o. (He embezzled, so they caught him.)
See: di.  Haramudira maa zu la liɣiri. (The embezzler stole the money.) Bɛ gbaai haramudiriba maa zaa. (They caught all the embezzlers.)
See: awa.  O ʒini ni hawa ata. (He sat for three hours.) Hawa pirigili n-nyɛ li. (It's half an hour.)
Sim: danigi, haliya, katiŋa, laligi, vinvimni. See: haliya.  O yiŋa be la haya. (His home is far away.) Haya ka o yiŋa be? (How far is his home?)
Haya! chaŋ tum a tuma. (Off you go! go and do your work.) Haya! di kpalim. (Off you go! don't stay.)
Variant: haaya. Colloc: niŋ haya. From: (Eng.). See: haayi.  O yo la haya n-ti lɔri maa. (He paid rent for the lorry.) Haya maa galisiya. (The rent is high.)
See: -lana.  Hayalana maa yo la haya goli kam. (The tenant pays rent every month.) Hayalanima maa be duu maa ni. (The tenants are in the room.)
Hee! a yuli booni bɔ? (Hoy! what is your name?) Hee! kam na. (Hoy! come here.)
Variant: hee.  Heeja! n bi lahi mali liɣiri. (That's your lot! I have no more money.) Heeja! di niŋ lala. (Whoops! don't do that.)
From: (Eng.) < (Ar.). Syn: zabila. Sim: chilo, chilitɔŋ, chilinchihi.  O zaŋ hɛnna n niŋ o nina ni. (She put henna on her eyes.) Hɛnna nyɛla din mali viɛlim. (Henna is beautiful.)
Colloc: n-hii!  Hii! a kana. (Aha! you've come.) N-hii! pumpɔŋɔ a yipa kahigiri yɛri ti. (Aha! now you are speaking plainly.)
Variant: m, i. Etym: Mampruli hiŋ'.  Hin'! deemi li. (Here you are! take it.) O yɛli hin' ka o deei. (He said 'Get!' and he took it.)

Variant: Yibilisi. Syn: Yɔhinda, Sintani. Sim: yibilisi. From: (Ha.) < (Ar.).  Ibilisi nyɛla ŋun bɔri ni niriba tum tuumbiɛri. (The Devil wants people to do evil.) Yibilisi du o, o ku tooi ʒini. (Madness has come upon him, he can't sit still.)

Variant: aguunadam. Syn: adaam. Sim: daadam, ninsala, sala. From: (Ha.) < (Ar.) < (Heb.).  Igunaadam kam mali o biɛhigu. (Every human being has their own life.) Ti zaa nyɛla igunaadamnima. (We are all human beings.)
Variant: ii. Cpart: aai. Sim: mm.  Iin, n ni chaŋ. (Yes, I will go.) Chɛliya ka yi "Iin" kul nyɛla "Iin", ka yi "Aai" mi kul nyɛla "Aai". (Let your "Yes" be just "Yes" and your "No" just "No".)

Variant: immaani, yimaani. Colloc: mali imaani, ka imaani. Sim: naani, yɛda. From: (Ha.) < (Ar.).  O mali imaani pam, o diri la alikauli. (He has great faith, he keeps promises.) Imaani zuɣu, o tooi gbaai saawara. (Because of faith, he can make plans.)
Inhii! n di nasara. (Hah! I won.) Inhii! a bi tooi niŋ shɛli. (Aha! you can't do anything.)
Sim: ashaala. From: (Ar.).  Inshaala! di viɛla pam. (Golly! it's very good.) Inshaala o ni kana. (Hopefully he will come - not knowing for sure.)

Syn: baɣa, baɣibuɣira, bihiŋmɛra, bɔha, bukpaha, dodihira, jinwara, laasabuniŋda, takatibinima. From: (Ha.) < (Ar.).  O chaŋ ti bɔhi isaabi. (He went to consult divination.) Isaabi nyɛla din tooi yɛli yɛla. (Divination can tell things.)
Colloc: isinia lana. From: (Ar.).  Bɛ yiini la isinia jiŋli ni. (They sing Islamic praise songs in the mosque.) Isinia lana maa mali yɛm. (The song-leader has wisdom.)
Variant: je. Colloc: ja lana. Sim: to.  O ja la kuɣa ka leei tandi. (He crushed stones into sand.) Di zaŋya bɛ ni ja biŋkɔb' so lana. (Don't use a castrated animal.)

english: twin; pair; wooden idols representing twin spirits
example: Colloc: X jabil' kpee. Syn: libili. Sim: jabila. See: jadɔɣu; samanjaa.  Tomas ŋun daa nyɛ jaa la. (Thomas who was the twin.) Ribɛka ni daa zaŋ jahi pua ti yab' Yisahaku sani. (Rebecca was pregnant with twins by Isaac.) Paɣi' so ŋun dɔɣiri jahi. (A woman who gives birth to twins.) Zaɣ' yini kam malila jaa. (Each one has a twin.)

english: to become lazy, bad, spoilt; to become sterile (earth); to lose water content (yam)
example: Sim: anyinsi, gbinyaɣili, vinyaɣili, wawuluŋ, zinyaɣili. Syn: chaɣisi.  O jaai ya, o bi lahi bɔri tuma. (He has become lazy, he no longer wants work.) Tiŋa maa jaai ya, di bi lahi wali. (The earth has become sterile, it no longer produces.) Nyuya maa jaai ya. (The yams have lost water content.)
Gen: di.  O jaai la bindirigu pam. (He ate plenty of food.) Kum ni, a ku tooi jaai. (In famine, you can't eat plenty.)
From: (Eng.).  O zaŋ jaaki n duhi lɔri maa. (He used a jack to lift the lorry.) Jaaki maa chihiya. (The jack broke.)
Jaalambɔɣu yi bi so so o sola binajaɣirigu. (If the jaalambɔɣu beetle is not better than anyone, it is better than the dung beetle.)
Jaamani maa yi la Germany na. (The German came from Germany.) O yɛri la Jaamani. (He speaks German.)

Variant: jaandɛ, jande. Colloc: kpam...jaande. Sim: n suhir'a.  Jaande, ti kpamdi a jaande. (Please, we beg you.) Ti kpamdi ti mabihi bɛn nyɛ pukpariba la jaande, ni bɛ kpaŋmi bɛ maŋa. (We urge our brethren who are farmers to be careful.)

Variant: jaangbee, jangbee, jaŋgbee, dangbee, daaŋgbee. Colloc: nam jaaŋgbee, baɣa jaaŋgbee. Sim: baɣigbee. Gen: dɔɣu.  O zaŋ o ni gbib' jaaŋgbɛ' shɛl' o nuu ni nyol' ni shihi nimdi maa. (He touched the meat with the tip of the staff he was holding.) Aduna jaaŋgbee maa daa zaŋ bɛ jaaŋgbɛhi maa vali. (Aaron's walkingstick swallowed up their sticks.) Dangbee din she sima bi lan zɔri sabilim. (The stick used for roasting groundnuts no longer fears being black.) Wuntia jaaŋgbee n-nyɛ din wuhiri ni a nam fukumsi tam la wuntia zuɣu. (A sceptre of righteousness shows your kingship stands on righteousness.)
O ti o la jaara. (He gave him an extra amount.) Jaara maa nyɛla biɛla. (The extra amount is small.)
O zaŋ jabiga n ʒi kom. (She used a holed calabash with string to carry water.) Jabiga maa be duu ni. (The calabash is in the room.)

english: twin, child who is a twin
example: Sim: jaa. See: jaa; -bila.  Bɛ dɔɣi la jabila. (They gave birth to twins.) O daa gbaala o jabil' kpee napɔŋkpaa. (He grabbed his twin brother's heel.)

english: old, unattractive yam seed; old unattractive person
example: O zaŋ jabiluŋ n ko. (He planted an old yam seed.) Jabiluŋ maa bi lahi mali yaa. (The old yam seed no longer has strength.) O nyɛla jabiluŋ, o bi lahi viɛla. (She is old and unattractive.)
See: jaa; dɔɣu.  Bɛ zaŋ jadɔɣu n jɛm jahi maa. (They used carved sticks to worship the twins.) Jadɔɣu maa be buɣili ni. (The twin-sticks are in the shrine.)

english: pot for sacrifice to deceased twins
example: See: jaa; duɣu.  Bɛ zaŋ jaduɣu n mali sara n-ti jahi maa. (They used the pot to sacrifice to the twins.) Jaduɣu maa be buɣili ni. (The twin-pot is in the shrine.)

english: to criticize, talk about sb. (in absence)
example: Bɛ jaɣiri la o dama o ka ni. (They are talking about him because he is not there.) Bɛn kaɣikaɣi ka bɛ jaɣi. (Those who are absent are the ones people talk about.)

english: to cut something with the hand (soft substance)
example: Sim: jaɣiri. See: binajaɣirigu.  O jaɣi la bindirigu maa. (He cut the soft food with his hand.) Binajaɣirigu ʒia n-guli bindilana ni o nye ka o jaɣi. (The dung beetle waited for the person to defecate so he could cut it.)

Variant: jaɣali. See: jakabili; jaɣazuŋ.  Jaɣa gbaagi o, o chaŋ asibiti. (Yaws caught him, he went to hospital.) Jaɣalana lu kambɔŋa ni, jaɣa zuŋ ka ya ŋɔ beni. (The yaws patient fell into the granary, and no one immune was there to help.)
Syn: dagumahili.  Jaɣimɛlitali bi viɛla. (Prostitution is not good.) O yi chaŋ jaɣimɛlitali ni, o ni tooi gbaai dɔriti. (If she goes into prostitution, she can catch diseases.)
Jaɣimɛlo maa ʒiya mɔɣuni. (The prostitute is at the lorry station.) Bɛ gbaai jaɣimɛlo maa. (They caught the prostitute.)

english: to cut unevenly/roughly; to weed badly
example: Sim: jaɣi.  O jaɣiri la bindirigu maa. (He cut the food roughly.) O jaɣiri la puu maa, di bi viɛla. (He is weeding the farm badly, it's not good.)
See: zahinama.  Jahanama nyɛla buɣim shee. (Hell is a place of fire.) Alahichinima ni ti kpe jahanama. (Sinners will enter hell.)

english: to deteriorate, terminate; to spoil completely
example: Tuma maa jahi ya. (The work has deteriorated.) Birigu zomiya; yɛla jahiya. (The deaf-mute is blind; everything is completely spoiled.)
See: jaa; bua.  Bɛ zaŋ jahibua n ti jahi maa. (They gave a scapegoat to the twins.) Jahibua maa kɔrigiya. (The scapegoat has been slaughtered.)

english: title for traditional Dagomba army officer
example: From: (Twi).  Jahinfo maa nyɛla toondana tobu ni. (The Jahinfo is a leader in war.) Bɛ boli jahinfo maa na. (They called the army officer.)
Variant: jaɣikabili. See: jaɣili.  Jakabili gbaagi o, o ku tooi chaŋ. (Yaws of the feet caught him, he can't walk.) Jakaba maa nyɛli o pam. (The foot yaws hurt him a lot.)
Variant: jakpuŋkpuŋa.  Jakpiŋkpiŋ' lɔɣiri biɛma. (The puffer fish sneaks along the ponds.) O gbaai la jakpiŋkpiŋa mɔɣili ni. (He caught a puffer fish in the dam.)

Variant: jahinjee, jahinjahi, jahinjɛya.  Jalinjee maa mɛri la o tɛɣu tia zuɣu. (The weaver-bird builds its nest on the tree.) Jahinjɛya ayi bi kɔhirila siidi yini? (Don't two sparrows sell for one cedi?)

english: to be frisky, gambol; to be stirred, excited
example: Colloc: yiɣi jam. Sim: nyam.  Bihi maa yiɣiri jamda mɔɣu ni. (The children are jumping and gambolling in the bush.) Ka niriba zaa jam. (And all the people were stirred up.) Bɔm tuma n-ti a maŋa, kaman diɛma ni yiɣi jam tuma. (Find something to do, like entertainments and exciting stuff.)

english: piece of stick; throwing-stick, club, cudgel
example: Variant: jaambɔŋgu. Sim: doli, guɣu, guliŋguɣu, guluŋguɣu, kulinduli, lɛbila, mimarili. Colloc: kaburi jambɔŋ.  O gbibi la jambɔŋ o nuu ni. (He held a club in his hand.) Saazuɣu jambɔŋ kan' na, sokam taɣirila o zuɣu ni. (A club thrown in the air is falling; everyone protects their head.) O bi zaŋdi kaburi jaambon' titali buri buŋgu. (He doesn't use a big flail to thresh sesame.)
See: jaŋa; birigu.  Jambirigu maa be tia zuɣu. (The small red monkey is on the tree.) Jambirigu ŋubiri la bindira. (The monkey is eating food.)
See: -lɔɣu.  Jandɔɣu maa mali yaa pam. (The male monkey is very strong.) Jandɔɣu maa guli la o bihi. (The male monkey guards his young.)
See: jaaŋgbee.  O zaŋ jangbee n sɔŋ o chandi. (He used a walkingstick to help him walk.) Jangbee maa nyɛla dɔɣu. (The walkingstick is made of wood.)

Variant: jɛŋa. Syn: ŋmaaŋa. Sim: jɛŋnookuriga, kulibɔŋjaŋa, ŋmansuliga.  Jaŋa lari juɣu zuɣu polo ka ŋun jaŋ' gbina fa? (The monkey laughs at the vulture's bald head, but what about his own buttocks?) A yi tibi jaŋa nyinvɔɣu, o malimi n-ŋubiri a kariwana. (If you cure a monkey's toothache, he uses it to eat your corn.) Kiŋkansi zuɣu ka jaŋa gbɛri tia zuɣu. (Because of violence, the monkey sleeps in the tree.)

SynR: gbuɣimbila. See: -bila.  Jaŋgbimbila maa ŋmɛri la diɛma. (The lion-cub is playing.) Bɛ kul taami kaman bɛ ni yi gbaai jaŋgbimbil' ka chɛ o ma mɔɣu ni ka o taai shɛm la. (They are fierce like a lioness whose cub is taken.)

Syn: gbuɣinli, gbuŋgburugu, yɔnaa, zuɣigbilli.  Bɛ yi gbaai jaŋgbin' nyaŋ bia ka chɛ o ka o chirig' a. (If they take a mother lion's cub and she meets you.) Jaŋgbina mini kunduna gɔbu shee. (A place where lions and hyenas roam.)
Jaŋkumbuŋ maa be mɔɣu ni. (The palm civet is in the bush.) Jaŋkumbuŋ nyɛla biŋkɔbigu. (The palm civet is an animal.)
Liɣiri ŋɔ nyɛla japaa. (This money is fake.) Japaa maa bi viɛla. (The counterfeit is not good.)
Sim: ko, yɛbisi.  O jari la o puu. (He ploughed his farm.) Jarimiya yi kɔbiti din bi jari ŋɔ. (Plough your unploughed farms.)
Variant: jasheenima. Sim: pipia, kparichia. From: (Twi).  O zaŋ jashee n nyu kom. (He used a glass to drink water.) Jashee maa yi lu, di nyɛɣira. (If the glass falls, it breaks.)
From: (Eng.) < (Grk.) < (Heb).  O lo la jasipa o nuu ni. (She wore jasper on her finger.) Jasipa nyɛla kuɣ' viɛlli. (Jasper is a beautiful stone.)
Sim: jeeŋa, nansanjɛrigu.  Jatolɛlle maa be kom ni. (The crab is in the water.) O gbaai la jatolɛlle. (He caught a crab.)

Colloc: Jɔɔdɛn mɔɣili.  Yisa daa yi Nazarɛt Galili tiŋgbɔŋ ni ka Jɔn Baptaizira sanna ka Jɔn ti baptaiz' o Jɔɔdɛn mɔɣili ni. (Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.) Jɔɔdɛn mɔɣili maa waɣila. (The River Jordan is long.)

Colloc: sɔɣi jɔɣu ni.  Yihimi a nuhi a jɔɣu ni. (Take your hands out of your lap.) Buŋ' maa ni daa nya Yawɛ malaika maa, ka o teei dɔni Baalam jɔɣu ni. (When the donkey saw the angel, it lay down under Balaam.) Noo cheerila o bihi niŋdi o jɔɣu ni. (The hen pecks its young to put them under its wings.) Din nyɛ n zaŋ n tuumbiɛri sɔɣi n jɔɣu ni. (That I should hide my bad deeds in my lap.)

english: to limp on the toes; to make a start prematurely
example: Colloc: jɔhi chaŋ. Sim: kpahi, juhi, daŋ. See: dajɔha.  Bindi pa goo amaa a yi no li, a ni jɔhi. (Shit is not a thorn, but if you step on it, you'll limp.) O jɔhi chaŋ ka ti na bi paai. (He started early before we arrived.)
Colloc: to jɔŋ. InvNabstr: 'kahili.  Jɛŋkuno ka yiŋa ka jɛŋgbarisi tɔri jɔŋ. (When the cat's away, the mice play.) Jɔŋ maa wumiya salima ni. (The liveliness is heard at the festival.)
O je la noo maa pɔi ka di kpi. (He slaughtered the fowl just before it died.) Jeebu maa niŋ ya. (The slaughtering has been done.)

english: to be weary, tired; to do sth. in vain
example: Variant: ja. Serial: wolim je. Sim: baɣi, gbarigi, gbari, ŋɔhigi, wum, kabisi, viɛrigi, bilisi, kɔŋ, tarigi, ziŋ. Ant: tooi. See: jabigi; jɛligi.  O jɛya zaa. (He is completely exhausted.) N baɣiri jɛya. (My arms are tired.) Ka o bɔ li je. (And he looked for it in vain.) Subee daa bi garigi Shitobu shɛli hali ka Shitobu ti guhi o je. (Subee gave no reply until Shitobu got tired of waiting.) K' o wɔlim wɔlim n je. (And he tried repeatedly, but in vain.)

Colloc: je suli, je...maŋa. Sim: chihi, dima, zaɣisi.  N je ni n nini nya bia ŋɔ bahibu. (I can't bear to see this child die.) Akai! N je lala. (Hey! I don't like that.) A dataa nye ma k' a je ma lala? (Am I your wife's lover that you hate me like that?) Tab' jɛlinsi nyɛla din tahiri zabili na. (Mutual hatred brings quarrels.) Sal' je suli ku zaɣisi dibu. (A person who is angry will still eat.)
O lo la jee o nuu ni. (She wore a straw bangle on her wrist.) Kulikarijee bi ŋmani baŋa. (A big grass bracelet can't resemble a real bracelet.)
Variant: jeeni. Syn: ʒiɛhi.  O nyu la dam ka chani jeena. (He drank pito and staggered along.) A ti ti la wain ka ti nyu, ka di chɛ ka ti chani jeena. (You gave us wine and it made us reel.)
Sim: jatolɛlle, nansanjɛrigu.  Jeeŋa maa be kom ni. (The crab is in the water.) O gbaai la jeeŋa. (He caught a crab.)
Variant: jɛsint. From: (Eng.) < (Grk.).  O lo la jeesint o nuu ni. (She wore jacinth on her finger.) Jeesint nyɛla kuɣ' viɛlli. (Jacinth is a beautiful stone.)
Jɛbɔ maa kpaŋsiri la wahi wabu. (The dance leader encourages the dancing.) Jɛbɔ maa mi amaajiro viɛnyɛla. (The leader knows the amaajiro well.)
Variant: jabɔbigu.  Jɛbɔbigu gbaagi o. (Body lice caught him.) Kookalilana n-wumdi jɛbɔbigu kumsi. (The wise person hears the crying of body lice.)

Variant: 'jabi. Colloc: soobul' jɛba.  Puuni duu maa daa malila duno' jaba. (The inner room had double doors.) Bɛ ni zaŋ soobul' mini soobul' jɛba yɛli bindir' shɛli. (Food they winnow with shovel and fork.)

Variant: jabili. Sim: wulli.  Maligumaana yiŋbia zaŋdila dirigu din mali jɛba ata kana. (The priest's servant brought a three-tined fork.) Di simdi ka firilatamdigu maa ti mali jaba ayɔbu di luɣa zuɣu. (The lampstand should have six branches on its sides.)
Sim: biŋŋmahindili.  Bɛ mɛ la jɛduɣu n zali. (They made a statue and set it up.) Jɛduɣu maa ʒiya dundɔŋ ni. (The statue is in the courtyard.)
Variant: jɛfali.  O ti o deemba la jɛfaligu. (He gave his father-in-law the son-in-law gifts.) Jɛfaligu maa nyɛla bindira mini liɣiri. (The son-in-law gifts are food and money.)
Syn: kpɔɣu.  Jɛhi maa be kaha zuɣu. (The corn-silk is on the millet.) O zaŋ jɛhi n lo mia. (He used raffia to make rope.)

english: to beat (meat), tenderize; to sift (flour); to alleviate
example: Variant: jahi. Serial: bahi jɛhi.  O jɛhi la nimdi maa ka di bala. (She beat the meat to make it tender.) N jahirla zim. (I am sifting flour.) Tim maa jɛhi o mi, amaa di ku tibi o. (The medicine alleviates him, but won't cure him.)
O zaŋ jɛli n ti o. (He gave him half.) Jɛli maa nyɛla din saɣi. (The half is enough.)

english: to lean upon/against; to entrust, foist upon; to surrender/yield
example: O jɛli la dɔɣu maa. (He leaned against the tree.) A ni niŋ o naani ka chɛ a tuma jɛl' o? (Will you rely on him and entrust your work to him?) a baɣa ka n tizo maa ni chɛ tuma jɛli ma ŋɔ ni? (Don't you care that my sister has lumbered me with the work?)
Colloc: jɛligi maŋa. See: je.  O jɛligi o maŋa vuhigu ni. (He relaxed himself in rest.) Jɛligim a maŋa ka di gbarigi. (Relax yourself so you don't get tired.)
O nyɛla jɛliima, o deei o ba fali. (He is the elder son, he inherited his father's property.) Jɛliima maa su la yiŋa. (The elder son is in charge of the house.)
Variant: jeluŋ.  O zaŋ jɛliŋ n duɣi. (She used yam powder to cook.) Jɛliŋ maa nyɛla bindirigu. (Yam powder is food.)

Colloc: tiŋgbaŋ jɛma yilli.  Naawuni jɛma laɣiŋgu shɛhi zaa. (All God's worship places.) Ti ku nya ti ni yɛn zaŋ yɛl' shɛli fiɣisi o, naɣila zaŋ chaŋ o Naawuni jɛma la polo. (We won't find anything to accuse him of, except in his worship of God.)

english: to bounce, jump up and down
example: Colloc: yiɣiri jɛmdi.  Bihi maa jɛmdi la garo zuɣu. (The children are bouncing on the bed.) Zoya daa yiɣiri jɛmdi kaman piɛlahi la. (The mountains skipped like rams.)

english: to serve; to worship; to greet with a present
example: Syn: paɣi, puhi. See: jɛmbu duu.  Yiŋbi' yino ku tooi jɛm duumanima ayi. (One servant cannot serve two masters.) Jɛmmiya Yawɛ ni suhupiɛlli! (Praise the Lord with joy!) Doo ŋɔ jɛmdi la paɣa ŋɔ niriba ni liɣiri mini guli. (The man pays attentions to the woman's people with money and kola.)
Sim: jaŋa, kulibɔŋjaŋa, jɛnnɔkurigu, jambirigu, ŋmaaŋa, ŋmansuliga.  Jɛmbaŋa maa be tia zuɣu. (The small red monkey is on the tree.) Jɛmbaŋa ŋubiri la bindira. (The monkey is eating food.)
Jɛmbia maa be duu maa zuɣu. (The gable is on top of the room.) Jɛmbia maa viɛla. (The gable is beautiful.)
See: jɛm; bia.  O nyɛla jɛmbia, o tumdi la o duuma sani. (He is a servant, he works for his master.) Jɛmbihi maa zaa tum ya. (All the servants have worked.)

See: jɛm; duu.  Yawɛ jilima n-daa pal' o jɛmbu duu maa. (The Lord's glory filled his temple.) Lala jɛmbu duu tab' kpuɣibu maa pa paɣ' kpuɣibo maŋli. (A wedding in such a place of worship is not a valid marriage.)
See: jɛm.  Di chɛliya ka Yawɛ jɛmda be yi ni. (Don't let a worshipper of the Lord be among you.) Naawuni jɛmdi' maŋa. (God's genuine worshippers.)
binshɛli din nyɛ ʒiɛmani ŋɔ bin' jɛmdili la. (Everything this generation respects.) Jɛmdili maa nyɛla Naawuni. (The worthy of worship is God.)
Ti ni nya ninvuɣ' shɛb' zaa nimaani nyɛla wɔɣiri jɛmpɔna. (All the people we saw there are giants.) Jɛmpɔna maa waɣa pam. (The giants are very tall.)
O nyɛla jɛnda o tuma ni. (He is outstanding in his work.) Jɛnda n-nyɛ ŋun tooi niŋ li. (An exceptional person can do it.)
Sim: jɛni, jɛndɔɣu.  Jɛnda maa gbibi li. (The pivot holds it.) Jɛnda maa be sunsuuni. (The pivot is in the middle.)
O deei la jɛnda maa. (He took the responsibility.) Jɛnda maa nyɛla toondana. (The leader is a chief.)
Sim: jɛnda. See: jɛni; dɔɣu.  O zaŋ jɛndɔɣu n wɔhi gali. (She used a spindle to spin thread.) Jɛndɔɣu maa nyɛla dɔɣu. (The spindle is made of wood.)

english: to concern oneself; to oppose, do against; to manage to
example: Colloc: di shahira biɛɣu jɛndi X.  Di jɛndi a ya? (Does it concern you?) Miriya ka yi di ʒiri shɛhira n-jɛndi yi kpee. (Don't bear false witness against your neighbour.) Bɛ daa na bi jɛndi baŋ o kpiɔŋ maa daashili. (They hadn't yet managed to learn the secret of his strength.)

O gbibila gummua o nuu ni, ka mali o nubihi wahiri jɛni. (She holds the distaff and spins the spindle with her fingers.) Kalo ka bɛ mali niŋdi bɛ nubihi m-mali milisiri li ka zaŋ jɛni n-wɔhi li ka di niŋ mia. (They put whiting on their fingers, twist it, take a spindle and whirl it to make thread.) Bia maa mali jɛni. (The child has whooping cough.)
O yo la jɛnjɛni. (He paid a farthing.) Jɛnjɛni bi lahi beni pumpɔŋɔ. (Farthings are no longer around now.)
O ŋmɛri la jɛnjili. (He plays the bow.) Jɛnjili maa kum nyɛɣisa. (The bow's sound is sweet.)
Syn: guntarigu. See: jɛni; tari.  O zaŋ gumdi n niŋ jɛntarigu ni. (She put cotton in the receptacle.) Jɛntarigu maa pali gumdi. (The receptacle is full of cotton.)

Variant: jaŋgbariga. Colloc: duu jɛŋgbariga.  Jɛŋgbariga mi ni jɛŋkuno gbihirimi, asheela jɛŋkuno ne. (A mouse thinks the cat is sleeping, but the cat is awake.) Binshɛɣu dam duu ka jɛŋgbariga deei yu' biɛɣu. (Something makes noise in the room and the mouse gets a bad name.) Jɛŋkuno ka yiŋa ka jɛŋgbarisi tɔri jɔŋ. (When the cat's away, the mice play.) Duu jɛŋgbariga n-nyɛ o. (He is an internal enemy.)

Variant: gyaŋgbe-yoɣora. See: Gbɛngbiɛɣu; yɔɣirili.  Jɛŋgbɛyɔɣirili tia maa be mɔɣu ni. (The Moringa tree is in the bush.) Bɛ zaŋdi jɛŋgbɛyɔɣirili dɔri n mɛri yiya. (They use Moringa wood to build houses.)

Variant: jɛngbini, jɛŋgbuni, jaŋgbani. SynR: wanzam, zullana.  Kaman bɛ ni gbahi jɛŋgbini bihi ka o suhu yiɣisi shɛm la. (As a leopard is angry when its cubs are caught.) Jɛŋgbuni kpibiga ku ŋubi mɔri faashee nimdi. (An orphan leopard won't eat grass, only meat.) Jɛŋgbina mini bubihi ti laɣim doya. (Leopards and goat-kids will lie together.) jɛngbina ni tooi taɣi bɛ niŋgbuna bomaboma? (Can leopards change their spots?)
Jɛŋgbiringa tia maa wala nyɛɣisa. (The jɛŋgbiringa tree's berries are sweet.) Biɛla maa tɔhi la jɛŋgbiringa. (The children picked jɛŋgbiringa berries.)
Variant: jenkpubirigu, jaŋpɔbiriga. Sim: zɔŋ, zinzɔŋ.  Jɛŋkpibiriga maa gɔndi la tia zuɣu yuŋ. (The bat hangs on the tree at night.) Jɛŋkpibirisi pam be mɔɣu ni. (Many bats are in the bush.)

Variant: jɛnkpurigoo. See: jɛŋkpibiriga; goo.  Jɛŋkpibirigoo tia maa mali gɔhi pam. (The bat-thorn shrub has many thorns.) Bɛ zaŋdi jɛŋkpibirigoo dɔri n tibiri dɔriti. (They use bat-thorn wood for medicine.)
Jɛŋkpiliga maa gbi la bɔɣili. (The mole dug a hole.) Jɛŋkpiliga be mɔɣu ni. (The mole is in the bush.)
See: -bila.  Jɛŋkumbila maa ŋmɛri la diɛma. (The kitten is playing.) Jɛŋkumbila maa doli la o ma. (The kitten follows its mother.)
Variant: jɛnkunbirigu, jɛŋkunbiruŋ.  Jɛŋkunbiruŋ maa be mɔɣu ni. (The feral cat is in the bush.) Jɛŋkunbiruŋ bi miri niriba. (Feral cats don't come near people.)
See: -lɔɣu.  Jɛŋkunlɔɣu maa gbɛri la. (The tom-cat is sleeping.) Jɛŋkunlɔɣu maa gbaai la waɣi. (The tom-cat caught a snake.)

Variant: jɛnkunti. Syn: dudɔɣu. Sim: dulaamihi, naamam.  Bandɔɣu yi mali kɔbiri o zuli ni bɛ bi boon' o jɛŋkuno. (If a lizard has hair on its tail, they don't call it a cat.) Jɛŋgbariga mi ni jɛŋkuno gbihirimi, asheela jɛŋkuno ne. (A mouse thinks the cat is sleeping, but the cat is awake.) Jɛŋkuno ka yiŋa ka jɛŋgbarisi tɔri jɔŋ. (When the cat's away, the mice play.)

Variant: jɛnnɔkurigu. Sim: jaŋa, kulibɔŋjaŋa, jɛmbaŋa, jambirigu, ŋmaaŋa, ŋmansuliga.  Jɛŋnookuriga maa be tia zuɣu. (The small red monkey is on the tree.) Jɛŋnookuriga ŋubiri la bindira. (The monkey is eating food.)
Jɛpaa maa yi la Tokyo na. (The Japanese came from Tokyo.) Jɛpahi pam be Ghana. (Many Japanese are in Ghana.)
Liɣiri ŋɔ nyɛla jɛpaa. (This money is fake.) Jɛpaa maa bi viɛla. (The fake is not good.)
Sim: kpukpɔɣu.  O gbaai la jɛpaɣa mɔɣili ni. (He caught a catfish in the dam.) Jɛpaɣa maa nyɛɣisa. (The catfish is tasty.)
Syn: Kpariba.  Jɛra maa nyɛla ban be Gonja yaɣili. (The Kpariba people are in the Gonja area.) Bɛ wa la Jɛra salima ni. (They danced the Jɛra dance at the festival.)
N kul je ba la jɛri' maŋli. (I hate them with perfect hatred.) Jɛri maa bi viɛla. (Hatred is not good.)
O jɛri la puu maa. (He is hoeing the farm carelessly.) Jɛri ya, di bi viɛla. (Rough-cutting is not good.)

english: to be foolish; to overdo, do to excess
example: Cpart: yɛm. See: jɛrigu.  Biɛhiŋ kani, n jɛrigiya pam ni bɛ boli ma daadam. (Undoubtedly I am too foolish to be called a human being.) Bɛ zaa nyɛla bɛn jɛrigi ka ka yɛm. (They are all foolish and have no sense.) O jɛrigi ya o tuma ni. (He overdid his work.)

Sim: yiriŋ, yiruŋ. See: jɛrigu; jɛrigi.  Paɣ' jɛrigu kul tahirila pampam. (A foolish woman is always shouting.) Yinim' bɛn nyɛ zoon' jɛra ŋɔ. (You foolish blind people.) Dagɔr' jɛrigu nya ŋmariga m-mi ni biɛɣu. (The foolish adulterer saw the morning star and thought it was daybreak.)

Variant: jɛriti. Colloc: jɛrigu binmulli. Syn: zoligu. Sim: saguya, yinyaa. Cpart: yɛndaana.  o so ninvuɣ' kurigu ŋun nyɛ naa, ka nyɛ jɛrigu. (Better an old man who is a king than a fool.) Jɛrigu kari lariŋga duhi tia ka maani buɣim. (The fool chases a squirrel up a tree and prepares a fire.) Jɛrigu ka saa buri fɔŋ ni. (A fool is beaten by the rain even in town.) Shini pala jɛrigu ŋun yɛra pala yinyaa. (The quiet person is not a fool, the talker is not mad.)

Syn: jɛritali. See: -lim.  O yuli Nabal, ka jɛrilim mi shiri be o kɔbili ni. (His name is Nabal, and folly goes with him.) Bɛ ku ŋmaligi labi jɛrilim biɛhigu ni yaha. (They won't return to a life of folly.) Hal n yi nyu bara gba, m bi tum jɛrilim. (Even if I boasted, I wouldn't act foolishly.)

Syn: jɛrilim. See: -tali.  Bɛ noya ni yɛtɔɣa piinimi ni jɛritali, ka naari ni putɔɣu ni yinyari. (Their speech starts with foolishness and ends with spite.) Jɛritali bi viɛla. (Foolishness is not good.)

Syn: sarika. From: (Eng.) 'jail'.  Bɛ zaŋ o ti kpɛhi duu Jɔnatan yiŋa, dama bɛ daa zaŋ o yiŋ' maa leela jɛriyali. (They imprisoned him at Jonathan's house, because they had made his house a jail.) Jɛriyali maa ni, niriba pam be ni. (In the prison, there are many people.)
Sim: pɔra, 'bila. Cpart: wɔɣu, wɔɣa, 'wɔɣinli. See: jinjini; toobalaji.  O ji ya. (He has become short.) Tia maa ji ya. (The tree has become short.)

Colloc: so' jia, da' jia. Sim: pɔra, bila. Cpart: wɔɣu. Ant: wɔɣa, wɔɣinli, 'wɔɣili. See: dajitɔŋli; jinjini.  Yi wuɣiri sarisi pari ninvuɣ' waɣila mini ninvuɣ' jihi zaa zuɣuri. (You weave veils for people tall and short.) Do'jia ku limisi sima ni. (A short man cannot disappear in a groundnut farm.) Nimaani polo ni daa nyɛ so' jia maa. (That was the short cut.) Da' jia n-nyɛ li. (It's a short stick.)

Colloc: ŋmaai X jia. Sim: 'jia.  ŋ-ŋmaai li jiya. (To cut it short.) Yɛla maa jia ŋmaabu. (A summary of the matter.) Ninvuɣ' shɛb' bɛn bi zɔri nambɔɣu kun ya ŋmaai yi yuma jia. (Merciless people kill you and cut short your years.)
Sim: kpi, jifa. See: jifa.  O jiba ya. (He died naturally.) Jibu maa niŋ ya. (The natural death has happened.)
O nyɛla 'jibiga, o mali yaa. (He is short and strong.) 'jibiga maa tooi kpuɣi binshɛɣu. (The tough person can lift things.)

Cpart: kɔrigili. Sim: biŋkpiŋ, kum. Sit: nimdi, kɔrigi, je. From: (Ar.).  Bɛ di diri buɣa maligu nimdi mini jifa ni binʒim. (They shouldn't eat meat from pagan sacrifices or non-halal meat with blood.) Afa bi diri jifa. (The Muslim scholar doesn't eat improperly butchered meat.)

english: to shiver, tremble; to worry, be anxious; to covet
example: Variant: jiɣinda, jiɣitiri, jiɣiti. Colloc: jiɣi X zuɣu. Sim: bo, bole, kɔre, kɔrisi, kpuɣisi, yɛliyura, yiŋ, yirigi, yu, zɔhi, zuɣisi, ye, paɣi.  Yi di chɛ ka yi suhuri zɔhira bee n-jiɣiri. (Don't let your hearts be unsettled or alarmed.) Kpeen jiɣijiɣilana tirila yɛlimaŋ' gɔbili. (An impatient elder gives left-handed truth.) Maata! Maata! A jiɣirimi ka nahind' amaŋ' bina pam zuɣu. (Martha! Martha! You are worrying about many things.) Bee bi jiɣitiri waa zuɣu. (A leg is not anxious about dancing.)
See: jiɣi; jiɣinda.  O nyɛla jiɣijiɣi, o bi guhira. (He is impatient, he doesn't wait.) Jiɣijiɣi bi viɛla. (Being rash is not good.)

See: jiɣi.  N suhu bi doya ka n kul jiɣinda n fabila maa zuɣu. (My thoughts trouble me and I am distraught.) Miriya ka yi jiɣindi yi nyɛviya bee yi niŋgbuna zuɣu. (Do not worry about your life or your bodies.) Di chɛliya ka yi suhuri jiɣindi shɛli zuɣu. (Don't let your hearts be anxious about anything.)
O ŋmɛri la jiliga viɛnyɛla. (He plays the xylophone well.) Jiliga maa kum nyɛɣisa. (The xylophone's sound is sweet.)
Variant: jiliga. Etym: OV *GI: ; OV *JE/I ; OVN *JIL -DI/A.  O jila biɛri o. (His veins hurt.) Jiligu maa gbibi o nimgbina. (The tendon holds his flesh.)

Colloc: mɔŋ X jilima, jilima booi, booi X jilima. Sim: dariza, firijita, jiri, sizuura, tibiginsim. From: (Ha.). See: jiri.  Bidibiga tir' o ba jilima, ka dabili mi tiri o dan' jilima. (A son gives his father respect, a slave gives his master.) A zaŋ jiri mini jilima pili ba. (You crown them with glory and honour.) Yi jilima ni booi. (Your dignity will decrease.) Jɛmbu du' palli ŋɔ jilima ni gari jɛmbu du' kurili la jilima. (The glory of the new temple will exceed the old.)

english: glorious, honourable person; Lord of Glory
example: See: -lana.  O nyɛla jilimalana, sokam mali o jilima. (He is an honourable person, everyone respects him.) A nyɛla jilimalana, ka mali dariza. (You are glorious and majestic.)
See: gbɔŋ; jiŋli.  O zaŋ jiliŋgbaŋ n puhi jiŋli. (He used a prayer-mat to pray.) Jiliŋgbaŋ maa be jiŋli ni. (The prayer-mat is in the mosque.)

Syn: muni. Sim: jilli.  Kulipal' bia kumdila nyu-ŋmaa zuɣu, pa ni ziŋjilli ŋun zuɣu. (A fisherman's child cries for a piece of yam, not for a whole fish.) Jilli maa zaa di ya. (The whole thing has been eaten.)
Colloc: kɔhi/da jilli. Sim: 'jilli.  O da la bindira jilli. (He bought food in bulk.) Jilli kɔhibu nyɛla din mali nyɔri. (Selling wholesale is profitable.)

english: sinew, tendon; root fibres; portion of meat/fish
example: Variant: jiligu. Colloc: che n-chihi X jila, ba jila.  N ni niŋ jila niŋ ya. (I will put sinews on you.) O daa chɛ n-chihi wɔr' shɛb' bɛn kpalim zaa jila. (He hamstrung all the remaining horses.) Naawuni yɛligu bi ba jila bɛ suhuri ni. (God's word hadn't rooted well in their hearts.) O ŋma nimdi maa jila jila. (He cut the meat into pieces.) Kulipal' bia kumdila nyu-ŋmaa zuɣu, pa ni ziŋjilli ŋun zuɣu. (A fisherman's child cries for a slice of yam, not for a piece of fish.)
O jim liɣiri, o bi lahi bɔri shɛli. (He has more than enough money, he needs nothing else.) Jimbu nyɛla din viɛla. (Having abundance is good.) Colloc: O jim liɣiri.
O di bindirigu naai ka jim. (He finished eating and belched.) Jimbu bi viɛla niriba tooni. (Belching before people is not good.)

english: to be stiff; to be paralysed with fear
example: O niŋgbuŋ jin ya. (His body is stiff.) Dabiɛm zuɣu, o jin ya. (Because of fear, he became paralysed.)
O lo la jina o nyaaŋa. (He wore a bandolier on his back.) Jina maa nyɛla paɣaba jina. (The fetish is for women.)

Colloc: to jina, jina tɔbu, jina tɔbu duu, mali jina. See: jintɔra.  Bɛ daa laɣim ni bɛ to yɛlli maa jina. (They met to consult on the matter.) Bɔzuɣu ka zuliya kam tɔri jin' yoya? (Why are all tribes making useless plots?) M mini ba ku mali jina. (I will not associate with them.) Dunia Jina Duu. (The United Nations.)
See: jinwara.  Bɛ wa la jina. (They danced the fetish dance.) Jina waa nyɛla din mali viɛlim. (The fetish dance is beautiful.)
Jinjaɣu maa be mɔɣu ni. (The beetle is in the bush.) Jinjaɣu maa nyɛla binnɛma. (The beetle is an insect.)
Jinjaɣu gbaagi o, o naba biɛri o. (Toe disease caught him, his feet hurt.) Jinjaɣu maa nyɛla dɔro. (The disease is painful.)
Variant: jinjɛla.  O ŋmɛri la jinjala. (He plays the stringed instrument.) Jinjala maa kum nyɛɣisa. (The instrument's sound is sweet.)
Sim: goonje.  O ŋmɛri la jinjɛluŋ. (He plays the string instrument.) Jinjɛluŋ maa kum nyɛɣisa. (The instrument's sound is sweet.)

Variant: jɛnjɛm, jɛnjɛmŋma, jɛnjɛŋŋmaa. Syn: baraʒiɛ, kuriga, kpalaŋa. See: jinjɛnlani; jinjɛŋŋmaa.  Tummi tuma n-gari jinjɛm' muɣi gbalipini. (Working is better than having tight shorts.) Bɛ so jinjɛm piɛla. (They should wear white knickers.) Kaman nira ni so jɛnjɛŋŋmaa ka di tab' o ningbuŋ shɛm la. (As shorts cling tightly, so I bound them to me.)
See: jinjɛm; lani.  O kparila jinjɛnlani. (He wears baggy pantaloons.) Jinjɛnlani maa viɛla. (The baggy pantaloons are beautiful.)

Variant: jinjɛmŋmaa, jɛnjɛmŋma, jɛnjɛŋŋmaa. Sim: baraʒiɛ, kuriga, kpalaŋa. Syn: jinjɛm. See: jinjɛm; ŋmaa.  Cham' ti da jinjɛŋŋma' piɛlli n-zaŋ so. (Go and buy white shorts and put them on.) A ti she jinjɛŋŋmahi ti ba ka di limsi bɛ zaɣim. (Make shorts for them to cover their nakedness.)

Sim: bara, barilim, karigi, 'kpabili, ŋmɛlim, tibigi. Cpart: dani, falim, faai, gaŋsi, kɔɣisi, kɔɣu, 'kpaɣisili, nyuui, pib, viɛbiluŋ, wab. See: ji; jia.  O nyɛla jinjini, o waɣilim bi paai. (He is short and stocky, his height doesn't reach.) Jinjini maa mali yaa. (The stocky person is strong.)
Sim: buhili.  O diri la jinjini. (He eats boiled yam.) Jinjini nyɛla bindirigu din nyɛɣisa. (Boiled yam is tasty.)

See: jina; to.  Jintɔriba maa ni daa wum lala maa ka suli ku ba pam. (When the councillors heard this, they became very angry.) O nyɛla jintɔra, o be la jina tɔbu duu ni. (He is a councillor, he is in the assembly hall.)

Variant: jiŋwara. Sim: baɣa, bɔha, bukpaha, laasabuniŋda, takatibinima.  Naa Sool chaŋ jinwara sani. (King Saul went to a fire-dancer diviner.) Sool daa pun kari la bukpahinim' mini jinwariba Izraɛl tiŋgbɔŋ ni zaa. (Saul had driven out sorcerers and fire-dancers from all Israel.)
O jiŋ ya, o bi lahi mi o ni yɛn niŋ shɛm. (He is confused, he no longer knows what to do.) Di jiŋ a maŋa. (Don't put yourself off.)

english: to be stiff, hard to stir, indigestible
example: Bindirigu maa jiŋ ya, di bi tooi ŋubi. (The food is indigestible, can't be eaten.) Saɣim maa jiŋ ya. (The t.z. is stiff.)
Syn: 'jia. See: murijiŋa.  O nyɛla 'jiŋa. (He is short.) 'jiŋa maa waɣilim bi paai. (The short person's height doesn't reach.)

Variant: jinlaa. See: jiŋli; laa.  O zaŋ jiŋlaa n ʒi kom n ti jiŋli puhibu. (He used a kettle to carry water for prayers.) Yaɣimɛriba ni mɛri neen' shɛŋa m-bɔŋɔ furlanima, lakɔriti, yaɣiri jinlaa. (Potters make lamps, bowls, clay ablution vessels.)

Colloc: puhi jiŋli. Sim: afa. See: jiŋlaa; jiŋpuhira.  O chaŋ jiŋli ni n ti puhi. (He went to the mosque to pray.) Wuntia n-gari jiŋli. (Righteousness is better than mere prayer.) Jiŋli puhibu saɣiya. (It is time for worship.)
See: jiŋli; puhi; jiŋpuhiribini.  O nyɛla jiŋpuhira, o puhiri jiŋli dabsili kam. (He is a Muslim, he prays every day.) Jiŋpuhiriba maa laɣim ya jiŋli ni. (The Muslims gathered in the mosque.)

english: gifts given to Muslims for praying for the dead
example: See: jiŋli; puhi; bini.  Bɛ ti la jiŋpuhiribini n-ti kpiimba maa. (They gave gifts to the Muslims for praying for the dead.) Jiŋpuhiribini maa nyɛla liɣiri mini bindira. (The gifts are money and food.)
Colloc: jiŋsi zuɣu.  Bia yi ʒi bini din gari o, o zuɣu n-yɛn jiŋsi. (If a child carries a load too heavy, his head will nod.) O jiŋsi o zuɣu n wuhi ni o saɣi. (He nodded his head to show agreement.)
See: jirili.  O jira biɛri o. (His jaw hurts.) Jira maa nyɛla kɔbili. (The jaw is a bone.)

Variant: jidanbisa. Colloc: jirambisa zuɣusaa duu. Syn: daachimbisa. From: (Ha.).  O me la jirambisa. (He built a storey building.) Ilaija daa zaŋ bia maa yi jirambisa maa zuɣu n-siɣ' na. (Elijah brought the child down from upstairs.) O kpi, ka bɛ suug' o kom n-zaŋ o kpɛhi jirambisa zuɣusaa duu ti sɔŋ. (She died, they bathed her and laid her in an upstairs room.)
From: (Ha.).  O zaŋ jiransabilo n pili o garo. (He used a mosquito net over his bed.) Jiransabilo maa gu o ka chɛ duuŋa. (The mosquito net protects him from mosquitoes.)

Colloc: mali jiri, ka jiri. Sim: dariza, firijita, jilima, sizuura.  Naawuni, a chandi mali jiri pam. (God, your procession has great glory.) A zaŋ jiri mini jilima pili ba. (You crown them with glory and honour.) O mali jiri, niriba zaa mi o. (He has excellence, everyone knows him.)
Jiriginchili maa dimi o. (The lizard bit him.) Jiriginchili nyɛla binnɛma. (The lizard is a creature.)

Sim: yɛno, yee.  N daa chɛ n jira ti ninvuɣ' shɛb' bɛn dariti sibiri. (I offered my cheeks to those who pulled out my beard.) Nira yi niŋ kuɣa gari o kaŋubira puuni, o jira n-yɛn baɣi. (If you mix stones with millet, your jaws will get tired.)

english: Euphorbia balsamifera tree; riverside wood cluster
example: See: gbiriga.  Jiriyuŋ tia maa be mɔɣu ni. (The Euphorbia tree is in the bush.) Jiriyuŋ maa nyɛla tihi laɣingu. (The riverside wood is a cluster of trees.)
Variant: jitaya. From: (Eng.) < (Span.) < (It.) < (Grk.).  O ŋmɛri la jitaa. (He plays the guitar.) Jitaa maa kum nyɛɣisa. (The guitar's sound is sweet.)
Variant: jeeya, jiiya. From: (Eng.) 'gear'.  O taɣi la jiya n chɛ moto maa. (He changed gear to stop the car.) Jiya maa chihiya. (The gear-lever broke.)

english: lightly; for a little while, briefly
example: O gbihi la jo. (He slept briefly.) Jo ka ti chaŋ. (Let's go for a little while.)

english: to drop in a sticky form
example: See: joll; jolijoli.  Tim maa joligi la tiŋa. (The medicine dropped stickily on the ground.) Kom maa joligi la. (The water dropped stickily.)

Variant: joljol. See: joll.  Gingalanyɔri maa chani ka chɛri jolijoli. (The snail moves and leaves slime.) Kaman gingalanyɔri ni yi chaŋ gari ka bɛ jolijoli doya shɛm la. (Like a snail that goes and leaves slime.)
See: joligi; jolijoli.  Bindirigu maa nyɛla joll. (The food is sticky.) Joll bi viɛla nuu ni. (Sticky things are not nice in the hand.)
See: zuɣim.  O juɣim la tiŋa maa. (He disrupted the town.) Juɣimbu bi viɛla. (Disruption is not good.)
O tum tuma maa juɣimajuɣima. (He did the work roughly.) Juɣimajuɣima bi viɛla. (Doing things roughly is not good.)

Colloc: daa. juɣu. Etym: OV *YOB ; OVN *ZU-GA/SI~GU/TI. See: kulijuɣu; jukaŋgba.  Bɛ je juɣu ka o laana. (People hate the vulture, yet it soars.) Jaŋa lari juɣu zuɣu polo ka ŋun jaŋ' gbina fa? (The monkey laughs at the vulture's bald head, but what about his own buttocks?) Pinimiya zuɣukpina ka di be kaman juɣu zuɣu la. (Shave your heads like a vulture.) Kɔrisi yi mali a, a bi lan ŋubiri juri. (Even if you crave meat, you don't eat vultures.) Ŋunkam mi kpi mɔɣu ni, juri ni di o. (Anyone who dies in the bush, vultures will eat him.) O nyɛla daa juɣu, o be daa ni dabisili kam. (He is a frequent trader, he's in the market every day.)

english: to take a bit of food; to put aside; to lay out in little heaps
example: O juhi la bindirigu biɛla. (He took a bit of food.) O juhi la liɣiri maa. (He put the money aside.) O juhi la sima maa niŋ kpalansi ni. (She put the groundnuts in little heaps in basins.)
Sim: jɔhi, kpahi.  O juhi la dama o naba biɛri o. (He limps because his foot hurts.) Juhibu ni tooi niŋ di yi ti gbaai nira. (Limping can happen if someone is hurt.)
Variant: jukangba. See: juɣu.  Jukaŋgba maa yiɣiya zuɣusaa. (The large vulture flew up high.) Jukaŋgba maa be tia zuɣu. (The vulture is on the tree.)
From: (Eng.).  Bɛ zaŋ junipa dɔri n mɛ duu. (They used juniper wood to build the room.) Junipa tihi be mɔɣu ni. (Juniper trees are in the bush.)
Sim: balim balim, baalim, biɛla biɛla, fahi, fo, fɔhi, fooi, gbim, nyamm, surim.  O ʒiya juŋ. (He is sitting quietly.) Juŋ ka ti wum yɛltɔɣa. (Quietly, let's hear the story.)

Sim: taai, di.  Nira bi juuri firla n-zaŋ bin liŋa m-pindi li. (No one lights a lamp and covers it.) O daa niŋ buɣim juug' a. (He made a fire and set fire to you.) O daa niŋ buɣim juui ziŋa. (He lit a fire at the place.)

Variant: yuusi. From: (Eng.).  O juusi la o fɔn nyɛvili. (He used his phone.) Di juusi a liɣiri zaa saɣi. (Don't use all your money foolishly.) A ni tooi juusi buku ŋɔ n bɔhim Dagbani. (You can use this book to learn Dagbani.)
Pukpara maa daa kuli kari mi n-yihiri kom din pahi maa, dama di daa yɛn bɔri mi ni di kpe. (The farmers kept bailing out the excess water, because it was about to flood.)
A kuli ka-na, ka ti ʒini n-gbihi. (You just come here, and we will sit and sleep.)
O daa kari o taali n-ti o ma, dama o daa zu o liɣiri. (He confessed his wrongdoing to his mother, because he had stolen her money.)

english: to make something circular; to coil something
example: Bɛ karila tuzuɣisi ka bɛ yɛn zaŋ m-pa bɛ zuɣu. (They are making head-pads to put on their heads.)

english: to not exist; to be absent; to be dead
example: Saha shɛli ti ni daa paai naai, doo maa daa kani o yiŋa. (When we arrived, the man was not at home.)

english: to not have; to be without
example: N ka liɣiri din yɛn yo o tibiri. (I don't have the money to pay for his medicine.)
Ŋuni n-lee ka a bori? Ka Abu ka a bori? (Who then do you want? Is it Abu you want?)
O sa kana ka gari. (He came here yesterday and passed on.)
M-paɣi Naawumi, ka kari ni ti bihi. (I thanked God and prayed for our children.)

english: to pronounce a blessing or a curse; to cast a spell
example: Docti kpeen-kpeen so n-daa kana ti kari ti puu, ka ti puhi o ni alibarika. (A famous traditional healer came to bless our farm, and we gave him a cowrie for his blessing.)

english: a type of shrub (edible, white flowers); a "juju" to safeguard property
example: O bo kaa n-ti o puu. (He got protective medicine for his farm.)
From: (English).  O daa zaŋ o kaa n-chaŋ daa ni. (He took his car to go to the market.)
Muslinim' chanila Kaaba Haʒi ni. (Muslims go to the Ka'abah for the Hajj.)
From: (English).  O daa lo kaachii chinchini. (He bought a khaki cloth.)
Bɛ daa ti kaada maa nayili ni. (They gave the wedding gift at the palace.)

english: cooked balls of porridge (wrapped in leaves)
example: O daa kɔhi kaafa la daa ni. (She sold balls of porridge at the market.)
Biɛɣu kaai ka ti yiɣisi chaŋ puu ni. (Day dawned and we got up and went to the farm.)

english: to visit; to inspect; to look at/watch/survey
example: O daa kaai ti sana. (He visited us.)
From: (English?).  O zaŋla kaakaachi n-kpaai kom maa niŋ koliba ni. (She used a funnel to pour the water into the bottle.)
From: (Twi).  O zaŋla kaakaduro n-niŋ tii maa ni. (She put ginger in the tea.)

english: Kaaku (rank of officer of Dagomba gunmen)
example: Bɛ daa piigi Kaaku n-leei bɛ toondana. (They appointed Kaaku as their leader.)
Bɛ zaŋla kaalo n-shee yili maa. (They used whitewash to paint the house.)
Bɛ daa gbaai kaalua maa ka chaŋ n-ti o kootu. (They arrested the prostitute and took her to court.)
From: (English).  Kaansili maa laɣimya n-ti yɛli yɛla. (The council met to discuss matters.)
Bɛ daa zaŋ kaara maa n-ti paɣa maa. (They gave the wedding gift to the woman.)

english: whew! (exclamation of relief at narrow escape)
example: Kaasa! Ti daa tiligi mi. (Whew! We escaped.)
Kaashi! Di pala din viɛla. (Pah! That's not good.)
Bɛ daa zaŋ kaasia n-niŋ tulaale maa ni. (They put cassia in the perfume.)
From: (Hausa).  Kaayakaaya maa ʒi la nɛma maa zaa. (The porter carried all the loads.)
From: (Hausa).  Kaayayo maa ʒi la bindira maa chaŋ daa ni. (The female porter carried the food to the market.)

english: peg of hoe (iron ring securing hoe handle)
example: Kabaŋa maa n-daa chɛ ka kuli maa gbaai viɛnyɛla. (It was the iron ring that made the hoe hold fast properly.)
Bɛ daa zaŋ kabɔɣili maa n-kuri chi. (They used the threshing-floor to thresh the millet.)
O daa ŋme o nia ka di kabi ʒim pam. (He hit his hand and it bled a lot.)

english: to break; to snap (as a stick)
example: O daa kabi dari maa ka zaŋ li n-tu buɣim maa ni. (He broke the stick and put it into the fire.)

english: to burn (as food in a pot)
example: Saɣim maa kabi ya, dama a bi guli li. (The food has burnt, because you didn't watch it.)

english: to rush; to hurry; to do quickly
example: Bihi maa zaa daa kabi chaŋ na n-ti nya saŋmariga maa. (All the children rushed out to see the comet.)

english: a millet seed; seed of grain
example: Pl: kabiɛla.  Kabiɛlli yini n-daa kpalim m-pa ti puu ni. (Only one millet seed was left in our farm.)
Ti yɛn kɔ la kabiga yuuni ŋɔ. (We are going to plant acha grass this year.)
Pl: kabisi.  Kabiga maa daa ŋɛri buɣli maa pam. (The horse-fly was biting the donkey a lot.)
Tia maa zali waɣila, ka di zaa ni kabigi. (The tree branch was long, and it will break.)
Di zaŋ kaɣili kabili n-ti o, ka o zaŋ dihi o nia. (He was given a broken stalk and he used it to support his hand.)

english: a charm; a spell (to bind with juju power)
example: O daa chaŋ ti lo kabire ni o dim' maa di lahi tooi yi. (He went to cast a spell so that his enemy could not rise again.)
Bɛ daa zaŋ o niŋ kabiri ni wuntaŋa. (They buried him in the grave in the afternoon.)

english: a griddle (for charring roots and herbs)
example: O zaŋla kabiriga n-kabi tim maa. (She used a griddle to char the medicine.)
Kabiriga ŋɔ mali baŋsim pam. (This middle-aged woman has a lot of wisdom.)
Kabirili n-daa kpalim n-ti ti yuuni ŋɔ. (We had some seed-millet left over from last year.)

english: to be weary; to be weak
example: N kabisi ya n tuma maa ni. (I am weary from this work.)

english: to break into small pieces; to smash to pieces
example: O kabisi li ni nuɣu, ka di leei kamani puɣu la. (He broke it up with his hand, and it became like flour.)
Kabiya maa ka buɣim maa ni tooi niŋ kpɛŋ. (Without the bellows, the fire cannot get hot enough.)
A yi kul tum tuma kabo, a ni nya di sanyoo. (However hard you work, you will get its reward.)
Kaburi saha n-daa nyɛ li. (It was during the threshing time.)
O zaŋla kaburidɔɣu n-buri kaburi maa. (He is using a flail to thresh the millet.)
Kachɛɣu saha, sokam mali suhupiɛlli. (At harvest time, everyone is happy.)
Kachɛra maa pun kana puu ni na. (The harvesters have already come to the farm.)

english: the root or the source of a matter
example: Ti bi nya kafia zaŋ chaŋ yɛlimaŋli maa ni. (We have not gotten to the root of the truth of the matter.)
O zaŋla kafini n-feeri buɣim maa. (She is using a fan to fan the fire.)
Docti maa daa niŋ o kafiŋgbaŋ ni o doro maa yi. (The healer performed cupping on him so that the sickness would leave.)

english: a cupper; one who treats by blood-letting
example: Bɛ daa boli kafu na ni o ti tibgi o. (They called a cupper to come and treat him.)
O ŋmɛrila kafuɣila pam, amaa o bi tumdi shɛli. (He boasts a lot, but he does nothing.)
Kaɣa maa zaa kpeya. (All the stalks are dry.)

english: straw; stunted guinea corn stalks used as grass for horses
example: Bɛ zaŋla kaɣabihi m-pa wɔri maa. (They used the straw to feed the horses.)

english: horse grass (a type of grass for fodder)
example: Wɔri maa dira kaɣalom ni suhupiɛlli. (The horses are eating the grass happily.)
O nia nyɛla kaɣaluŋ ni tuma tumbu zuɣu. (His hands are rough from doing work.)

english: to do something completely (often with baŋ 'know')
example: O baŋla Dagbani kaɣi. (He knows Dagbani perfectly.)

english: to half-close something; to shade/half-close the eyes
example: O daa kaɣi o nina lihiri wuntaŋa. (He half-closed his eyes looking at the sun.)

english: to retreat; to retire; to avoid
example: O daa kaɣi yi kpaŋa ni bɛ di nya o. (He retreated to one side so they wouldn't see him.)
Bɛ zaŋla kaɣigbiŋ m-pa buɣim maa ni. (They used the straw stalks to add to the fire.)
O malila o yili maa kaɣikaɣi. (He fixed his house completely.)

english: to open something slightly; to reveal
example: O daa kaɣiki dunoli maa n-lihi na. (He opened the door a little and looked out.)
Bɛ daa zaŋ o niŋ kagili maa ni n-ti o. (They put him into the grave and buried him.)
Kaɣili ŋɔ waɣa pam. (This millet stalk is very long.)

english: to nag; to worry; to bother; to pester; to prevent
example: Bia maa kaɣirila o ba ni o da o binshɛɣu. (The child is pestering his father to buy him something.)

english: a type of long swamp grass used for mats
example: Bɛ zaŋla kaɣili n-wuɣi gbala. (They use that swamp grass to weave mats.)
Paɣa maa mali kaɣiligu pam o dabsili kam ni. (The woman has a lot of menstrual pain every month.)
Bia ŋɔ kaɣilimi, o bi wumdi yɛltɔɣa. (This child is defiant, he doesn't listen.)

english: the pith of guineacorn stalks and other reeds
example: Kaɣipuri maa nyɛla din baligi pam. (The pith of the stalk is very soft.)

english: a farm in its third year after yams
example: Ti puu pa nyɛla kaɣisɔɣu. (Our farm is now in its third year after yams.)

english: a split stalk for basket- and mat-weaving
example: O zaŋla kaɣishia n-pili piɛɣu maa. (She used the split stalks to start the basket.)

english: to cough up phlegm; to clear the throat
example: O daa kaɣisi o lɔŋni pɔi ka naan yi tɔɣisi yɛltɔɣa. (He cleared his throat before he spoke.)

english: to shell nuts; to remove bark; to wear out (metal); to scoop/scrape up refuse
example: Paɣiba maa kaɣisiri sima maa. (The women are shelling groundnuts.)

english: 'medicine' giving immunity from bullets and witchcraft
example: O daa bo kagiʒe ni bɛ ku tooi ŋme o. (He got bullet-proof medicine so they couldn't shoot him.)
O daa chɛla dunoli maa kaɣu. (He left the door slightly open.)
Kaɣu maa kumdi la yuŋ kam. (The owl hoots every night.)

english: to pack up; to pile up; to gather; to tie up; to knead
example: O daa kahi o nɛma zala o gbini. (He packed his things and placed them beside him.)
Paɣa maa daa kahi yuŋ maa zaa ka naan yi dɔɣi asiba. (The woman laboured all night before giving birth in the morning.)

english: to improve in health; to regain strength; to be refreshed
example: O ni daa nyu tim maa naai, o kahigi ya. (After he took the medicine, he regained his strength.)

english: to reveal; to present; to demonstrate
example: N pukpaara maa n-daa ti kahigi n wuhi ni lala tuma maa tum ya. (It was my fellow farmer who revealed to me that that type of work is finished.)

english: a revelation; the Book of Revelation
example: Bɛ karimdila Kahigibu Litaafi Asaaba kam. (They read the Book of Revelation every Sunday.)
Bɛ daa di li zaa kahikahi. (They ate it all up completely.)

english: raw; unripe; young (of leaves); immature; frisky (of children); clumsy (of adults)
example: Di di nim' kahili, dama di ni tooi che ka a doro gbaagi a. (Don't eat raw meat, because it can make you sick.)

english: to twist (a rope); to wring out/squeeze sth.; to hug/squeeze; to wink/blink/clench
example: O kahim o nuhi pɔi ka naan yi pili tuma. (He wrung his hands before starting work.)
Kahimpiɛɣu maa viɛlim m-paai sokam nina. (The butterfly's beauty caught everyone's eye.)
Bɛ daa zaŋ kahiŋga viɛlli n-kpiri o. (They wrapped him in a fine shroud.)
Kahinkɔɣu maa daa ʒi tia zuɣu n-kumdi. (The crow was sitting on the tree cawing.)

english: a wild plant eaten by cattle (lit. 'crow's yam')
example: Niŋgɔbiri maa dira kahiŋkɔɣunyuuli maa. (The cattle are eating the crow's yam plant.)
Ti puu ni kahu ŋɔ daa viɛla pam yuuni ŋɔ. (The millet in our farm was very good this year.)
Kaito! A bi mi shɛli. (Tchah! You know nothing.)
Pl: kakpɔndira.  Bɛ zaŋla kakpɔndigu n-kpari dunoli maa. (They used a prop to keep the door-shutter in place.)

english: grain improperly hulled; guinea corn chaff
example: Kakpiri maa zaa kpalim ya. (All the chaff remained.)

english: an omen (good or bad); a nickname of Musa (the Prophet Moses)
example: Bɛ daa nya lala ka di leei kalaamu n-ti ba. (They saw that and it became an omen for them.)

english: Kalahi (title of an officer in the Dagomba army)
example: Bɛ daa piigi Kalahi n-leei bɛ toondana. (They appointed Kalahi as their leader.)
O piɛbrila kalamboo ka di kumsi nyaɣisa. (He is blowing a flute and its sound is sweet.)
From: (Arabic) via (Hausa) 'alikalami'.  O zaŋla o kalami n-sabi gbaŋ. (He used his pen to write a letter.)
Bɛ daa zaŋ kalamosi n-niŋ tulaale maa ni. (They put calamus in the perfume.)

english: a person without a guardian; an orphaned or isolated person
example: Pl: kalanima.  Kalana ŋɔ niŋ bala ka sokam zo o. (This orphan is young and everyone pities him.)
From: (English).  O lihila kalanda maa ni n-nya dabsili shɛli din pun gari. (He looked at the calendar to see which day had passed.)

english: a baby under two weeks old
example: Bɛ na bi yihiri kalee maa naŋa. (They don't yet take the newborn baby outside.)
O daa kɔhi kalɛwɛle la daa ni. (She sold fried plantain chips in the market.)
O kali liɣiri maa zaa. (He counted all the money.)
O kanda o yili maa zaa. (She is sweeping all of her house.)

english: a custom; tradition; tribal rite; hereditary tendency; a type of medicine
example: Ti kali wuhiya ni ti niŋmi lala. (Our tradition says we should do this.)

english: a prostitute who stays in a brothel
example: Bɛ daa gbaai kalibaa maa ka chaŋ n-ti o kootu. (They arrested the prostitute and took her to court.)

english: drumming at a chief's palace or where chiefs are gathered
example: Ti ni daa chaŋ nayili na, ti wum kaliluŋa. (When we went to the chief's palace, we heard drumming.)

english: to coil; to writhe; to move like a snake
example: Wahu maa kalimdi gindi tiŋa. (The snake is coiling and moving on the ground.)
Kalima maa zooi kuɣa maa zuɣu. (The moss is growing thick on the rock.)
O puu ni kalinchaŋ maa di pam. (The kalinchaŋ yams in his farm yielded a lot.)
O kali kalinkalin n-chaŋ sani. (He walked quickly to go to him.)
O zaŋla kalinli n-zali duɣu maa. (She used a pot-stand to put the pot on.)

english: a number; a count; a reckoning; a total
example: Bɛ daa zaŋ ninvuɣu shɛba ban kana maa kalinli. (They took the count of the people who came.)
Motoka kalinli maa piriya. (The car's wheel is punctured.)
O kalinsi nyaaŋa, ti zaa niŋ suhusaɣiŋgu. (After his death, we all became sad.)

english: state of not having; being without something
example: Di simdi bihim kalinsi di muɣisi a. (You shouldn't let not having milk bother you.)

english: a big sore; a serious sore
example: O nia ni kalinyaɣu maa bi tiri o suhudoo. (The big sore on his hand is not letting him rest.)

english: the smell of burnt animal matter; the smell of burning hair
example: N wumla kaliŋ maa, so n-nyo binshɛɣu. (I smell something burning, someone has burnt something.)
O daa zani kaliŋkaliŋ n-ti o. (He stood precisely in front of him.)
Kaliŋkɔɣu maa yɛla kamani o mali la yɛlibɔra. (The crow's cry sounds like it has something to say.)

english: a thorny tree whose roots are used medicinally
example: Bɛ zaŋla kaliwa tia vari n-tibiri doriti. (They use the leaves of the kaliwa tree to treat illnesses.)

english: the bark of the kaliwa tree (used medicinally)
example: O zaŋla kaliwa bihili n-tobi n-ti o. (He ground the bark of the kaliwa tree and gave it to him.)

english: a swelling in the glands of the groin
example: O mali kaliwali ka chɛ ka o chandi to. (He has a swelling in his groin which makes it hard for him to walk.)

english: grits (not-finely-ground white guinea corn soaked for a day)
example: Bɛ daa zaŋ kaliyama n-ti samba maa. (They gave the travellers the soaked grits.)

english: a segment; joint of a stalk
example: Kaɣili maa kalli kam nyɛla din mali yaa. (Every joint of the stalk is strong.)

english: lime; bone-ashes (used in spinning cotton or as whitewash)
example: O zaŋla kalo n-niŋ o nubihi ni ka mali milisiri li. (He put whiting on his fingers and is twisting it.)
O daa kɔhi kalo la daa ni. (She sold enamel bowls at the market.)

english: lastborn; the last child a woman bears
example: Ŋuna n-nyɛ kaluɣi o ma bihi ni. (He is the lastborn among his mother's children.)
O chaŋ kalun n-ti o ma lahabali. (He went quickly to give his mother the news.)
O nya kam pam o biɛhigu ni. (He has suffered a lot in his life.)
Bin' shɛli kam mali di saha. (Everything has its time.)
Di niŋ kamaata ni ti chaŋ. (It is necessary that we go.)
Kamaliga maa viɛlim nyaɣisi n-nina. (The starling's beauty pleases my eyes.)
O ŋmanila o ba kaman kom ni kɔɣili. (He looks like his father, like water and a calabash.)
O zaŋla kamantoo n-niŋ ʒɛri maa ni. (She put tomatoes in the soup.)

english: a type of tree (bitter, box-wood type)
example: Kambaŋa tia ŋɔ mali tim pam. (This kambaŋa tree has a lot of medicinal properties.)
Kambɔnluŋa maa ŋmɛri la kambon-waa. (The warrior's drummer is beating the warriors' dance.)

english: chief over drummers and warriors; war-chief
example: Bɛ daa boli Kambɔnnaa na ni o ti sɔŋ ba. (They called the war-chief to come and help them.)

english: Head Warrior-chief (title of a senior elder)
example: Kambɔnnakpɛm n-daa gbibi tooni tɔbu maa ni. (The Head Warrior-chief led the way in the battle.)
O baŋla Kambɔnsili pam. (He knows the Twi language very well.)

english: a hunter with a gun (of the Kambonsi class)
example: Kambɔntɔha maa daa ku ningbuni maa. (The hunter killed the animal.)
Kambɔŋ maa pali ni kahu. (The granary is full of millet.)

english: a Dagomba gunman; member of the warriors' class
example: Kambɔŋa maa gbibi la malifa. (The gunman holds a gun.)

english: an Ashanti person; Akan person; southern Ghanaian
example: Kambɔŋa so n-daa kana tiŋa ŋɔ ni. (An Ashanti person came to this town.)

english: a swelling in the joints; arthritis
example: O kpana maa mali kambɔŋgbara ka chɛ ka di to o. (He has arthritis in his knees which causes him pain.)
Bɛ yɛn diɛm la kambon-waa chuɣu maa ni. (They will dance the warriors' dance at the festival.)
